Bracelet of Rock Magic.entries.0: While you wear this gold bracelet, it grants you immunity to being {@condition petrified}, and it allows you to cast {@spell flesh to stone} (save {@dc 15}) as an action. Once the spell has been cast three times, the bracelet can no longer cast it. Thereafter, you can cast {@spell stone shape} as an action. After you have done this thirteen times, the bracelet loses its magic and turns from gold to lead.
Bracelet of Rock Magic.entries.1.entries: The bracelet's affinity with earth manifests as an unusual curse. Creatures of flesh that are strongly related to earth and stone, such as stone giants and dwarves, have advantage on the saving throw against {@spell flesh to stone} cast from the bracelet. If such a creature's save is successful, the bracelet breaks your attunement to it and casts the spell on you. You make your saving throw with disadvantage, and on a failed save you are {@condition petrified} instantly.
Bracelet of Rock Magic.entries.1.name: Проклятие
Bracelet of Rock Magic.name: Bracelet of Rock Magic
Bracer of Flying Daggers.entries.0: This armband appears to have thin {@item dagger|phb|daggers} strapped to it. As an action, you can pull up to two magic daggers from the bracer and immediately hurl them, making a ranged attack with each dagger. A dagger vanishes if you don't hurl it right away, and the daggers disappear right after they hit or miss. The bracer never runs out of daggers.
Bracer of Flying Daggers.name: Bracer of Flying Daggers
Bracers of Archery.entries.0: While wearing these bracers, you have proficiency with the {@item Longbow|XPHB} and {@item Shortbow|XPHB}, and you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls made with such weapons.
Bracers of Archery.name: Bracers of Archery
Bracers of Asmodeus.entries.0: You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing these bracers, if you do not wear armor or use a shield at the same time.
Bracers of Asmodeus.entries.1.entries.0: While attuned to the bracers, you become obsessed with plotting, scheming, and manipulation. You always barter for better deals, often using secrets or leveraging other offers in the process. If you ever decline an opportunity to better yourself financially at another's expense, you immediately take {@damage 3d10} necrotic damage. Only the {@spell Remove Curse} spell allows you to end attunement to this item.
Bracers of Asmodeus.entries.1.name: Проклятие
Bracers of Asmodeus.entries.2.entries.0: This item corrupts. See the "{@adventure Infernal Item Corruption|CoA|16|Infernal Item Corruption}" section.
Bracers of Asmodeus.entries.2.name: Corrupting
Bracers of Asmodeus.name: Bracers of Asmodeus
Bracers of Celerity.entries.0: This pair of lightweight bronze bracers is lined with soft, purple velvet and engraved with swirling designs.
Bracers of Celerity.entries.1: While you're wearing these bracers, all your speeds increase by 10 feet, and you have advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the paralyzed or restrained condition on yourself.
Bracers of Celerity.name: Bracers of Celerity
Bracers of Defense.entries.0: While wearing these bracers, you gain a +2 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} if you are wearing no armor and using no {@item Shield|XPHB}.
Bracers of Defense.name: Bracers of Defense
Brass Dragon Scale Mail.name: Brass Dragon Scale Mail
Brass mug with jade inlay.name: Brass mug with jade inlay
Brazier of Commanding Fire Elementals.en: While you are within 5 feet of this brazier, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to summon a {@creature Fire Elemental|XMM}. The elemental appears in an unoccupied space as close to the brazier as possible, understands your languages, obeys your commands, and takes its turn immediately after you on your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count. The elemental disappears after 1 hour, when it dies, or when you dismiss it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. The brazier can't be used this way again until the next dawn.
Bread (loaf).name: Bread (loaf)
Breastplate of Balance.entries.0: This burnished copper breastplate looks as if it were made of interlocking gears. Merchant's scales are emblazoned across the chest.
Breastplate of Balance.entries.1: The armor has 4 charges. You can use the charges in the following ways while wearing the armor:
Breastplate of Balance.entries.2.items.0: When you or a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself is about to roll a {@dice d20} with advantage or disadvantage, you can expend 1 charge and take a reaction to prevent the roll from being affected by advantage or disadvantage.
Breastplate of Balance.entries.2.items.1: As a bonus action, you can expend 2 charges to cast the {@spell Lesser Restoration} spell from the armor.
Breastplate of Balance.entries.3: The armor regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.
Breastplate of Balance.entries.4: This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.
Breastplate of Balance.name: Breastplate of Balance
Breathing Bubble.entries.0: This translucent, bubble-like sphere has a slightly tacky outer surface, and you gain the item's benefits only while wearing it over your head like a helmet.
Breathing Bubble.entries.1: The bubble contains 1 hour of breathable air. The bubble regains all its expended air daily at dawn.
Breathing Bubble.name: Breathing Bubble
Brewers Supplies.entries.0.items.0.name: Характеристика:
Brewers Supplies.entries.0.items.1.entri: Detect poisoned drink ({@dc 15}), or identify alcohol ({@dc 10})
Brewers Supplies.entries.0.items.1.name: Использование:
Brewers Supplies.entries.0.items.2.name: Craft:
Brewers Supplies.name: Brewer's Supplies
Bridle of Capturing.entries.0: This fine leather bridle tugs at the hand when first held, as though it longs to reach out for a beast nearby. When you hold one end of the bridle, you can use an action to speak its command word, causing the other end to lash out at a beast you can see within 20 feet of you. The target must succeed on a {@dc 17} Charisma saving throw or have the bridle tie itself around its neck, then fall under your command as if subjected to the {@spell dominate beast} spell. Once the targeted is affected, you do not need to hold the other end of the bridle to command it. With a successful saving throw, the target becomes immune to the bridle's power until the next dawn.
Bridle of Capturing.entries.1: A creature controlled by the bridle can be released by the creature that bound it as a bonus action. A creature controlled by the bridle can make a {@dc 17} Charisma check each day at dawn. On a success, the creature is no longer affected by the bridle.
Bridle of Capturing.name: Bridle of Capturing
Bright Fungal Cloak.entries.0: While wearing a Bright Fungal Cloak, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to furl or unfurl it. When the cloak is unfurled, it sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 5-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 5 feet.
Bright Fungal Cloak.entries.1: One pound of fungus is sewn into a Bright Fungal Cloak. This fungus can be eaten as food. Once all the fungus is consumed, the cloak becomes a mundane set of {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}.
Bright Fungal Cloak.name: Bright Fungal Cloak
Bronze Dragon Scale Mail.name: Bronze Dragon Scale Mail
Bronze crown.name: Bronze crown
Bronze spyglass (Brazen Coalition).name: Bronze spyglass (Brazen Coalition)
Brooch of Living Essence.entries.0: While wearing this nondescript brooch, spells and anything else that would detect or reveal your creature type treat you as humanoid, and those that would reveal your alignment treat it as neutral.
Brooch of Living Essence.name: Brooch of Living Essence
Brooch of Shielding.entries.0: While wearing this brooch, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Force damage, and you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to damage from the {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB} spell.
Brooch of Shielding.name: Brooch of Shielding
Brooch of the Elements.entries.0: The central jewel of this butterfly-shaped brooch swirls with multicolored iridescence.
Brooch of the Elements.entries.1: The brooch has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when a creature you can see within 60 feet of you hits a target with an attack roll and deals damage, you can expend 1 charge and change one damage type the attack deals to one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder.
Brooch of the Elements.name: Brooch of the Elements
Broom of Flying.entries.0: This wooden broom functions like a mundane broom until you stand astride it and take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to make it hover beneath you, at which time it can be ridden in the air. It has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 50 feet. It can carry up to 400 pounds, but its {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} becomes 30 feet while carrying over 200 pounds. The broom stops hovering when you land or when you're no longer riding it.
Broom of Flying.entries.1: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can send the broom to travel alone to a destination within 1 mile of you if you name the location and are familiar with it. The broom comes back to you when you take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and use a command word if the broom is still within 1 mile of you.
Broom of Flying.name: Broom of Flying
Bucket.entries.0: A Bucket holds up to half a cubic foot of contents.
Bucket.name: Bucket
Bullseye Lantern.entries.0: A Bullseye Lantern burns Oil as fuel to cast {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 60-foot Cone and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 60 feet.
Bullseye Lantern.name: Bullseye Lantern
Bundle of sheet music representing the l: Bundle of sheet music representing the lost dirges of a famous composer
Burglars Pack.entries.0: A Burglar's Pack contains the following items: {@item Backpack|XPHB}, {@item Ball Bearings|XPHB}, {@item Bell|XPHB}, 10 {@item Candle|XPHB|Candles}, {@item Crowbar|XPHB}, {@item Hooded Lantern|XPHB}, 7 flasks of {@item Oil|XPHB}, 5 days of {@item Rations|XPHB}, {@item Rope|XPHB}, {@item Tinderbox|XPHB}, and {@item Waterskin|XPHB}.
Burglars Pack.name: Burglar's Pack
Burnt Othur Fumes.entries.0: A creature subjected to Burnt Othur Fumes must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or take 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Poison damage, and it must repeat the save at the start of each of its turns. On each successive failed save, the creature takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Poison damage. After three successful saves, the poison ends.
Burnt Othur Fumes.name: Burnt Othur Fumes
Butchers Bib.entries.0: This black leather apron is perpetually covered by blood, even after being washed off. You gain the following benefits while wearing the apron:
Butchers Bib.entries.1.items.0: Once per turn when you roll damage for a melee attack with a weapon, you can reroll the weapon's damage dice. If you do so, you must use the second total.
Butchers Bib.entries.1.items.1: Your weapon attacks that deal slashing damage score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.
Butchers Bib.name: Butcher's Bib
Calimemnon Crystal.entries.0: This immaculately cut sixty-sided diamond fits in one hand, yet it contains an entire genie empire. The Calimemnon Crystal was made by high elf magic to imprison the notorious genie lords Calim and Memnon, who once ruled and warred over what is now Calimshan.
Calimemnon Crystal.entries.1: When viewed by a creature with {@sense Truesight|XPHB}, the normally beautiful diamond is a grotesque sight: trapped within the gemstone are more than a hundred genies, including the physical forms of Calim and Memnon. The genies scream and strain in agonized fury, pressing their belligerent faces against the walls of their crystal line prison.
Calimemnon Crystal.entries.2.entries.0: While attuned to the crystal, you gain the following benefits:
Calimemnon Crystal.entries.2.entries.1.i: You can use the Calimemnon Crystal as an Arcane Focus.
You have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet and can hover.

You can cast these spells with the crystal (spell save DC 18): {@spell Create or Destroy Water|XPHB}, {@spell Enlarge/Reduce|XPHB}, {@spell Invisibility|XPHB} (targeting yourself only), and {@spell Major Image|XPHB}.
Calimemnon Crystal.entries.2.name: Properties of the Crystal
Calimemnon Crystal.entries.3.entries.0: The temperature drops precipitously in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the crystal. Whenever the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} enters a creature's space and whenever a creature enters the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} or ends its turn there, the creature takes ({@dice 2d6}) Cold damage. A creature takes this damage only once per turn. While attuned to the crystal, you are unaffected by the aura, and you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to disable or enable the aura.
Calimemnon Crystal.entries.3.name: Aura of Cold
Calimemnon Crystal.entries.4.entries.0: While holding the crystal, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to fire six rays of volatile energy from the crystal at one or more creatures within 60 feet. You can aim the rays at one target or at several. For each ray, choose one of the following effects:
Calimemnon Crystal.entries.4.entries.1.i: The target makes a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking ({@dice 6d6 + 6}) Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
This property can't be used again until the next dawn.
Calimemnon Crystal.entries.4.name: Healing/Damage Rays
Calimemnon Crystal.entries.5.entries.0: The Artifact has the following random properties (see chapter 7 of the Dungeon Master's Guide):
Calimemnon Crystal.entries.5.entries.1.i: 1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Beneficial Properties|XDMG|minor beneficial} property
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Major Beneficial Properties|XDMG|major beneficial} property
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|XDMG|minor detrimental} property
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Major Detrimental Properties|XDMG|major detrimental} property
Calimemnon Crystal.entries.5.name: Random Properties
Calimemnon Crystal.entries.6.entries.0: The Calimemnon Crystal is destroyed if the creature attuned to it casts the {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell and wishes the crystal destroyed. Nothing else can harm it. When the crystal is destroyed, all the genies in the crystal are released.
Calimemnon Crystal.entries.6.name: Destroying the Crystal
Calimemnon Crystal.name: Calimemnon Crystal
Calligraphers Supplies.entries.0.items.1: Write text with impressive flourishes that guard against forgery ({@dc 15})
Calligraphers Supplies.name: Calligrapher's Supplies
Caltrop.entries.0: As an action, you can spread a single bag of caltrops to cover a 5-foot-square area. Any creature that enters the area must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or stop moving and take 1 piercing damage. Until the creature regains at least 1 hit point, its walking speed is reduced by 10 feet. A creature moving through the area at half speed doesn't need to make the saving throw.
Caltrop.name: Caltrop
Caltrops.entries.0: As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can spread Caltrops from their bag to cover a 5-foot-square area within 5 feet of yourself. A creature that enters this area for the first time on a turn must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take 1 Piercing damage and have its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn. It takes 10 minutes to recover the Caltrops.
Caltrops.name: Caltrops
Camel.name: Camel
Candle Mace.entries.0: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The head of this mace sheds bright light in a 5-foot-radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet. When you wield this mace, you can extinguish or ignite its light as an action.
Candle Mace.name: Candle Mace
Candle of Invocation.entries.0: This candle's magic is activated when the candle is lit, which requires a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. After burning for 4 hours, the candle is destroyed. You can snuff it out early for use at a later time. Deduct the time it burned in increments of 1 minute from its total burn time.
Candle of Invocation.entries.1: While lit, the candle sheds {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} in a 30-foot radius. While you are within that light, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests}. In addition, a Cleric or Druid in the light can cast level 1 spells they have prepared without expending spell slots.
Candle of Invocation.entries.2: Alternatively, when you light the candle for the first time, you can cast {@spell Gate|XPHB} with it. Doing so destroys the candle. The portal created by the spell links to a particular Outer Plane chosen by the DM or determined by rolling on the following table.
Candle of Invocation.name: Candle of Invocation
Candle of the Deep.entries.0: The flame of this candle isn't extinguished when immersed in water. It gives off light and heat like a normal candle.
Candle of the Deep.name: Candle of the Deep
Candle.entries.0: For 1 hour, a lit Candle sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 5-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 5 feet.
Candle.name: Candle
Canian Fork.entries.0: You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, you can make one additional attack with it as a bonus action on each of your turns.
Canian Fork.entries.1.entries.0: You're unwilling to part with this weapon while attuned to it. You're also vulnerable to radiant damage and each time you receive magical healing, you must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw.
Canian Fork.entries.1.entries.1: On a failed save, the healing has no effect. Only the {@spell Remove Curse} spell allows you to end attunement to this item.
Canian Fork.entries.1.name: Проклятие
Canian Fork.entries.2.entries.0: This item corrupts. See the "{@adventure Infernal Item Corruption|CoA|16|Infernal Item Corruption}" section.
Canian Fork.entries.2.name: Corrupting
Canian Fork.name: Canian Fork
Canoe.entries.0: A canoe can be purchased in Port Nyanzaru for 50 gp. It holds up to six Medium creatures and has a maximum speed of 2 mph. It is otherwise identical to a {@item rowboat}.
Canoe.name: Canoe
Canvas (1 sq. yd.).name: Canvas (1 sq. yd.)
Cap of Vanishing.entries.0: This cap has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the cap, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and expend 1 charge to give yourself the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition for 10 minutes. The effect ends early if the hat is removed, or immediately after you make an attack roll, deal damage, or cast a spell.
Cap of Vanishing.name: Cap of Vanishing
Cap of Water Breathing.entries.0: While wearing this cap underwater, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to create a bubble of air around your head. This bubble allows you to breathe normally underwater. This bubble stays with you until the cap is removed or you are no longer underwater.
Cap of Water Breathing.name: Cap of Water Breathing
Cape of Enlargement.entries.0: Ancient runes are stitched in silver thread along the hem of this grayish-purple cape.
Cape of Enlargement.entries.1: The cape has 3 charges. As a bonus action while wearing the cape, you can expend 1 of its charges to enlarge yourself, granting yourself the following benefits:
Cape of Enlargement.entries.2.items.0: Your size increases by one category—from Medium to Large, for example. If there isn't enough room for your size to increase by one category, you instead become the maximum possible size in the space available.
Cape of Enlargement.entries.2.items.1: You have advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws.
Cape of Enlargement.entries.2.items.2: When you hit with an attack roll using a weapon or an unarmed strike, you can add your proficiency bonus to the attack's damage.
Cape of Enlargement.entries.3: These benefits last for 10 minutes or until you use another bonus action to dismiss them. The cape regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.
Cape of Enlargement.name: Cape of Enlargement
Cape of the Mountebank.entries.0: This cape smells faintly of brimstone. While wearing it, you can use it to cast {@spell Dimension Door|XPHB} as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.
Cape of the Mountebank.entries.1: When you teleport with that spell, you leave behind a cloud of smoke. The space you left is {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB} by that smoke until the end of your next turn.
Cape of the Mountebank.name: Cape of the Mountebank
Card Sharps Deck.entries.0: The cards of this deck shimmer around the edges. While holding this deck, you can use the following properties:
Card Sharps Deck.entries.1.items.0.entri: As an action, you can use this deck to make a ranged spell attack by throwing a spectral card and using Dexterity for the attack roll. The card has a range of 120 feet and deals {@damage 1d8} force damage on a hit.
Card Sharps Deck.entries.1.items.0.name: Deadly Deal
Card Sharps Deck.entries.1.items.1.entri: As an action, you can shuffle the deck and cast the {@spell Spray of Cards|BMT} spell at 3rd level from the deck (spell save {@dc 15}). Once the deck has cast the spell, it can't cast the spell again until the next dawn.
Card Sharps Deck.entries.1.items.1.name: Spray of Cards
Card Sharps Deck.name: Card Sharp's Deck
Carnelian.entries.0: A orange to red brown gemstone.
Carnelian.name: Carnelian
Carpenters Tools.entries.0.items.0.name: Характеристика:
Carpenters Tools.entries.0.items.1.entri: Seal or pry open a door or container ({@dc 20})
Carpenters Tools.entries.0.items.1.name: Использование:
Carpenters Tools.entries.0.items.2.entri: {@item Club|XPHB}, {@item Greatclub|XPHB}, {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}, {@item Barrel|XPHB}, {@item Chest|XPHB}, {@item Ladder|XPHB}, {@item Pole|XPHB}, {@item Portable Ram|XPHB}, {@item Torch|XPHB}
Carpenters Tools.entries.0.items.2.name: Craft:
Carpenters Tools.name: Carpenter's Tools
You can make this carpet hover and fly by taking a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and using the carpet's command word. It moves according to your directions if you are within 30 feet of it.

A 3 ft. × 5 ft. carpet can carry up to 200 lb. at a fly speed of 80 feet. A carpet can carry up to twice the weight shown on the table, but its {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} is halved if it carries more than its normal capacity.
Carpet of Flying, 3 ft. × 5 ft..name: Carpet of Flying, 3 ft. × 5 ft.
You can make this carpet hover and fly by taking a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and using the carpet's command word. It moves according to your directions if you are within 30 feet of it.

A 4 ft. × 6 ft. carpet can carry up to 400 lb. at a fly speed of 60 feet. A carpet can carry up to twice the weight shown on the table, but its {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} is halved if it carries more than its normal capacity.
Carpet of Flying, 4 ft. × 6 ft..name: Carpet of Flying, 4 ft. × 6 ft.
You can make this carpet hover and fly by taking a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and using the carpet's command word. It moves according to your directions if you are within 30 feet of it.

A 5 ft. × 7 ft. carpet can carry up to 600 lb. at a fly speed of 40 feet. A carpet can carry up to twice the weight shown on the table, but its {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} is halved if it carries more than its normal capacity.
Carpet of Flying, 5 ft. × 7 ft..name: Carpet of Flying, 5 ft. × 7 ft.
You can make this carpet hover and fly by taking a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and using the carpet's command word. It moves according to your directions if you are within 30 feet of it.

A 6 ft. × 9 ft. carpet can carry up to 800 lb. at a fly speed of 30 feet. A carpet can carry up to twice the weight shown on the table, but its {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} is halved if it carries more than its normal capacity.
Carpet of Flying, 6 ft. × 9 ft..name: Carpet of Flying, 6 ft. × 9 ft.
Carriage.name: Carriage
Carrion Crawler Mucus.entries.0: A creature subjected to Carrion Crawler Mucus must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. The creature also has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition while {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} in this way. The creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Carrion Crawler Mucus.name: Carrion Crawler Mucus
Cart.name: Cart
Cartographers Map Case.entries.0.entries: At rank 3, your map case becomes a common magic item. In addition to storing normal maps, your cartographer's map case can be used to generate a special map identifying a shortcut. You can use your action to make a {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check, with a success revealing a map buried in your cartographer's map case noting a relevant shortcut. Your travel time is reduced by half while you follow that route. If you succeed at the check by 5 or more, the map includes notes on the terrain, granting you advantage on the next ability check you make to travel through the mapped area in the next hour. Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a long rest.
Cartographers Map Case.entries.0.name: Map of Shortcuts
Cartographers Map Case.entries.1.entries: Starting at rank 3, you can use an action to make a {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check and search your cartographer's map case to find a map either related to your current mission or inspiring a new one. On a success, you find a map with a prominent landmark. The map has information on the natural terrain within one mile of the landmark. Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until dawn seven days later.
The Ancient Sarcophagus of Gerald Smith
"The Last Resting Place of My Rich Brother that is Also a Bear Cave"
A range of peaks known as the Slightly Wobbly Spires
An ever-flowing, nonmagical spigot of pure, clear water in the middle of nowhere
A cave filled with screaming bioluminescent worms
The most haunted forest for ten miles in any direction
A huge pile of rocks with no other rocks within sight
Cartographers Map Case.entries.1.name: Map of the Moment
Cartographers Map Case.name: Cartographer's Map Case
Cartographers Tools.entries.0.items.1.en: Draft a map of a small area ({@dc 15})
Cartographers Tools.name: Cartographer's Tools
Carved bone statuette.name: Carved bone statuette
Carved ivory statuette.name: Carved ivory statuette
Carved wooden harp with ivory inlay and: Carved wooden harp with ivory inlay and zircon gems
Catapult Munition.entries.0: A catapult munition roughly the size and weight of a cannonball.
Catapult Munition.entries.1: The munition can be thrown up to 30 feet and explodes on impact. Any creature within a 15-foot-radius sphere centered on the point of impact must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw, taking 35 ({@damage 10d6}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. In addition, each object in that area that isn't being worn or carried takes 35 ({@damage 10d6}) fire damage.
Catapult Munition.name: Catapult Munition
Cauldron of Plenty.entries.0: This cauldron is made of thick copper that has turned green with age. It is 4 feet wide, has a mouth 3½ feet in diameter, weighs 50 pounds, and can hold up to 30 gallons of liquid. Embossed on its bulging sides are images of satyrs and nymphs in repose, holding ladles. The cauldron comes with a lid and has side handles. It sits on five little clawed feet that keep it from tipping.
Cauldron of Plenty.entries.1: If water is poured into the cauldron and stirred for 1 minute, it transforms into a hearty, hot stew, which can provide one nourishing meal for up to four people per gallon. The stew remains hot while in the cauldron, then cools naturally after it is removed. The outside of the cauldron remains safe to touch despite the heat of the stew.
Cauldron of Plenty.entries.2: The cauldron can create stew three times. It then ceases to function until the next dawn, when it regains all its uses.
Cauldron of Plenty.name: Cauldron of Plenty
Cauldron of Rebirth.entries.0: This Tiny pot bears relief scenes of heroes on its cast-iron sides.
Cauldron of Rebirth.entries.1: You can use the cauldron as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your spells, and it functions as a suitable component for the {@spell Scrying|XPHB} spell.
Cauldron of Rebirth.entries.2.entries.0: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can use the cauldron to create a {@item Potion of Greater Healing|XDMG|Potion of Healing (greater)}, which takes 1 minute. The potion lasts for 24 hours, then loses its magic if not consumed.
Cauldron of Rebirth.entries.2.name: Brew Potion
Cauldron of Rebirth.entries.3.entries.0: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can cause the cauldron to grow large enough for a Medium creature to crouch within. You can revert the cauldron to its normal size as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, harmlessly shunting anything that can't fit inside to the nearest unoccupied space.
Cauldron of Rebirth.entries.3.entries.1: If you place the corpse of a Humanoid into the cauldron and cover the corpse with 200 pounds of salt (which costs 10 GP) for at least 8 hours, the salt is consumed and the creature returns to life as if by {@spell Raise Dead|XPHB} at the next dawn. Once used, this property can't be used again for 7 days.
Cauldron of Rebirth.entries.3.name: Raise Dead
Cauldron of Rebirth.name: Cauldron of Rebirth
Censer of Controlling Air Elementals.ent: While gently swinging this censer, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to summon an {@creature Air Elemental|XMM}. The elemental appears in an unoccupied space as close to the censer as possible, understands your languages, obeys your commands, and takes its turn immediately after you on your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count. The elemental disappears after 1 hour, when it dies, or when you dismiss it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. The censer can't be used this way again until the next dawn.
Ceremonial gold armor with black pearls
Ceremonial silver dagger with gold pomme: Ceremonial silver dagger with gold pommel and black pearl (Legion of Dusk)
Chain.entries.0: As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can wrap a Chain around an unwilling creature within 5 feet of yourself that has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, or {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition if you succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check. If the creature's legs are bound, the creature has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until it escapes. Escaping the Chain requires the creature to make a successful {@dc 18} Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) check as an action. Bursting the Chain requires a successful {@dc 20} Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check as an action.
Chain.name: Chain
Chalcedony.name: Chalcedony
Chalk (1 piece).name: Chalk (1 piece)
Chariot.name: Chariot
Charlatans Die.entries.0: Whenever you roll this six-sided die, you can control which number it rolls.
Charlatans Die.name: Charlatan's Die
Charm of Plant Command.entries.0: This fist-sized charm is made from a bundle of dried plant stems wrapped in silver thread. Hung on a leather thong, it is typically worn around the neck or attached to a belt.
Charm of Plant Command.entries.1: This charm has 3 charges. While you bear the charm, you can expend 1 charge as an action to cast the {@spell speak with plants} spell. For the duration of the spell, you also have advantage on Charisma checks made to influence the behavior, demeanor, and attitude of plants. The charm regains all expended charges at dawn each day.
Charm of Plant Command.name: Charm of Plant Command
Charred Wand of Magic Missiles.name: Charred Wand of Magic Missiles
Cheese (wedge).name: Cheese (wedge)
Chest of Preserving.entries.0: Food and other perishable items do not age or decay while inside a Chest of Preserving. The chest is 2½ feet long, 1½ feet wide, and 1 foot tall with a half-barrel lid. The chest has a lock, which can be picked with {@item thieves' tools|PHB} and a successful {@dc 15} Dexterity check. Smashing the lock or any other part of the chest renders it nonmagical.
Chest of Preserving.name: Chest of Preserving
Chest.entries.0: A Chest holds up to 12 cubic feet of contents.
Chest.name: Chest
Chicken.name: Chicken
Chime of Exile.entries.0: This silver chime is engraved with delicate magic sigils. While holding the chime, you can use an action to cast the {@spell Banishment} spell (spell save {@dc 20}). If the target of the spell has 50 hit points or fewer, it automatically fails its saving throw. Once the chime has been used to cast the spell, it can't be used this way again until the next dawn.
Chime of Exile.name: Chime of Exile
Chime of Opening.entries.0: This hollow metal tube measures about 1 foot long and weighs 1 pound. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can strike the chime to cast {@spell Knock|XPHB}. The spell's customary knocking sound is replaced by the clear, ringing tone of the chime, which is audible out to 300 feet. The chime can be used 10 times. After the tenth time, it cracks and becomes useless.
Chime of Opening.name: Chime of Opening
Chip of Creation.entries.0: This solid, hand-sized flake of rock is a minor piece of the {@item Stone of Creation|AitFR-AVT} and contains a portion of its power.
Chip of Creation.entries.1.entries.0: Each chip of creation is inextricably linked to its own demiplanar room, as per the {@spell demiplane} spell. This room exists as long as both this chip and the Stone of Creation do. A door to the room only exists when created using a {@spell demiplane} spell, whether a chip of creation is used in the casting or not.
Chip of Creation.entries.1.name: Linked Room
Chip of Creation.entries.2.entries.0: While attuned to a chip of creation, you can cast {@spell demiplane} at will but only to reach the demiplanar room associated with that chip of creation or another demiplane that you know the nature and contents of, and only if the target demiplane was created by another piece of the {@item Stone of Creation|AitFR-AVT}.
Chip of Creation.entries.2.name: Demiplane
Chip of Creation.entries.3.entries.0: A chip of creation is an artifact in its own right and impervious to most damage, but a {@spell disintegrate} spell is sufficient to destroy it. After it has been attuned once, a chip that has gone {@dice 1d4 + 1} days unattuned becomes an inert, powerless bit of rock.
Chip of Creation.entries.3.entries.1: If the {@item Stone of Creation|AitFR-AVT} is destroyed, all its chips break attunement, lose all magical properties, and linked demiplanar rooms cease to exist at once. Creatures or objects within the room are instantly expelled into the immediate vicinity of the destroyed chip.
Chip of Creation.entries.3.entries.2: If it reaches the Elemental Chaos or is struck by a forked, metal rod attuned to the Elemental Chaos (as per the {@spell plane shift} spell), a chip is destroyed.
Chip of Creation.entries.3.name: Destroying a Chip
Chip of Creation.name: Chip of Creation
Chronolometer.entries.0: While attuned to this device, you have a +1 bonus to Intelligence saving throws. The first time you attune to the chronolometer, you choose one language you don't know. You subsequently know that language while attuned to the device.
Chronolometer.entries.1.entries.0: At the start of your turn, roll a {@dice d6} (no action required). On a 1–3, you slow down time, gaining an additional action on your turn and doubling your speed until the end of the turn. On a 4–6, you go forward in time to warn yourself of what is to come. The next time you fail a saving throw, attack roll, or ability check, you can reroll the check and take either result. Once you use this feature of the chronolometer, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.
Chronolometer.entries.1.name: Time Bandit
Chronolometer.entries.2.entries.0: As a reaction when a creature you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage, that creature gains an additional action if it is the creature's turn, or can take an action immediately even though it isn't the creature's turn. Once you use this feature of the chronolometer, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.
Chronolometer.entries.2.name: Fate Swap
Chronolometer.entries.3.entries.0: While this component is not installed in the {@item Orrery of the Wanderer|AI}, its magic might function sporadically or with unpredictable side effects, as determined by the DM.
Chronolometer.entries.3.name: Part of a Whole
Chronolometer.name: Chronolometer
Chrysoberyl.entries.0: A yellow green to pale green gemstone.
Chrysoberyl.name: Chrysoberyl
Chrysoprase.name: Chrysoprase
Chunk of Meat.name: Chunk of Meat
Cinnamon.name: Cinnamon
Circlet of Blasting.entries.0: While wearing this circlet, you can cast {@spell Scorching Ray|XPHB} with it ({@hit 5} to hit). The circlet can't cast this spell again until the next dawn.
Circlet of Blasting.name: Circlet of Blasting
Circlet of Human Perfection.entries.0: The Circlet of Human Perfection transforms its attuned wearer into an attractive human of average height and weight. The circlet chooses the physical characteristics of the form, such as age, gender, skin color, hair color, and voice. Except for size, the wearer's statistics and racial traits don't change, nor do items worn or carried by the wearer. Removing the circlet ends the effect.
Circlet of Human Perfection.name: Circlet of Human Perfection
Citrine.name: Citrine
Claw of the Wyrm Rune.entries.0: This dragon's claw has been covered with a coat of molten silver, upon which has been inscribed the wyrm (dragon) rune. The claw has the following properties.
As an action, you can point the claw at a dragon within 30 feet of you. The dragon must then succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or gain vulnerability to all damage types until the end of your next turn. This property can be used three times. The claw regains all expended uses at the next dawn.
Claw of the Wyrm Rune.entries.1.name: Wyrmslayer
While the claw is displayed on your person, you have resistance to the damage caused by any dragon's breath weapon.
Claw of the Wyrm Rune.entries.2.name: Wyrm Shield
You can transfer the claw's magic to a place by tracing the wyrm rune on the ground with your finger. The point where you trace it becomes the center of a spherical area of magic that has a 100-foot radius and that is fixed to the place. The transfer takes 8 hours of work that requires the claw to be within 5 feet of you. At the end, the claw is destroyed, and the area gains the following property:
While in the 100-foot-radius sphere, any dragon has disadvantage on saving throws and can have a flying speed no higher than 10 feet.
Claw of the Wyrm Rune.entries.3.name: Wyrm Ward
Claw of the Wyrm Rune.name: Claw of the Wyrm Rune
Claws of the Umber Hulk.entries.0: These heavy gauntlets of brown iron are forged in the shape of an {@creature umber hulk||umber hulk's} claws, and they fit the wearer's hands and forearms all the way up to the elbow. While wearing both claws, you gain a burrowing speed of 20 feet, and you can tunnel through solid rock at a rate of 1 foot per round.
Claws of the Umber Hulk.entries.1: You can use a claw as a melee weapon while wearing it. You have proficiency with it, and it deals {@damage 1d8} slashing damage on a hit (your Strength modifier applies to the attack and damage rolls, as normal).
Claws of the Umber Hulk.entries.2: While wearing the claws, you can't manipulate objects or cast spells with somatic components.
Claws of the Umber Hulk.name: Claws of the Umber Hulk
Cleansing Stone.entries.0: A cleansing stone is a sphere 1 foot in diameter, engraved with mystic sigils. When touching the stone, you can use an action to activate it and remove dirt and grime from your garments and your person.
Cleansing Stone.entries.1: Such stones are often embedded in pedestals in public squares in Aundair or in high-end Ghallanda inns.
Cleansing Stone.name: Cleansing Stone
Climbers Kit.entries.0: A Climber's Kit includes boot tips, gloves, pitons, and a harness. As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can use the Climber's Kit to anchor yourself; when you do, you can't fall more than 25 feet from the anchor point, and you can't move more than 25 feet from there without undoing the anchor as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Climbers Kit.name: Climber's Kit
Cloak of Arachnida.entries.0: This fine garment is made of black silk interwoven with faint, silvery threads. While wearing it, you gain the following benefits.
Cloak of Arachnida.entries.1.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison damage.
Cloak of Arachnida.entries.1.name: Poison Resistance
Cloak of Arachnida.entries.2.entries.0: You have a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and along ceilings, while leaving your hands free.
Cloak of Arachnida.entries.2.name: Паучье лазание
Cloak of Arachnida.entries.3.entries.0: You can't be caught in webs of any sort and can move through webs as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.
Cloak of Arachnida.entries.3.name: Spider Walk
Cloak of Arachnida.entries.4.entries.0: You can cast {@spell Web|XPHB} (save {@dc 13}). The web created by the spell fills twice its normal area. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.
Cloak of Arachnida.entries.4.name: Web
Cloak of Arachnida.name: Cloak of Arachnida
Cloak of Billowing.entries.0: While wearing this cloak, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make it billow dramatically for 1 minute.
Cloak of Billowing.name: Cloak of Billowing
Cloak of Displacement.entries.0: While you wear this cloak, it magically projects an illusion that makes you appear to be standing in a place near your actual location, causing any creature to have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against you. If you take damage, the property ceases to function until the start of your next turn. This property is suppressed while your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is 0.
Cloak of Displacement.name: Cloak of Displacement
Cloak of Elvenkind.entries.0: While you wear this cloak, Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks made to perceive you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks.
Cloak of Elvenkind.name: Cloak of Elvenkind
Cloak of Invisibility.entries.0: This cloak has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the cloak, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to pull its hood over your head and expend 1 charge to give yourself the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition for 1 hour. The effect ends early if you pull the hood down (no action required) or cease wearing the hood.
Cloak of Invisibility.name: Cloak of Invisibility
Cloak of Many Fashions.entries.0: While wearing this cloak, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to change the style, color, and apparent quality of the garment. The cloak's weight doesn't change. Regardless of its appearance, the cloak can't be anything but a cloak. Although it can duplicate the appearance of other magic cloaks, it doesn't gain their magical properties.
Cloak of Many Fashions.name: Cloak of Many Fashions
Cloak of Protection.entries.0: You gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} and saving throws while you wear this cloak.
Cloak of Protection.name: Cloak of Protection
Cloak of the Bat.entries.0: While wearing this cloak, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks. In an area of {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, you can grip the edges of the cloak and use it to gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 40 feet. If you ever fail to grip the cloak's edges while flying in this way, or if you are no longer in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, you lose this {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}.
Cloak of the Bat.entries.1: While wearing the cloak in an area of {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, you can cast {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} on yourself, shape-shifting into a {@creature Bat|XMM}. While in that form, you retain your Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores. The cloak can't be used this way again until the next dawn.
Cloak of the Bat.name: Cloak of the Bat
Cloak of the Manta Ray.entries.0: While wearing this cloak, you can breathe underwater, and you have a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} of 60 feet.
Cloak of the Manta Ray.name: Cloak of the Manta Ray
Clockwork Amulet.entries.0: This copper amulet contains tiny interlocking gears and is powered by magic from Mechanus, a plane of clockwork predictability. Faint ticking and whirring noises emanate from within.
Clockwork Amulet.entries.1: When you make an attack roll while wearing the amulet, you can forgo rolling the {@dice d20} to get a 10 on the die. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.
Clockwork Amulet.name: Clockwork Amulet
Clockwork Dog.entries.0: This Tiny clockwork dog made of copper and tin comes with a copper wind-up key. As an action, you can use the key to wind the dog, after which it follows you for 12 hours. At the end of that duration, the clockwork dog stops until wound again. The dog has AC 5, 1 hit point, and a walking speed of 30 feet.
Clockwork Dog.name: Clockwork Dog
Cloth-of-gold vestments.name: Cloth-of-gold vestments
Clothes of Mending.entries.0: This elegant outfit magically mends itself to counteract daily wear and tear. Pieces of the outfit that are destroyed can't be repaired in this way.
Clothes of Mending.name: Clothes of Mending
Clothing, cold weather.entries.0: This outfit consists of a heavy fur coat or cloak over layers of wool clothing, as well as a fur-lined hat or hood, goggles, and fur-lined leather boots and gloves.
Clothing, cold weather.entries.1: As long as cold weather clothing remains dry, its wearer automatically succeeds on saving throws against the effects of {@hazard extreme cold}.
Clothing, cold weather.name: Clothing, cold weather
Cloves.name: Cloves
Cobblers Tools.entries.0.items.0.name: Характеристика:
Cobblers Tools.entries.0.items.1.entries: Modify footwear to give {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the wearer's next Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) check ({@dc 10})
Cobblers Tools.entries.0.items.1.name: Использование:
Cobblers Tools.entries.0.items.2.name: Craft:
Cobblers Tools.name: Cobbler's Tools
Coiling Grasp Tattoo.entries.0: Produced by a special needle, this magic tattoo has long intertwining designs.
Coiling Grasp Tattoo.entries.1.entries.0: To attune to this item, you hold the needle to your skin where you want the tattoo to appear, pressing the needle there throughout the attunement process. When the attunement is complete, the needle turns into the ink that becomes the tattoo, which appears on the skin.
Coiling Grasp Tattoo.entries.1.entries.1: If your attunement to the tattoo ends, the tattoo vanishes, and the needle reappears in your space.
Coiling Grasp Tattoo.entries.1.name: Tattoo Attunement
Coiling Grasp Tattoo.entries.2.entries.0: While the tattoo is on your skin, you can, as an action, cause the tattoo to extrude into inky tendrils, which reach for a creature you can see within 15 feet of you. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or take {@damage 3d6} force damage and be {@condition grappled} by you. As an action, the creature can escape the grapple by succeeding on a {@dc 14} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check. The grapple also ends if you halt it (no action required), if the creature is ever more than 15 feet away from you, or if you use this tattoo on a different creature.
Coiling Grasp Tattoo.entries.2.name: Grasping Tendrils
Coiling Grasp Tattoo.name: Coiling Grasp Tattoo
Coin of Decisionry.entries.0: Starting at rank 2, your coin of decisionry becomes a common magic item. When you flip the coin, it always lands with the Acquisitions Incorporated sigil face down, and a message appears on the "tails" face. Roll a {@dice d4} on the following table to determine the message.
Coin of Decisionry.entries.2: The coin has absolutely no divination abilities, and its results when you use it are random. But nobody else knows that. When a creature within 10 feet of you flips the coin (after having had its powerful prognostication powers dutifully explained), you can exert your will to control its operation as a bonus action, choosing the result that appears after it lands as a means of gently coercing the user toward a specific course of action. The creature flipping the coin can detect your manipulation with a successful {@dc 13} Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check.
Coin of Decisionry.entries.3.entries.0: When you reach rank 3, your coin of decisionry gains a measure of actual divination power and becomes an uncommon magic item. In addition to its normal function, you can use an action to flip the coin of decisionry twice while pondering a specific plan or objective, noting both random results. If you succeed on a {@dc 15} Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) check, you learn which of the two results is more applicable to the course of action. This property of the coin can't be used again until the next dawn.
Coin of Decisionry.entries.3.name: Better Odds
Coin of Decisionry.name: Coin of Decisionry
Coin of Delving.entries.0: This scintillating copper coin sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. If dropped a distance greater than 5 feet, the coin issues a melodious ringing sound when it hits a surface. Any creature that can hear the chime can determine the distance the coin dropped based on the tone.
Coin of Delving.name: Coin of Delving
Cold Absorbing Tattoo.name: Cold Absorbing Tattoo
Comet Card.entries.0: As an action, you can hold this card aloft and call down a fiery meteor to a point you can see within 120 feet of yourself. Each creature within a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on that point must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 6d6} bludgeoning damage and {@damage 6d6} fire damage, or half as much damage on a successful save. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
Comet Card.name: Comet Card
Command Amulet.entries.0: Each {@creature Shield Guardian|XMM} is magically bound to an amulet. While the guardian and its amulet are on the same plane of existence, the amulet's wearer can telepathically call the guardian to travel to it, and the guardian knows the distance and direction to the amulet. If the guardian is within 60 feet of the amulet's wearer, half of any damage the wearer takes (round up) is transferred to the guardian.
Command Amulet.entries.1: A shield guardian's command amulet might be passed down through a magic-using society or family for generations.
Command Amulet.name: Command Amulet
Common Clothes.name: Common Clothes
Common Glamerweave.entries.0: Glamerweave is clothing imbued with harmless illusory magic. While wearing the common version of these clothes, you can use a bonus action to create a moving illusory pattern within the cloth.
Common Glamerweave.name: Common Glamerweave
Common Wine (bottle).name: Common Wine (bottle)
Component Pouch.entries.0: A Component Pouch is watertight and filled with compartments that hold all the free Material components of your spells.
Component Pouch.name: Component Pouch
Concertina.entries.0: A concertina is a less charming kind of accordion.
Concertina.entries.1: In addition to knocking out a killer polka, a creature playing the concertina can use an action to cause an area of mist or water up to 30 feet in diameter and 6 inches deep to freeze solid.
Concertina.entries.2: Once used, this feature of the concertina can't be used again until the next dawn.
Concertina.entries.3: Any creatures touching the floor must succeed on a {@dc 20} Dexterity saving throw or be {@condition restrained} by the ice. As an action, a {@condition restrained} creature can attempt to free itself or another creature within its reach from the ice, doing so with a successful {@dc 20} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check. A character freed from the ice is no longer {@condition restrained} by it. The ice melts in an hour.
Concertina.name: Concertina
Conch of Safe Rest.entries.0: You can use an action to blow this pearlescent conch like a {@item horn|PHB}, creating an effect identical with that of a {@spell Leomund's tiny hut} spell. The conch disappears once the spell takes effect. The effect ends if you leave the spell's area.
Conch of Safe Rest.name: Conch of Safe Rest
Conch of Teleportation.entries.0: This item is an ordinary, albeit rather large, conch shell that has been inscribed with the uvar rune. The conch measures 2½ feet long and weighs 20 pounds.
Conch of Teleportation.entries.1: As an action, you can cast the {@spell teleport} spell by blowing into the shell. The destination is fixed, and there is no chance of either a mishap or the spell being off target. Anyone teleported by the conch appears in a specific location designated by the item's creator at the time the uvar rune is inscribed on the conch. It doesn't allow teleportation to any other destination. Once its spell is cast, the conch can't be used again until the next dawn.
Conch of Teleportation.name: Conch of Teleportation
Concussion Grenade.entries.1: This grenade explodes in a concussive blast that fills a 20-foot-radius sphere. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking ({@damage 6d6}) force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
Concussion Grenade.name: Concussion Grenade
Condensed Order.entries.0: Condensed Order is a silvery powder that can be extracted from those of a lawful persuasion. Devils bound for the war fronts of Avernus take flasks and snuff boxes of the stuff to fortify themselves against exposure to the raw chaos of demons. Taking the substance requires an action and makes you immune to the flesh warping feature of demonic ichor. It also gives you advantage on saving throws against any effect from a demonic source. These benefits last for 8 hours.
Condensed Order.name: Condensed Order
Constantoris Portrait.entries.0: This painting by famed artist Dkesii Kwan depicts Constantori, a beautiful courtier, who was paid a staggering sum to be Dkesii's model. Whether Constantori's actual appearance matches the painting remains a subject of debate. The portrait is one of several paintings commissioned by the late Daiyani Grysthorn, a crime lord who frequently gave magical paintings as gifts to her most esteemed associates.
Constantori's Portrait is a sentient, lawful evil item with an Intelligence score of 14, a Wisdom score of 12, and a Charisma score of 8. It can hear within a range of 120 feet and has {@sense darkvision} within a range of 60 feet, but it can't see anything behind itself.

The painting can converse in Common, Draconic, and Elvish as if it were a living person, though Constantori's mouth doesn't move. Whenever conversation occurs within the portrait's auditory range, the painting eagerly gathers secrets, the names of secret tellers, significant events, or any political conversations.
Constantoris Portrait.entries.1.name: Sentience
Constantori's Portrait is demanding, condescending, and vain. It doesn't like being covered or placed out of sight, and it loudly condemns anyone who tries to remove it from its gold-leaf frame.
Constantoris Portrait.entries.2.name: Personality
The painting's primary purpose is to observe and recall conversations. Over the past few decades, Constantori's Portrait has quietly observed countless conversations and now possesses an unquantifiable amount of lore—everything from criminal conspiracies to secret passwords. The DM decides what the painting knows and what it doesn't.

While attuned to the painting, you can take an action to telepathically contact it over any distance, provided you and the painting are on the same plane of existence. The painting can't telepathically contact you, however. Maintaining telepathic contact with the painting requires your {@status concentration} (as if {@status concentration||concentrating} on a spell).
Constantoris Portrait.entries.3.name: Wealth of Information
While you are attuned to the painting, you can command it to guard its location against one or more creatures you identify as the painting's enemies. The painting performs this function until you command it to stop or until your attunement to the painting ends.

The painting has 3 charges. When a creature the painting identifies as its enemy starts its turn in a space the painting can see, the painting expends 1 of its charges to cast {@spell magic missile} (3 missiles), targeting that creature. The painting regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

The painting is a Small object with AC 12, 20 hit points, and immunity to poison damage. In its gold-leaf frame, the painting weighs 15 pounds. If the painting has at least 1 hit point and is targeted by a {@spell mending} spell, it regains {@dice 2d6} hit points.
Constantoris Portrait.entries.4.name: Guardian Portrait
Constantoris Portrait.name: Constantori's Portrait
Cooks Utensils.entries.0.items.0.name: Характеристика:
Cooks Utensils.entries.0.items.1.entries: Improve food's flavor ({@dc 10}), or detect spoiled or poisoned food ({@dc 15})
Cooks Utensils.entries.0.items.1.name: Использование:
Cooks Utensils.entries.0.items.2.name: Craft:
Cooks Utensils.name: Cook's Utensils
Copper (cp).entries.0: Common coins come in several different denominations based on the relative worth of the metal from which they are made. The three most common coins are the gold piece (gp), the silver piece (sp), and the copper piece (cp).
Copper (cp).entries.1: With one gold piece, a character can buy a belt pouch, 50 feet of good rope, or a goat. A skilled (but not exceptional) artisan can earn one gold piece a day. The gold piece is the standard unit of measure for wealth, even if the coin itself is not commonly used. When merchants discuss deals that involve goods or services worth hundreds or thousands of gold pieces, the transactions don't usually involve the exchange of individual coins. Rather, the gold piece is a standard measure of value, and the actual exchange is in gold bars, letters of credit, or valuable goods.
Copper (cp).entries.2: One gold piece is worth ten silver pieces, the most prevalent coin among commoners. A silver piece buys a laborer's work for a day, a flask of lamp oil, or a night's rest in a poor inn.
Copper (cp).entries.3: One silver piece is worth ten copper pieces, which are common among laborers and beggars. A single copper piece buys a candle, a torch, or a piece of chalk.
Copper (cp).entries.4: In addition, unusual coins made of other precious metals sometimes appear in treasure hoards. The electrum piece (ep) and the platinum piece (pp) originate from fallen empires and lost kingdoms, and they sometimes arouse suspicion and skepticism when used in transactions. An electrum piece is worth five silver pieces, and a platinum piece is worth ten gold pieces.
Copper (cp).entries.5: A standard coin weighs about a third of an ounce, so fifty coins weigh a pound.
Copper (cp).name: Copper (cp)
Copper Alms-Coin.name: Copper Alms-Coin
Copper Dragon Scale Mail.name: Copper Dragon Scale Mail
Copper Zib.name: Copper Zib
Copper chalice with silver filigree.name: Copper chalice with silver filigree
Copper stein with silver filigree (Braze: Copper stein with silver filigree (Brazen Coalition)
Copper.name: Медный
Coral.name: Coral
Costume.entries.0: While wearing a Costume, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any ability check you make to impersonate the person or type of person it represents.
Costume.name: Costume
Cotton Cloth (1 sq. yd.).name: Cotton Cloth (1 sq. yd.)
Covered Wagon.entries.0: This vehicle is a {@item Wagon|XPHB} covered by durable canvas. A creature has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover} while riding inside this vehicle.
Covered Wagon.name: Covered Wagon
Cow.name: Cow
Cracked Driftglobe.entries.0: This small sphere of thick glass weighs 1 pound. If you are within 60 feet of it, you can speak its command word and cause it to emanate the {@spell light} spell.
Cracked Driftglobe.name: Cracked Driftglobe
Crampons.entries.0: A crampon is a metal plate with spikes that is strapped to the sole of a boot. A creature wearing crampons can't fall {@condition prone} while moving across {@hazard slippery ice}.
Crampons.name: Crampons
Crook of Rao.entries.0: Ages ago, the serene god Rao created a tool to shield his fledgling faithful against the evils of the Lower Planes. Yet, as eons passed, mortals developed their own methods of dealing with existential threats, and the crook was largely forgotten. In recent ages, though, the Crook of Rao was rediscovered and leveraged against the rising power of the Witch Queen Iggwilv (one of the names of the wizard Tasha). Although she was defeated, Iggwilv managed to damage the crook during the battle, infecting it with an insidious curse-and the potential for future victory. In the aftermath, the crook was again lost. Occasionally it reappears, but the famed artifact is not what it was. Whether or not the artifact's bearers realize its full threat, few risk using the Crook of Rao -potentially for the final time.
Crook of Rao.entries.1.entries.0: The artifact has the following random properties, which you can determine by rolling on the tables in the "Artifacts" section of the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|DMG}:
Crook of Rao.entries.1.entries.1.items.0: 2 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Beneficial Properties|dmg|minor beneficial} properties
Crook of Rao.entries.1.entries.1.items.1: 1 {@table Artifact Properties; Major Beneficial Properties|dmg|major beneficial} property
Crook of Rao.entries.1.entries.1.items.2: 1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|dmg|minor detrimental} property
Crook of Rao.entries.1.name: Random Properties
Crook of Rao.entries.2.entries.0: The crook has 6 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells (save {@dc 18}) from it: {@spell aura of life} (2 charges), {@spell aura of purity} (2 charges), {@spell banishment} (1 charge), {@spell beacon of hope} (1 charge), {@spell mass cure wounds} (3 charges). The crook regains {@dice 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn.
Crook of Rao.entries.2.name: Заклинания
Crook of Rao.entries.3.entries.0: While you are attuned to the crook and holding it, you can spend 10 minutes to banish all but the mightiest fiends within 1 mile of you. Any fiend with a challenge rating of 19 or higher is unaffected. Each banished fiend is sent back to its home plane and can't return to the plane the Crook of Rao banished it from for 100 years.
Crook of Rao.entries.3.name: Absolute Banishment
Crook of Rao.entries.4.entries.0: Whenever the Crook of Rao's Absolute Banishment property is used, or when its last charge is expended, roll on the Extraplanar Reversal table. Any creatures conjured as a result of this effect appear in random unoccupied spaces within 60 feet of you and are not under your control.
Crook of Rao.entries.4.name: Failing Matrix
Crook of Rao.entries.5.entries.0: When the Crook was last used against Iggwilv, the Witch Queen lashed out at the artifact, infecting its magical matrix. Over the years, this curse has spread within the crook, threatening to violently pervert its ancient magic. If this occurs, the Crook of Rao, as it is currently known, is destroyed, its magical matrix inverting and exploding into a 50-foot-diameter portal. This portal functions as a permanent {@spell gate} spell cast by Iggwilv. The gate then, once per round on initiative count 20, audibly speaks a fiend's name in Iggwilv's voice, doing so until the gate calls on every fiend ever banished by the Crook of Rao. If the fiend still exists, it is drawn through the gate. This process takes eighteen years to complete, at the end of which the gate becomes a permanent portal to Pazunia, the first layer of the Abyss.
Crook of Rao.entries.5.entries.1.caption: Extraplanar Reversal
A portal to a random plane opens. The portal closes after 5 minutes.
{@dice 1d10} {@creature barbed devil||barbed devils} and {@dice 1d10} {@creature vrock||vrocks} appear.
1 {@creature arcanaloth}, 1 {@creature night hag}, and 1 {@creature rakshasa} appear.
1 {@creature balor} and 1 {@creature pit fiend} appear. At the DM's discretion, a portal opens into the presence of an archdevil or demon lord instead, then closes after 5 minutes.
Iggwilv's Curse (see the Iggwilv's Curse property).
Crook of Rao.entries.5.name: Iggwilv's Curse
Crook of Rao.entries.6.entries.0: The Crook of Rao can either be destroyed or repaired by journeying to Mount Celestia and obtaining a tear from the eternally serene god Rao. One way to make the emotionless god cry would be to reunite Rao with the spirit of his first worshiper who sought revelations beyond the multiverse long ago. The Crook dissolves if immersed in the god's tear for a year and a day. If washed in the tear daily for 30 days, the Crook loses its Failing Matrix property.
Crook of Rao.entries.6.name: Destroying or Repairing the Crook
Crook of Rao.name: Crook of Rao
Crossbow Bolt Case.entries.0: A {@item Crossbow Bolt Case|XPHB} holds up to 20 Bolts.
Crossbow Bolt Case.name: Crossbow Bolt Case
Crowbar.entries.0: Using a Crowbar gives you {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks where the Crowbar's leverage can be applied.
Crowbar.name: Crowbar
Crown of Horns.entries.0: The Crown of Horns contains the essence and intelligence of Myrkul, one of the Dead Three. This ghastly crown is a pale silver circlet with four curved bones set around its rim. On the crown's brow is set a black diamond whose depths swirl with weird, malignant energy.
Crown of Horns.entries.1: Myrkul created the Crown of Horns at the height of his power. When Myrkul was slain by Mystra, he imbued the crown with his dying essence. Myrkul has since returned to become one of the Dead Three, and the Crown of Horns continues to further his gruesome will by seeking out mortal agents and gradually transforming them into powerful Undead scions. Should a wearer prove unworthy of this honor, the crown teleports away to find a new mortal to corrupt for its master.
Crown of Horns.entries.10.entries.0: The crown contains the essence of Myrkul and exists to spread that god's will, which is to slay the living, raise the dead, and sow fear across the Material Plane. The crown urges its wearer to spread dread and destruction wherever possible.
Crown of Horns.entries.10.name: Personality
Crown of Horns.entries.11.entries.0: The crown is destroyed if it is worn by Myrkul's successor, Kelemvor, while he sits on the throne of Myrkul's former realm, the Bone Castle.
Crown of Horns.entries.11.name: Destroying the Crown
Crown of Horns.entries.2.entries.0: The crown is cursed. Attuning to the crown extends the curse to you. Only a {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell or the will of Myrkul can remove this curse. As long as you remain cursed, the following properties apply to you:
Crown of Horns.entries.2.entries.1.items: You are unwilling to part with the crown, wearing it at all times, and you can't voluntarily end your {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to the crown.

You slowly transform into a monstrous servant of Myrkul over the course of 365 days. During that time, the transformation is purely cosmetic; these cosmetic changes slowly fade if you end {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to the crown before completing the transformation. When the transformation is complete, you become a {@creature Lich|XMM} under the DM's control. Each time you use this Artifact's {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} of Undeath or Myrkul's Hand benefit, there is a 10 percent cumulative chance that you instantly complete this transformation.
Crown of Horns.entries.2.name: Cursed
Crown of Horns.entries.3.entries.0: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to unleash a wave of unholy light in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. Each creature in the {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} makes a DC 18 Constitution saving throw, taking ({@dice 4d8}) Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A Humanoid killed by {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} of Undeath rises at the start of your next turn as a {@creature Specter|XMM} that follows your verbal orders.
Crown of Horns.entries.3.name: Cone of Undeath
Crown of Horns.entries.4.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Necrotic damage.
Crown of Horns.entries.4.name: Damage Immunity
Crown of Horns.entries.5.entries.0: While wearing the crown, you emit an aura of terrifying energy in a 60-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. A creature that enters this area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition. A {@condition Frightened|XPHB} target must use its movement on its turns to get as far away as possible from you, moving by the safest route.
Crown of Horns.entries.5.entries.1: If you damage a {@condition Frightened|XPHB} target or if a {@condition Frightened|XPHB} target ends its turn more than 120 feet away from you, the target can repeat the Wisdom save, ending the condition on itself on a success. A creature that succeeds on the save against this effect is immune to it for 1 minute, after which it can be affected again.
Crown of Horns.entries.5.entries.2: The aura is inactive while you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. You can activate or deactivate the aura as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Crown of Horns.entries.5.name: Fear Aura
Crown of Horns.entries.6.entries.0: While wearing the crown, you can cast the {@spell Power Word Kill|XPHB} spell (save DC 18) from it. A Humanoid killed by this spell rises at the start of your next turn as a {@creature Wraith|XMM} that follows your verbal orders. Once you use the crown to cast this spell, you can't cast it again from the crown until the next dawn.
Crown of Horns.entries.6.name: Myrkul's Hand
Crown of Horns.entries.7.entries.0: While wearing the crown, you can cast the {@spell Teleport|XPHB} spell. The crown can also cast this spell of its own free will. Once the spell has been cast using the crown, it can't be cast again until 7 days have passed.
Crown of Horns.entries.7.name: Телепортация
Crown of Horns.entries.8.entries.0: The Artifact has the following random properties (see chapter 7 of the Dungeon Master's Guide):
Crown of Horns.entries.8.entries.1.items: 1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Beneficial Properties|XDMG|minor beneficial} property
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|XDMG|minor detrimental} property
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Major Detrimental Properties|XDMG|major detrimental} property
Crown of Horns.entries.8.name: Random Properties
Crown of Horns.entries.9.entries.0: The Crown of Horns is a sentient Neutral Evil Artifact with an Intelligence of 10, a Wisdom of 16, and a Charisma of 19. It has hearing and {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} out to 120 feet.
Crown of Horns.entries.9.entries.1: The crown communicates telepathically with its wearer and knows Common.
Crown of Horns.entries.9.name: Sentience
Crown of Horns.name: Crown of Horns
Crown of Lies.entries.0: After betraying and nearly destroying the lich Vecna, the warrior Kas found himself trapped in the Shadowfell, imprisoned in a Domain of Dread called Tovag. There, he languished as a vampire. In time, the Dark Powers of the Domain of Dread lured Kas to a hidden forge, where he found the Crown of Lies. Once Kas vowed to deliver Vecna into the Dark Powers' clutches and donned the crown, the Dark Powers released Kas. From there, Kas set out to ruin his former master. Should Kas fail, the Dark Powers will reclaim him.
Crown of Lies.entries.1: The crown is made of burnished and entwined metal rods. To attune to it, you must place it on your head and speak a true desire of your heart. You know how to attune to the crown when you touch it.
Crown of Lies.entries.2.entries.0: The Crown of Lies has the following random properties (see the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|DMG} for options):
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Beneficial Properties|dmg|minor beneficial} property
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Major Beneficial Properties|dmg|major beneficial} property
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|dmg|minor detrimental} property
Crown of Lies.entries.2.name: Random Properties
Crown of Lies.entries.3.entries.0: While attuned to the crown, you can use an action to transform yourself to look and feel like any creature you've seen at least once and whose size is no more than one size smaller or larger than yours.
Crown of Lies.entries.3.entries.1: The new form mimics the chosen creature's appearance exactly, including its voice. Your size and speed are replaced by the chosen creature's. You otherwise retain your own game statistics. While in this new form, the crown melds into your person and is undetectable.
Crown of Lies.entries.3.entries.2: Your new form lasts until you die, your attunement to the crown ends, or you use another action to transform into a different creature or your true form. Interactions with you while you are transformed by the crown reveal no illusory magic, nor do they reveal anything other than details about the creature you're disguised as. You count as the chosen creature for the purposes of spells, traps, and other defenses that wouldn't target the chosen creature.
Crown of Lies.entries.3.entries.3: While in your disguised form, any lies you tell always seem to be true, no matter what magical or mundane methods are used to try to detect your falsehoods. You are the recipient of {@spell Sending} spells addressed to you and the creature you are disguised as, and {@spell Scrying} and similar spells that target the creature you are disguised as actually target you. The only way to reveal your true nature while transformed by the crown is with a {@spell Wish} spell.
Crown of Lies.entries.3.entries.4: While wearing this crown in your true form, you can choose for the crown to be visible if you wish.
Crown of Lies.entries.3.name: Perfect Disguise
Crown of Lies.entries.4.entries.0: If a creature wearing the crown is killed by the creature it is disguised as, the crown disintegrates and is destroyed.
Crown of Lies.entries.4.name: Destroying the Crown
Crown of Lies.name: Crown of Lies
Crown of Whirling Comets.entries.0: This delicate silver tiara is decorated with stellar iconography. While you wear the crown, the gems on the tiara's points detach and closely orbit your head.
Crown of Whirling Comets.entries.1: The crown has 6 charges for the following properties, which you can use while wearing the crown:
Crown of Whirling Comets.entries.2.items: As a bonus action, you can spend 1 charge to gain the power of flight for 10 minutes. For the duration, you gain a flying speed equal to your walking speed, and you can hover. While flying, you glow faintly with starlight.

As an action, you can spend any number of charges to launch bolts of frigid starlight. You launch a number of bolts equal to the number of charges spent, and you can direct the bolts to target one creature or several, so long as all creatures are within 120 feet of you and you can see them. The bolts automatically strike their targets, and each bolt deals {@damage 2d4} cold damage.
As an action, you can spend 3 charges and cast the {@spell Ice Storm} spell (save {@dc 16}).
Crown of Whirling Comets.entries.3: The crown regains {@dice 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn.
Crown of Whirling Comets.name: Crown of Whirling Comets
Crown of the Forest.entries.0: While wearing this crown, a character automatically fails any Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check made to see through an illusion. An illusion that is touched still has its true nature revealed. However, a character wearing a crown has such a strong sense that the illusion is real that they might not know which of their senses to believe.
Crown of the Forest.entries.1: If this crown is closely examined by a character who succeeds on a {@dc 20} Intelligence ({@skill Nature}) or Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check, or with a {@spell detect magic} spell cast using a 3rd-level or higher spell slot, the nature of its magic is discovered.
Crown of the Forest.name: Crown of the Forest
Crown of the Wrath Bringer.entries.0: This jagged iron circlet bears ornaments in the shape of the enemy rune. When worn, the crown glows with pale light as it draws upon the wearer's fury to strike opponents with vicious terror.
Crown of the Wrath Bringer.entries.1: When you make an attack roll against a creature and hit it while wearing this crown, you can spend and roll one of your unspent Hit Dice. The creature takes extra psychic damage equal to the number rolled.
Crown of the Wrath Bringer.entries.2.ent: As an action, you can invoke the crown's rune to cast the {@spell fear} spell (save {@dc 15}) with it; the spell has a duration of 1 minute and doesn't require {@status concentration}. Once the rune has been invoked, it can't be invoked again until the next dawn.
Crown of the Wrath Bringer.name: Crown of the Wrath Bringer
Crusaders Shortsword.entries.0: The Crusader's Shortsword is a sentient lawful good {@item +1 shortsword} (Intelligence 11, Wisdom 13, Charisma 13). It has hearing and normal vision out to a range of 120 feet. It communicates by transmitting emotion to the creature carrying or wielding it.
Crusaders Shortsword.entries.1: The sword's purpose is to fight evil. The sword has the following additional properties:
Crusaders Shortsword.entries.2.items.0: The sword continually sheds bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet. Only by destroying the sword can this light be extinguished.
Crusaders Shortsword.entries.2.items.1: A lawful good creature can attune itself to the sword in 1 minute.
Crusaders Shortsword.entries.2.items.2: While attuned to the weapon, the sword's wielder can use the sword to cast the {@spell crusader's mantle} spell. Once used, this property of the sword can't be used again until the next dawn.
Crusaders Shortsword.name: Crusader's Shortsword
Crystal Ball of Mind Reading.entries.0: While touching this crystal orb, you can cast {@spell Scrying|XPHB} (save {@dc 17}) with it. In addition, you can cast {@spell Detect Thoughts|XPHB} (save {@dc 17}) targeting creatures you can see within 30 feet of the spell's sensor. You don't need to concentrate on this {@spell Detect Thoughts|XPHB} spell to maintain it during its duration, but it ends if the {@spell Scrying|XPHB} spell ends.
Crystal Ball of Mind Reading.name: Crystal Ball of Mind Reading
Crystal Ball of Telepathy.entries.0: While touching this crystal orb, you can cast {@spell Scrying|XPHB} (save {@dc 17}) with it. In addition, you can communicate telepathically with creatures you can see within 30 feet of the spell's sensor. You can also cast {@spell Suggestion|XPHB} (save {@dc 17}) through the sensor on one of those creatures. You don't need to concentrate on this {@spell Suggestion|XPHB} to maintain it during its duration, but it ends if {@spell Scrying|XPHB} ends.
