Hand of Vecna.entries.6.entries.0: If the {@item Eye of Vecna|XDMG} and the Hand of Vecna are both attached to the same creature and that creature is slain by the {@item Sword of Kas|XDMG}, both the eye and the hand burst into flame, turn to ash, and are destroyed. Any other attempt to destroy the eye or hand seems to work, but the Artifact reappears in one of Vecna's many hidden vaults, where it waits to be rediscovered.
Hand of Vecna.entries.6.name: Destroying the Eye and Hand
Hand of Vecna.name: Hand of Vecna
Handheld mirror set in a painted wooden: Handheld mirror set in a painted wooden frame
Harbor Moon.entries.0: Platinum crescent inset with electrum, about three inches long with a hole large enough for a {@item nib|wdh} to fit in (1 harbor moon = 5,000 {@item nib|wdh|nibs})
Harbor Moon.name: Harbor Moon
Harkons Bite.entries.0: A dire wolf tooth dangles from this simple cord necklace. While you wear it, the necklace grants you a +1 bonus to ability checks and saving throws.
Harkons Bite.entries.1.entries.0: Attuning to Harkon's Bite curses you until either Harkon Lukas removes the necklace from you or you are targeted by a {@spell remove curse} spell or similar magic. As long as you remain cursed, you cannot remove the necklace.
Harkons Bite.entries.1.entries.1: Upon donning or removing the necklace, whether you are attuned to it or not, you are afflicted with {@variantrule Player Characters as Lycanthropes|mm|werewolf lycanthropy} as detailed in the Monster Manual. The curse lasts until the dawn after the next full moon. If you are still wearing the necklace at this time, you are afflicted with the lycanthropy again.
Harkons Bite.entries.1.name: Проклятие
Harkons Bite.name: Harkon's Bite
Harp of Gilded Plenty.entries.0: This golden harp is sculpted in the image of the god Iallanis, depicted as a young cloud giant woman. When a creature comes within 5 feet of the harp, the instrument animates and is capable of speaking, singing, and playing by itself.
Harp of Gilded Plenty.entries.1: Whenever you attempt to attune to the harp, you must first make either a {@dc 15} Charisma ({@skill Performance}) check or a {@dc 20} Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) check to convince the harp that you are worthy, attuning to the harp on a success. If you fail, you can't attempt to attune to the harp again until the next dawn. Once you have successfully attuned to the harp, the harp resizes to suit you.
Whenever you make a Charisma check while attuned to the harp, you can treat a roll of 9 or lower on the die as a 10.
Harp of Gilded Plenty.entries.2.name: Stalwart Song
If you spend 10 minutes playing the harp, you can cast the {@spell heroes' feast} spell from it. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until {@dice 1d10 + 10} days have passed.
Harp of Gilded Plenty.entries.3.name: Feast of Plenty
As an action, you can use the harp to cast the {@spell calm emotions} spell (save {@dc 19}). When the spell is cast using the harp, its duration increases to 1 hour, provided you maintain {@status concentration} on the spell. This property can be used five times, and it regains all uses at dawn.
Harp of Gilded Plenty.entries.4.name: Soothing Melody
The harp is a sentient, chaotic good object with an Intelligence of 13, a Wisdom of 15, and a Charisma of 20. It has hearing and {@sense darkvision} to a range of 120 feet.
The harp can speak, read, and understand Common and Giant. It can also communicate telepathically with the creature attuned to it.
The harp has a dramatic and pompous personality, taking extreme pride in the quality of music produced from its strings. If the harp is shorter than 6 feet tall, it bemoans its height.
Harp of Gilded Plenty.entries.5.name: Sentience
Harp of Gilded Plenty.name: Harp of Gilded Plenty
Harper Pin.entries.0: When you attune to this pin, choose a Harper persona (see the {@book Forgotten Realms: Heroes of Faerûn|FRHoF|6|Harper Personas} for examples), including an alignment and a creature type. While wearing this pin, you register as that persona when targeted by magic to determine your creature type, alignment, or location.
Harper Pin.entries.1: The type of pin determines its rarity and effects.
Harper Pin.entries.2.entries.0: When you attune to this pin, you can devise a general line of thought of 25 words or fewer. While you wear this pin, a creature that reads your thoughts detects your preprogrammed line of thought instead. A creature that takes the {@action Study|XPHB} action while reading your thoughts makes a DC 13 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check. On a successful check, it becomes aware that the detected thoughts are preprogrammed.
Harper Pin.entries.2.name: Silver Harper Pin
Harper Pin.entries.3.entries.0: While wearing this pin, you can cast the {@spell Nondetection|XPHB} spell on yourself. The duration of this spell is permanent until you disable the effect (no action required), you remove the pin, or you are no longer attuned to the pin.
Harper Pin.entries.3.name: Golden Harper Pin
Harper Pin.name: Harper Pin
Harper Trinket.entries.0.caption: Harper Trinkets
Harper Trinket.entries.0.rows.1.1: A powder compact with a hidden compartment
Harper Trinket.entries.0.rows.3.1: A trick coin that's heads on both sides
Harper Trinket.entries.0.rows.5.1: A scroll of sheet music for an unknown instrument
Harper Trinket.name: Harper Trinket
Hat of Disguise.entries.0: While wearing this hat, you can cast the {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB} spell. The spell ends if the hat is removed.
Hat of Disguise.name: Hat of Disguise
Hat of Many Spells.entries.0: This pointed hat has the following properties.
Hat of Many Spells.entries.1.entries.0: While holding the hat, you can use it as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Wizard spells. Any spell you cast using the hat gains a special Somatic component: you must reach into the hat and "pull" the spell out of it.
Hat of Many Spells.entries.1.name: Заклинательная фокусировка
Hat of Many Spells.entries.2.entries.0: While holding the hat, you can try to cast a level 1+ spell you don't know. The spell must be on the Wizard spell list, it must be of a level you can cast, and it can't have Material components costing more than 1,000 GP. Once you decide on the spell, you must expend a spell slot of the spell's level. Then, to determine whether you cast the spell, make an Intelligence ({@skill Arcana|XPHB}) check ({@dc 10} plus the spell's level). On a successful check, you cast the spell using its normal casting time, and you can't use this property again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. On a failed check, you fail to cast the spell and a random effect occurs instead, determined by rolling on the following table.
Hat of Many Spells.entries.2.entries.1: Any spell you cast from the hat uses your spell save DC and spell attack bonus.
Hat of Many Spells.entries.2.entries.2.r: You cast a random spell determined by rolling {@dice 1d10}: on a {@b 1}, {@spell Enlarge/Reduce|XPHB} (enlarge effect); on a {@b 2}, {@spell Enlarge/Reduce|XPHB} (reduce effect); on a {@b 3}, {@spell Faerie Fire|XPHB}; on a {@b 4}, {@spell Fireball|XPHB}; on a {@b 5}, {@spell Gust of Wind|XPHB}; on a {@b 6}, {@spell Invisibility|XPHB} (cast on yourself); on a {@b 7}, {@spell Lightning Bolt|XPHB}; on an {@b 8}, {@spell Phantasmal Force|XPHB}; on a {@b 9}, {@spell Polymorph|XPHB}; on a {@b 10}, {@spell Stinking Cloud|XPHB}.
You have the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn, believing something awesome just happened.

You pull a magic item out of the hat. Roll {@dice 1d6} to determine the item's rarity: on a {@b 1-3}, {@filter Common|items|rarity=Common}; on a {@b 4-5}, {@filter Uncommon|items|rarity=Uncommon}; on a {@b 6}, {@filter Rare|items|rarity=Rare}. The DM chooses the item, which disappears after 1 hour if it's not consumed or destroyed before then.
A harmless swarm of butterflies fills a 10-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} within 30 feet of yourself. The swarm disperses after 1 minute.

You pull a nonmagical object out of the hat. Roll {@dice 1d4} to determine the object: on a {@b 1}, a vial of Acid; on a {@b 2}, a flask of Alchemist's Fire; on a {@b 3}, a Crowbar; on a {@b 4}, a lit Torch.
You suffer a bout of "magic sickness" and have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 hour.
You have the {@condition Petrified|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.

You pull a nonmagical object out of the hat. Roll {@dice 1d4} to determine the object: on a {@b 1}, a {@item Dagger|XPHB}; on a {@b 2}, a {@item Rope|XPHB} with a {@item Grappling Hook|XPHB} tied to one end; on a {@b 3}, a bag of {@item Caltrops|XPHB}; on a {@b 4}, a gem worth 50 GP.

A creature appears in an unoccupied space as close to you as possible. The creature isn't under your control and acts as it normally would, and it disappears after 1 hour or when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. Roll {@dice 1d4} to determine the creature: on a {@b 1}, a {@creature Camel|XMM}; on a {@b 2}, a {@creature Constrictor Snake|XMM}; on a {@b 3}, an {@creature Elephant|XMM}; on a {@b 4}, a {@creature Mule|XMM}.
A {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} {@creature Swarm of Bats|XMM} flies out of the hat, occupies your space, and attacks you.

A vertical, 10-foot-diameter, two-way portal to another plane of existence opens in an unoccupied space within 30 feet of you and remains open until the end of your next turn. The DM determines where it leads.
Hat of Many Spells.entries.2.name: Unknown Spell
Hat of Many Spells.name: Hat of Many Spells
Hat of Vermin.entries.0: This hat has 3 charges. While holding the hat, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to expend 1 charge and summon your choice of a {@creature Bat|XMM}, a {@creature Frog|XMM}, or a {@creature Rat|XMM}. The summoned creature magically appears in the hat and tries to get away from you as quickly as possible. The creature is {@variantrule Indifferent [Attitude]|XPHB|Indifferent} toward you and other creatures, and it isn't under your control. It behaves as an ordinary creature of its kind and disappears after 1 hour or when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. The hat regains all expended charges daily at dawn.
Hat of Vermin.name: Hat of Vermin
Hat of Vortexes.entries.0: This hat has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and expend 1 charge while holding the hat to release a magical vortex from it. The vortex fills a 10-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} originating from you and lasts for 1 hour. While the vortex is present, its area is {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.
Hat of Vortexes.entries.1: You decide a vortex's visual details when you create it. For instance, the vortex might be multicolored or glittery.
Hat of Vortexes.name: Hat of Vortexes
Hat of Wizardry.entries.0: This cone-shaped hat is adorned with moons and stars. While you are wearing it, you gain the following benefits.
Hat of Wizardry.entries.1.entries.0: You can use the hat as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Wizard spells.
Hat of Wizardry.entries.1.name: Заклинательная фокусировка
Hat of Wizardry.entries.2.entries.0: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can try to cast a cantrip that you don't know. The cantrip must be on the Wizard spell list and have a casting time of an action, and you make a {@dc 10} Intelligence ({@skill Arcana|XPHB}) check. On a successful check, you cast the spell. On a failed check, the spell fails, and the action used to cast the spell is wasted. In either case, you can't use this property again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Hat of Wizardry.entries.2.name: Unknown Spell
Hat of Wizardry.name: Hat of Wizardry
Hazirawn.entries.0: A sentient (neutral evil) greatsword, Hazirawn is capable of speech in Common and Netherese. Even if you aren't attuned to the sword, you gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. If you are not attuned to Hazirawn, you deal an extra {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage when you hit with the weapon.
Hazirawn.entries.1.entries.0: While you are attuned to this weapon, its bonus on attack and damage rolls increases to +2, and a hit deals an extra {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage (instead of {@dice 1d6}).
Hazirawn.entries.1.name: Increased Potency
Hazirawn.entries.2.entries.0: Hazirawn has 4 charges to cast spells. As long as the sword is attuned to you and you are holding it in your hand, you can cast {@spell detect magic} (1 charge), {@spell detect evil and good} (1 charge), or {@spell detect thoughts} (2 charges). Each night at midnight, Hazirawn regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges.
Hazirawn.entries.2.name: Заклинания
Hazirawn.entries.3.entries.0: While you are attuned to the weapon, any creature that you hit with Hazirawn can't regain hit points for 1 minute. The target can make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending this effect early on a success.
Hazirawn.entries.3.name: Wounding
Hazirawn.name: Hazirawn
Headband of Intellect.entries.0: Your Intelligence score is 19 while you wear this headband. It has no effect on you if your Intelligence is 19 or higher without it.
Headband of Intellect.name: Headband of Intellect
Healers Kit.entries.0: A Healer's Kit has ten uses. As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can expend one of its uses to stabilize an {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} creature that has 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} without needing to make a Wisdom ({@skill Medicine|XPHB}) check.
Healers Kit.name: Healer's Kit
Heart Weavers Primer.entries.0: This pristine book smells faintly of a random scent you find pleasing. When found, the book contains the following spells: {@spell antipathy/sympathy}, {@spell charm person}, {@spell dominate person}, {@spell enthrall}, {@spell hypnotic pattern}, {@spell modify memory}, and {@spell suggestion}. It functions as a spellbook for you.
Heart Weavers Primer.entries.1: While you are holding the book, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your wizard spells.
Heart Weavers Primer.entries.2: The book has 3 charges, and it regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. You can use the charges in the following ways while holding it:
Heart Weavers Primer.entries.3.items.0: If you spend 1 minute studying the book, you can expend 1 charge to replace one of your prepared wizard spells with a different spell in the book. The new spell must be of the enchantment school.
Heart Weavers Primer.entries.3.items.1: When you cast an enchantment spell, you can expend 1 charge to impose disadvantage on the first saving throw one target makes against the spell.
Heart Weavers Primer.name: Heart Weaver's Primer
Heartstone.entries.0: This lustrous black gem allows a {@creature night hag} to become ethereal while it is in her possession. The touch of a heartstone also cures any disease. Crafting a heartstone takes 30 days.
Heartstone.name: Heartstone
Hell Hound Cloak.entries.0: This dark cloak is made of cured {@creature hell hound} hide. As an action, you can command the cloak to transform you into a {@creature hell hound} for up to 1 hour. The transformation otherwise functions as the {@spell polymorph} spell, but you can use a bonus action to revert to your normal form.
Hell Hound Cloak.entries.1.entries.0: This cloak is cursed with the essence of a {@creature hell hound}, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. Until the curse is broken with {@spell remove curse} or similar magic, you are unwilling to part with the cloak, keeping it within reach at all times.
Hell Hound Cloak.entries.1.name: Проклятие
Hell Hound Cloak.entries.2: The sixth time you use the cloak, and each time thereafter, you must make a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the transformation lasts until dispelled or until you drop to 0 hit points, and you can't willingly return to normal form. If you ever remain in {@creature hell hound} form for 6 hours, the transformation becomes permanent and you lose your sense of self. All your statistics are then replaced by those of a {@creature hell hound}. Thereafter, only {@spell remove curse} or similar magic allows you to regain your identity and return to normal. If you remain in this permanent form for 6 days, only a {@spell wish} spell can reverse the transformation.
Hell Hound Cloak.name: Hell Hound Cloak
Helm of Awareness.entries.0: While wearing this helmet, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls.
Helm of Awareness.name: Helm of Awareness
Helm of Brilliance.entries.0: This helm is set with {@dice 1d10} diamonds, {@dice 2d10} rubies, {@dice 3d10} fire opals, and {@dice 4d10} opals. Any gem pried from the helm crumbles to dust. When all the gems are removed or destroyed, the helm loses its magic.
Helm of Brilliance.entries.1: You gain the following benefits while wearing the helm.
Helm of Brilliance.entries.2.entries.0: As long as it has at least one diamond, the helm emits a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. When at least one Undead is within that area, the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} is filled with {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB}. Any Undead that starts its turn in that area takes {@damage 1d6} Radiant damage.
Helm of Brilliance.entries.2.name: Diamond Light
Helm of Brilliance.entries.3.entries.0: As long as the helm has at least one fire opal, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cause one weapon you are holding to burst into flames. The flames emit {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet. The flames are harmless to you and the weapon. When you hit with an attack using the blazing weapon, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} Fire damage. The flames last until you take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to extinguish them or until you drop or stow the weapon.
Helm of Brilliance.entries.3.name: Fire Opal Flames
Helm of Brilliance.entries.4.entries.0: As long as the helm has at least one ruby, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire damage.
Helm of Brilliance.entries.4.name: Ruby Resistance
Helm of Brilliance.entries.5.entries.0: You can cast one of the following spells (save {@dc 18}), using one of the helm's gems of the specified type as a component: {@spell Daylight|XPHB} (opal), {@spell Fireball|XPHB} (fire opal), {@spell Prismatic Spray|XPHB} (diamond), or {@spell Wall of Fire|XPHB} (ruby). The gem is destroyed when the spell is cast and disappears from the helm.
Helm of Brilliance.entries.5.name: Заклинания
Helm of Brilliance.entries.6.entries.0: Roll {@dice 1d20} if you are wearing the helm and take Fire damage as a result of failing a saving throw against a spell. On a roll of 1, the helm emits beams of light from its remaining gems and is then destroyed. Each creature within a 60-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you must succeed on a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw or be struck by a beam, taking Radiant damage equal to the number of gems in the helm.
Helm of Brilliance.entries.6.name: Taking Fire Damage
Helm of Brilliance.name: Helm of Brilliance
While wearing this helm, you can cast {@spell Comprehend Languages|XPHB} from it.
Helm of Comprehending Languages.name: Helm of Comprehending Languages
Helm of Devil Command.entries.0: This bulky, eyeless helmet is made for a {@creature pit fiend} but magically resizes to fit the heads of other wearers.
Helm of Devil Command.entries.1: While wearing the helm, you can see out of it as though you weren't wearing it. In addition, you know the exact location and type of all devils within 1,000 feet of you. You can telepathically communicate with a devil within range, or you can broadcast your thoughts to all devils within range. The devils receiving your broadcasted thoughts have no special means of replying to them.
Helm of Devil Command.entries.2: The helm has 3 charges. As an action, you can expend 1 charge to cast {@spell dominate monster} (save {@dc 21}), which affects devils only. (The spell fails and the charge is wasted if you target any creature that's not a devil.) If a devil can see you when you cast this spell on it, the devil knows you tried to charm it. The helm regains all its charges 24 hours after its last charge is expended.
Helm of Devil Command.entries.3: If you are not a devil, using the helm's {@spell dominate monster} property in the Nine Hells has a {@chance 20} chance of attracting a {@creature narzugon|MTF}, which arrives on the back of a {@creature nightmare} mount in {@dice 1d4} hours. The narzugon tries to recover the helm, killing you if necessary to obtain it. If it gets the helm, the narzugon tries to deliver it to its infernal master.
Helm of Devil Command.name: Helm of Devil Command
Helm of Disjunction.entries.0: The wizard Mordenkainen crafted this sleek, golden helmet. If he created other helms of disjunction, they have yet to be found.
Helm of Disjunction.entries.1: As an action while wearing this helm, you can use it to emit an antimagic pulse in a 300-foot-radius sphere centered on yourself. The pulse has the following effects:
Helm of Disjunction.entries.2.entries.0: Potions and scrolls in the area are destroyed. All other magic items in the area have their magical properties suppressed for 1 minute. Artifacts are unaffected by this property, as are magic items on your person.
Helm of Disjunction.entries.2.name: Magic Item Disjunction
Helm of Disjunction.entries.3.entries.0: Any ongoing spell ends if the creature, object, or area affected by it is even partly in the pulse's area.
Helm of Disjunction.entries.3.name: Spell Disjunction
Helm of Disjunction.entries.4.entries.0: The antimagic pulse is strong enough to knock down creatures close to you. Each creature within 30 feet of you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or have the {@condition prone} condition.
Helm of Disjunction.entries.4.entries.1: Once you use the helm's property, it can't be used again until {@dice 1d4} days have passed.
Helm of Disjunction.entries.4.name: Wave of Force
Helm of Disjunction.name: Helm of Disjunction
Helm of Perfect Potential.entries.0: This copper-hued helm contains a shard of the Elemental Chaos embedded in its forehead, surrounded by a motif of a rising sun. Legend says Annam fashioned this helm for his daughter Diancastra to hold the fragment of chaos she used to prove her worth to her father.
Helm of Perfect Potential.entries.1.entr: The helm has the following random properties, determined by rolling on the appropriate table in the Dungeon Master's Guide:
2 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Beneficial Properties|dmg|minor beneficial} properties
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Major Beneficial Properties|dmg|major beneficial} property
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|dmg|minor detrimental} property
Helm of Perfect Potential.entries.1.name: Random Properties
Helm of Perfect Potential.entries.2.entr: While wearing the helm, you have advantage on Charisma ({@skill Deception}) and Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks.
Helm of Perfect Potential.entries.2.name: Master of Guile
Helm of Perfect Potential.entries.3.entr: As a bonus action while wearing the helm, you can launch a bolt of searing elemental energy toward a creature you can see within 90 feet of yourself. The target must make a {@dc 20} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 4d6} acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage (your choice). On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage.
Helm of Perfect Potential.entries.3.name: Arrow of Elemental Havoc
Helm of Perfect Potential.entries.4.entr: The helm has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d6} charges each dawn. As an action while wearing the helm, you can expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells (save {@dc 20}): {@spell elemental weapon} (1 charge), {@spell call lightning} (2 charges), {@spell wall of fire} (3 charges), {@spell conjure elemental} (4 charges), {@spell tsunami} (5 charges).
Helm of Perfect Potential.entries.4.name: Использование заклинаний
Helm of Perfect Potential.name: Helm of Perfect Potential
Helm of Telepathy.entries.0: While wearing this helm, you have telepathy with a range of 30 feet, and you can cast {@spell Detect Thoughts|XPHB} or {@spell Suggestion|XPHB} (save {@dc 13}) from the helm. Once either spell is cast from the helm, that spell can't be cast from it again until the next dawn.
Helm of Telepathy.name: Helm of Telepathy
Helm of Teleportation.entries.0: This helm has 3 charges. While wearing it, you can expend 1 charge to cast {@spell Teleport|XPHB} from it. The helm regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.
Helm of Teleportation.name: Helm of Teleportation
Helm of Underwater Action.entries.0: While wearing this brass helmet, you can breathe underwater, you gain {@sense darkvision} with a range of 60 feet, and you gain a swimming speed of 30 feet.
Helm of Underwater Action.name: Helm of Underwater Action
Helm of the Gods.entries.0: While wearing this helm, you know whether there is a celestial or fiend within 30 feet of you, as well as where the creature is located, provided the creature isn't behind {@quickref Cover||3||total cover}.
Helm of the Gods.entries.1: Whenever you finish a long rest while wearing the helm, you can pray to one of the gods listed on the Helm of the Gods table and store the listed spell in the helm, replacing any spell that is already stored there. The save DC for the spell is 13.
Helm of the Gods.entries.2: The helm has 3 charges. To cast a spell from the helm, you must expend 1 charge, and the helm regains {@dice 1d3} charges daily at dawn.
Helm of the Gods.name: Helm of the Gods
Helm of the Scavenger.entries.0: This ornate chair is designed to propel and maneuver a ship through space.
The following properties of the helm come into play even when no creature is attuned to it:

When placed aboard a vessel weighing between 1 and 100 tons, the helm generates an envelope of fresh air around the ship while it is in the void of space (but not underwater). This envelope extends out from the edges of the hull in all directions for a distance equal in length to the vessel's beam, so that creatures aboard and near the ship can breathe normally in space. The temperature within the air envelope is 70 degrees Fahrenheit.

When placed aboard a vessel weighing between 1 and 100 tons, the helm generates an artificial gravity field while the ship is in the void of space, so that creatures can walk on the ship's decks as they normally would. Creatures and objects that fall overboard bob in a gravity plane that extends out from the main deck for a distance equal in length to the vessel's beam.
Helm of the Scavenger.entries.1.name: Passive Properties
The sensation of being attuned to the helm is akin to being immersed in warm water. While attuned to the helm, you gain the following abilities while you sit in it:
You can use the helm to propel the vessel across or through water and other liquids at a maximum speed in miles per hour equal to your highest-level unexpended spell slot.
You can use the helm to propel the vessel through air or space at a maximum speed in miles per hour equal to your highest-level unexpended spell slot × 10.
Provided you have at least one unexpended spell slot, you can steer the vessel, albeit in a somewhat clumsy fashion, in much the same way that oars or a rudder can maneuver a seafaring ship.
Whenever you like, you can see what's happening on and around the vessel as though you were standing in a location of your choice aboard it.
Helm of the Scavenger.entries.2.name: Active Properties
While attuned to the helm, you cannot expend your own spell slots.
Helm of the Scavenger.entries.3.name: Drawback
Helm of the Scavenger.name: Helm of the Scavenger
Hematite.name: Hematite
Herbalism Kit.entries.0.items.0.name: Характеристика:
Herbalism Kit.entries.0.items.1.name: Использование:
{@item Antitoxin|XPHB}, {@item Candle|XPHB}, {@item Healer's Kit|XPHB}, {@item Potion of Healing|XDMG}
Herbalism Kit.entries.0.items.2.name: Craft:
Herbalism Kit.name: Набор травника
Heretic.entries.0: The blasphemous weapon Heretic was created by a cult to steal power from good-aligned gods. The blade hungers to strike down servants of the Upper Planes and weaken the forces of good.
Heretic.entries.1: You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Heretic has 6 charges for the following properties; the sword regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} charges daily at dawn:
Heretic.entries.2.entries.0: Once per turn when you hit a creature with this weapon, you can expend 1 of its charges to attempt to render the target powerless. The target must succeed on a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition paralyzed} condition until the start of your next turn. Celestials have disadvantage on the save.
Heretic.entries.2.name: Destroy Devotion
Heretic.entries.3.entries.0: While holding the sword, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells from it: {@spell Detect Evil and Good} (1 charge), {@spell Fly} (2 charges), or {@spell True Seeing} (3 charges).
Heretic.entries.3.name: Faith Hunter
Heretic.entries.4.entries.0: Heretic is a sentient, chaotic evil weapon with an Intelligence of 17, a Wisdom of 17, and a Charisma of 15. It has hearing and {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 120 feet.
Heretic.entries.4.entries.1: The weapon can speak, read, and understand Common and Giant, and it can communicate with its wielder telepathically. Its voice is soft and deep but rises in a wild furor when it's aware Celestials or worshipers of good-aligned deities are present. While you are attuned to it, Heretic also understands every language you know.
Heretic.entries.4.name: Sentience
Heretic.entries.5.entries.0: Heretic craves the destruction of good-aligned gods and their supporters. The blade is condescending and snobby, especially in the company of priests and other pious folk, at whom it often directs sneering comments. When Heretic identifies its quarry, it erupts in a frenzied need for violence.
Heretic.entries.5.name: Personality
Heretic.name: Heretic
Hew.entries.0: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Hew.entries.1: Hew is a +1 battleaxe that deals maximum damage when the wielder attacks a Plant creature or an object made of wood. The axe's creator used it for protection in the forest while he cut firewood.
Hew.name: Hew
Hewards Handy Haversack.entries.0: This backpack has a central pouch and two side pouches, each of which is an extradimensional space. Each side pouch can hold up to 200 pounds of material, not exceeding a volume of 25 cubic feet. The central pouch can hold up to 500 pounds of material, not exceeding a volume of 64 cubic feet. The haversack always weighs 5 pounds, regardless of its contents.
Hewards Handy Haversack.entries.1: Retrieving an item from the haversack requires a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action or a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} (your choice). When you reach into the haversack for a specific item, the item is always magically on top.
Hewards Handy Haversack.entries.2: If any of its pouches is overloaded, pierced, or torn, the haversack ruptures and is destroyed. If the haversack is destroyed, its contents are lost forever, although an Artifact always turns up again somewhere. If the haversack is turned inside out, its contents spill forth unharmed, and the haversack must be put right before it can be used again.
Hewards Handy Haversack.entries.3: Each pouch of the haversack holds enough air for 10 minutes of breathing, divided by the number of breathing creatures inside.
Hewards Handy Haversack.entries.4: Placing the haversack inside an extradimensional space created by a {@item Bag of Holding|XDMG}, {@item Portable Hole|XDMG}, or similar item instantly destroys both items and opens a gate to the Astral Plane. The gate originates where the one item was placed inside the other. Any creature within 10 feet of the gate and not behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} is sucked through it and deposited in a random location on the Astral Plane. The gate then closes. The gate is one-way only and can't be reopened.
Hewards Handy Haversack.name: Heward's Handy Haversack
Hewards Handy Spice Pouch.entries.0: This belt pouch appears empty and has 10 charges. While holding the pouch, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to expend 1 charge, name any nonmagical food seasoning (such as salt, pepper, saffron, or cilantro), and remove a pinch of the desired seasoning from the pouch. A pinch is enough to season a single meal. The pouch regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn.
Hewards Handy Spice Pouch.name: Heward's Handy Spice Pouch
Hewards Hireling Armor.entries.0: A number of Kwalish's experiments were attempts to research the works of the legendary mage Heward, who first crafted what he named hireling armor. While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC. In addition, the armor's animated straps can assist with the drawing and sheathing of weapons, such that you can draw or stow two one-handed weapons when you would normally be able to draw or stow only one.
Hewards Hireling Armor.entries.1: This armor also has six pockets, each of which is an extradimensional space. Each pocket can hold up to 20 pounds of material, not exceeding a volume of 2 cubic feet. The armor always weighs 10 pounds, regardless of its pockets' contents. Placing an object into one of the armor's pockets follows the normal rules for interacting with objects. Retrieving an item from a pocket of the armor requires you to use an action. When you reach into a pocket for a specific item, the item is always magically on top.
Hewards Hireling Armor.entries.2: Placing the armor inside an extradimensional space created by a {@item bag of holding}, a {@item Heward's handy haversack}, or a similar item instantly destroys both items and opens a gate to the Astral Plane. The gate originates where the one item was placed inside the other. Any creature within 10 feet of the gate is sucked through it and deposited in a random location on the Astral Plane. The gate then closes. The gate is one-way only and can't be reopened.
Hewards Hireling Armor.name: Heward's Hireling Armor
Hide of the Feral Guardian (Dormant).ent: It is believed that this polished and beautifully detailed leather armor was a gift from Melora, bestowed on a long-forgotten archdruid and champion of the natural world before the terrors of the Calamity.
While wearing the armor in its dormant state, you gain the following benefits:
The armor grants you a +1 bonus to AC.

While you are transformed by an effect that replaces any of your game statistics with those of another creature, you have a +1 bonus to melee attack and damage rolls, and you retain the benefits of this armor.

As an action, you can use the armor to cast {@spell polymorph} on yourself, transforming into a {@creature giant owl} while retaining your Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.
Hither-Thither Staff.entries.0: This walking stick has a brilliant gemstone fitted at the top. This staff has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.
Hither-Thither Staff.entries.1: As an action, you can expend 1 charge from the staff to create two linked teleportation portals, each one appearing on a flat surface of your choice that you can see within 1,500 feet of yourself. Alternatively, you can expend 1 charge as an action to relocate one or both portals, subject to the same limitation. The surface on which a portal appears must be big enough to accommodate it. Each portal is a two-dimensional, glowing oval ring, and together they create an open doorway up to 6 feet high and 4 feet wide. Any creature or object entering one portal exits from the other as if the two portals were a single opening that connects their locations.
Hither-Thither Staff.entries.2: A portal can appear on a moving surface, but the effect ends when the two portals move more than 1 mile apart. As a bonus action while holding the staff, you can close both portals (ending the effect) or suppress one portal, causing it to disappear until you relocate it (see above), during which time the remaining portal can't be used. The portals otherwise last for 24 hours.
Hither-Thither Staff.name: Hither-Thither Staff
Hollys Handy Haversack.entries.0: This backpack has a central pouch and two side pouches, each of which is an extradimensional space. Each side pouch can hold up to 200 pounds of material, not exceeding a volume of 25 cubic feet. The central pouch can hold up to 500 pounds of material, not exceeding a volume of 64 cubic feet. The haversack always weighs 5 pounds, regardless of its contents.
Hollys Handy Haversack.entries.1: Retrieving an item from the haversack requires a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action or a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} (your choice). When you reach into the haversack for a specific item, the item is always magically on top.
Hollys Handy Haversack.entries.2: If any of its pouches is overloaded, pierced, or torn, the haversack ruptures and is destroyed. If the haversack is destroyed, its contents are lost forever, although an Artifact always turns up again somewhere. If the haversack is turned inside out, its contents spill forth unharmed, and the haversack must be put right before it can be used again.
Hollys Handy Haversack.entries.3: Each pouch of the haversack holds enough air for 10 minutes of breathing, divided by the number of breathing creatures inside.
Hollys Handy Haversack.entries.4: Placing the haversack inside an extradimensional space created by a {@item Bag of Holding|XDMG}, {@item Portable Hole|XDMG}, or similar item instantly destroys both items and opens a gate to the Astral Plane. The gate originates where the one item was placed inside the other. Any creature within 10 feet of the gate and not behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} is sucked through it and deposited in a random location on the Astral Plane. The gate then closes. The gate is one-way only and can't be reopened.
Hollys Handy Haversack.name: Holly's Handy Haversack
Holy Symbol of Ravenkind.entries.0: The Holy Symbol of Ravenkind is a unique holy symbol sacred to the good-hearted faithful of Barovia. It predates the establishment of any church in Barovia. According to legend, it was delivered to a paladin named Lugdana by a giant raven—or an angel in the form of a giant raven. Lugdana used the holy symbol to root out and destroy nests of vampires until her death. The high priests of Ravenloft kept and wore the holy symbol after Lugdana's passing.
Holy Symbol of Ravenkind.entries.1: The holy symbol is a platinum amulet shaped like the sun, with a large crystal embedded in its center.
Holy Symbol of Ravenkind.entries.2: The holy symbol has 10 charges for the following properties. It regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} charges daily at dawn.
Holy Symbol of Ravenkind.entries.3.entri: As an action, you can expend 1 charge and present the holy symbol to make it flare with holy power. Vampires and {@creature vampire spawn} within 30 feet of the holy symbol when it flares must make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a target is {@condition paralyzed} for 1 minute. It can repeat the saving throw at the end of its turns to end the effect on itself.
Holy Symbol of Ravenkind.entries.3.name: Hold Vampires
Holy Symbol of Ravenkind.entries.4.entri: If you have the Turn Undead or the Turn the Unholy feature, you can expend 3 charges when you present the holy symbol while using that feature. When you do so, undead have disadvantage on their saving throws against the effect.
Holy Symbol of Ravenkind.entries.4.name: Turn Undead
Holy Symbol of Ravenkind.entries.5.entri: As an action, you can expend 5 charges while presenting the holy symbol to make it shed bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. The light is sunlight and lasts for 10 minutes or until you end the effect (no action required).
Holy Symbol of Ravenkind.entries.5.name: Sunlight
Holy Symbol of Ravenkind.name: Holy Symbol of Ravenkind
Holy Water.entries.0: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing a flask of Holy Water. Target one creature you can see within 20 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw ({@dc 8} plus your Dexterity modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or take {@damage 2d8} Radiant damage if it is a Fiend or an Undead.
Holy Water.name: Holy Water
Hooded Lantern.entries.0: A Hooded Lantern burns Oil as fuel to cast {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 30-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 30 feet. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can lower the hood, reducing the light to {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} in a 5-foot radius, or raise it again.
Hooded Lantern.name: Hooded Lantern
Hook of Fishers Delight.entries.0: This tiny silver fishhook has a little gold feather attached to it. For it to function, the feathered hook must be tied to the end of a fishing line and immersed in enough water to fill at least a 10-foot cube. At the end of each uninterrupted hour of immersion, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 6, a floppy, 6-inch-long magical fish appears on the end of the hook. The color and properties of the conjured fish are determined by rolling on the Hook of Fisher's Delight table. Once the hook conjures a fish, it can't do so again until the next dawn.
Hook of Fishers Delight.entries.1.rows.0: This tasty fish provides a day's worth of nourishment to one creature that eats it. The fish loses this property and rots if it's not eaten within 24 hours of being caught.
Hook of Fishers Delight.entries.1.rows.1: Once removed from the hook, this awful-tasting fish can be thrown up to 120 feet, targeting a creature the thrower can see. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}. The fish then disappears.
Hook of Fishers Delight.entries.1.rows.2: When released from the hook, this fish squirms free, sprouts wings, follows you around, and sings a beautiful tune in Aquan. It disappears after {@dice 2d4} hours or when reduced to 0 hit points. The fish uses the {@creature quipper} stat block, except that it can breathe air and has a flying speed of 30 feet.
Hook of Fishers Delight.entries.1.rows.3: This tasty fish provides a day's worth of nourishment to one creature that eats it and grants {@dice 2d10} temporary hit points to that creature. The fish loses these properties and rots if it's not eaten within 24 hours of being caught.
Hook of Fishers Delight.name: Hook of Fisher's Delight
Horizon Puzzle Cube.entries.0: A Horizon puzzle cube is an 8-inch magical cube made of gold, iron, crystal, and copper, and it is worth 5,000 gp to a sage or collector. A functioning puzzle cube can be solved with 30 minutes of work and a successful {@dc 25} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check. When the cube is solved, a 30-foot-diameter portal appears before the creature who solved the cube, leading to the northeastern platform of Horizon's Edge. The portal is two-way and remains open for 10 minutes or until a creature uses an action to change the puzzle's configuration. Puzzle cubes don't function while on Horizon's Edge, but a device somewhere in the demiplane allows a cube to function in reverse while the cube is fitted into it.
Horizon Puzzle Cube.name: Horizon Puzzle Cube
Horn of Beckoning Death.entries.0: Resembling a small dragon's horn, this arcane device glows with a hellish internal light when held. As an action while holding this horn, you can invoke a necromantic effect called the Beckoning Death.
Horn of Beckoning Death.entries.1: The Beckoning Death arrives at the start of your next turn and manifests as a crimson cloud of smoke that fills a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on the horn. The sphere doesn't move with the horn. At the start of each of your turns, the sphere's radius increases by 30 feet. The cloud disappears after 10 minutes or when your {@status concentration} on the effect ends. Any creature (including you) that has 9 or fewer hit points when it ends its turn in the cloud is slain by the Beckoning Death. A Humanoid killed in this way instantly transforms into a {@creature zombie} (see the Monster Manual). The zombie obeys the commands of the horn's creator, the lich Szass Tam.
Horn of Beckoning Death.entries.2: Once you use the horn's property, it can't be used again for 1 year.
Horn of Beckoning Death.name: Horn of Beckoning Death
Horn of Blasting.entries.0: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to blow the horn, which emits a thunderous blast in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} that is audible out to 600 feet. Each creature in the {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} makes a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 5d8} Thunder damage and has the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only. Glass or crystal objects in the {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} that aren't being worn or carried take {@damage 10d8} Thunder damage.
Horn of Blasting.entries.1: Each use of the horn's magic has a {@chance 20} chance of causing the horn to explode. The explosion deals {@damage 10d6} Force damage to the user and destroys the horn.
Horn of Blasting.name: Horn of Blasting
Horn of Silent Alarm.entries.0: This horn has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can blow the horn while expending 1 charge. One creature of your choice hears the horn's blare, provided that creature is within 600 feet of the horn. No other creature hears the horn.
Horn of Silent Alarm.name: Horn of Silent Alarm
Horn of Valhalla, Brass.entries.0: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to blow this horn. In response, warrior spirits from the plane of Ysgard appear in unoccupied spaces within 60 feet of you. Each spirit uses the {@creature Berserker|XMM} stat block and returns to Ysgard after 1 hour or when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. The spirits look like living, breathing warriors, and they have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions. Once you use the horn, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed.
Horn of Valhalla, Brass.entries.1: A brass horn summons 3 {@creature Berserker|XMM|Berserkers}. To use the brass horn, you must have {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB} with all Simple weapons.
Horn of Valhalla, Brass.entries.2: If you blow the horn without meeting its requirement, the summoned {@creature Berserker|XMM|Berserkers} attack you. If you meet the requirement, they are {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} to you and your allies and follow your commands.
Horn of Valhalla, Brass.name: Horn of Valhalla, Brass
Horn of Valhalla, Bronze.entries.0: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to blow this horn. In response, warrior spirits from the plane of Ysgard appear in unoccupied spaces within 60 feet of you. Each spirit uses the {@creature Berserker|XMM} stat block and returns to Ysgard after 1 hour or when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. The spirits look like living, breathing warriors, and they have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions. Once you use the horn, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed.
Horn of Valhalla, Bronze.entries.1: A bronze horn summons 4 {@creature Berserker|XMM|Berserkers}. To use the bronze horn, you must have training with all Medium armor.
Horn of Valhalla, Bronze.entries.2: If you blow the horn without meeting its requirement, the summoned {@creature Berserker|XMM|Berserkers} attack you. If you meet the requirement, they are {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} to you and your allies and follow your commands.
Horn of Valhalla, Bronze.name: Horn of Valhalla, Bronze
Horn of Valhalla, Iron.entries.0: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to blow this horn. In response, warrior spirits from the plane of Ysgard appear in unoccupied spaces within 60 feet of you. Each spirit uses the {@creature Berserker|XMM} stat block and returns to Ysgard after 1 hour or when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. The spirits look like living, breathing warriors, and they have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions. Once you use the horn, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed.
Horn of Valhalla, Iron.entries.1: A iron horn summons 5 {@creature Berserker|XMM|Berserkers}. To use the iron horn, you must have {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB} with all Martial weapons.
Horn of Valhalla, Iron.entries.2: If you blow the horn without meeting its requirement, the summoned {@creature Berserker|XMM|Berserkers} attack you. If you meet the requirement, they are {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} to you and your allies and follow your commands.
Horn of Valhalla, Iron.name: Horn of Valhalla, Iron
Horn of Valhalla, Silver.entries.0: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to blow this horn. In response, warrior spirits from the plane of Ysgard appear in unoccupied spaces within 60 feet of you. Each spirit uses the {@creature Berserker|XMM} stat block and returns to Ysgard after 1 hour or when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. The spirits look like living, breathing warriors, and they have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions. Once you use the horn, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed.
Horn of Valhalla, Silver.entries.1: A silver horn summons 2 {@creature Berserker|XMM|Berserkers}. They are {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} to you and your allies and follow your commands.
Horn of Valhalla, Silver.name: Horn of Valhalla, Silver
Horn of the Endless Maze.entries.0: You can use an action to blow this horn. In response, warrior spirits from the Abyss appear within 60 feet of you. These spirits look like minotaurs, and use the {@creature berserker} statistics. They return to Abyss after 1 hour or when they drop to 0 hit points. Once you use the horn, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed.
Horn of the Endless Maze.entries.1: The horn summons {@dice 3d4 + 3} {@creature berserker||berserkers}. To use the horn, you must be proficient with all simple weapons.
Horn of the Endless Maze.entries.2: If you blow the horn without meeting its requirement, the summoned {@creature berserker||berserkers} attack you. If you meet the requirement, they are friendly to you and your companions and follow your commands.
Horn of the Endless Maze.name: Horn of the Endless Maze
Horned Ring.entries.0: Allows an attuned wearer to ignore Undermountain's magical restrictions (see "Alterations to Magic").
Horned Ring.name: Horned Ring
Horror Trinket.entries.0: Before you finish making a character, roll once on the Horror Trinkets table for a unique object your character has with them at the start of their adventuring career. These trinkets hint toward various horrific tales and might lead to dreadful revelations or be nothing more than grim keepsakes. At the DM's discretion any of these trinkets might also be a Mist talisman (detailed in {@book chapter 3|VRGR|3|Mist Talismans}), a focal item that can lead the bearer through the Mists to a particular Domain of Dread.
Horror Trinket.entries.1.caption: Horror Trinkets
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.0.1: A picture you drew as a child of your imaginary friend
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.1.1: A lock that opens when blood is dripped in its keyhole
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.10.1: A wineskin that refills when interred with a dead person for a night
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.11.1: A set of silverware used by a king for his last meal
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.12.1: A spyglass that always shows the world suffering a terrible storm
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.13.1: A cameo with the profile's face scratched away
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.14.1: A lantern with a black candle that never runs out and that burns with green flame
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.15.1: A teacup from a child's tea set, stained with blood
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.16.1: A little black book that records your dreams, and yours alone, when you sleep
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.17.1: A necklace formed of the interlinked holy symbols of a dozen deities
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.18.1: A noose that feels heavier than it should
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.19.1: A birdcage into which small birds fly but once inside never eat or leave
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.20.1: A lepidopterist's box filled with dead moths with skull-like patterns on their wings
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.21.1: A jar of pickled ghouls' tongues
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.22.1: The wooden hand of a notorious pirate
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.23.1: A urn with the ashes of a dead relative
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.24.1: A hand mirror backed with a bronze depiction of a medusa
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.25.1: Pallid leather gloves crafted with ivory fingernails
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.26.1: Dice made from the knuckles of a notorious charlatan
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.27.1: A ring of keys for forgotten locks
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.28.1: Nails from the coffin of a murderer
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.29.1: A key to the family crypt
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.3.1: A spinning top carved with four faces: happy, sad, wrathful, and dead
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.30.1: A bouquet of funerary flowers that always looks and smells fresh
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.31.1: A switch used to discipline you as a child
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.32.1: A music box that plays by itself whenever someone holding it dances
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.33.1: A walking cane with an iron ferrule that strikes sparks on stone
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.34.1: A flag from a ship lost at sea
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.35.1: A porcelain doll's head that always seems to be looking at you
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.36.1: A wolf's head wrought in silver that is also a whistle
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.37.1: A small mirror that shows a much older version of the viewer
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.38.1: A small, worn book of children's nursery rhymes
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.4.1: The necklace of a sibling who died on the day you were born
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.40.1: A broken pendant of a silver dragon that's always cold to the touch
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.41.1: A small locked box that quietly hums a lovely melody at night, but you always forget it in the morning
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.42.1: An inkwell that makes one a little nauseous when staring at it
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.43.1: An old doll made from a dark, dense wood and missing a hand and a foot
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.45.1: A pouch made of flesh, with a sinew drawstring
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.46.1: A tiny spool of black thread that never runs out
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.47.1: A tiny clockwork figurine of a dancer that's missing a gear and doesn't work
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.48.1: A black wooden pipe that creates puffs of smoke that look like skulls
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.49.1: A vial of perfume, the scent of which only certain creatures can detect
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.5.1: A wig from someone executed by beheading
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.50.1: A stone that emits a single endless sigh
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.51.1: A rag doll with two red dots on its neck
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.52.1: A spring-loaded toy with a missing crank
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.53.1: A mason jar containing a harmless but agitated, animate ooze
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.54.1: A black wooden die with 1's on all the faces
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.55.1: A child's portrait with "born" written on the back, along with next year's date
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.57.1: A finger that's taken root in a small pot
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.58.1: A toolbox containing the remains of a dangerous but broken clockwork arachnid
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.59.1: A pitcher-sized, opalescent snail shell that occasionally, inexplicably shudders or tips over
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.6.1: The unopened letter to you from your dying father
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.60.1: The logbook of an ice-breaking ship called The Haifisch
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.61.1: A small portrait of you as a child, alongside your identically dressed twin
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.62.1: A silver pocket watch with thirteen hours marked on the face
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.63.1: A woodcut of a wolf devouring its own hind leg
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.64.1: A planchette etched with raven skulls
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.65.1: A moist coral figurine of a lamprey with arms, legs, and a bipedal stance
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.66.1: A bronze fingertrap sculpted with roaring tigers
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.67.1: A pearl necklace that turns red under the full moon
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.68.1: A fossil of a fish with humanoid features
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.7.1: A pocket watch that runs backward for an hour every midnight
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.70.1: A paper talisman with smudged ink
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.71.1: A locket containing the smeared image of an eyeless figure
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.72.1: A canopic jar with a lid sculpted like a goat
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.73.1: A jack-o'-lantern made from a small, pale gourd
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.75.1: A candle made from a severed hand
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.76.1: A clockwork device that beats like a heart
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.78.1: A glass eye with a live worm inside
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.79.1: A sheet with two eyeholes cut in it
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.8.1: A winter coat stolen from a dying soldier
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.80.1: The deed to someplace called Tergeron Manor
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.81.1: An ornate, wax-sealed crimson envelope that resists all attempts to open it
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.82.1: A mourning veil trimmed in black lace
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.83.1: A straitjacket covered in charcoal runes
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.84.1: A tattered, burlap mask with a crooked smile painted on it
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.85.1: A green ribbon designed to be worn as a choker
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.88.1: A copper ring with "mine" engraved on the inside
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.89.1: A glass ampoule containing a neon green fluid
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.9.1: A bottle of invisible ink that can only be read at sunset
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.90.1: An eye patch embroidered with a holy symbol
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.91.1: A severed big toe with a nail that continues to grow
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.92.1: A journal that has been heavily redacted
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.93.1: A glove with a mouth-like design stitched on the palm
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.94.1: An ornate but empty reliquary made of silver and fractured glass
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.95.1: A ceramic figure of a cat with too many eyes
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.96.1: A crumpled paper ticket bearing the words "admit none"
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.97.1: An electrum coin with your face on one side
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.98.1: A shrunken gremishka head that twitches when anyone casts magic nearby
Horror Trinket.entries.1.rows.99.1: A sunburst amulet with a red stone at the center
Horror Trinket.name: Horror Trinket
Horseshoes of Speed.entries.0: These horseshoes come in a set of four. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch one of the horseshoes to the hoof of a horse or similar creature, whereupon the horseshoe affixes itself to the hoof. Removing a horseshoe also takes a {@action Magic|XPHB} action.
Horseshoes of Speed.entries.1: While all four horseshoes are attached to the same creature, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is increased by 30 feet.
Horseshoes of Speed.name: Horseshoes of Speed
Horseshoes of a Zephyr.entries.0: These horseshoes come in a set of four. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch one of the horseshoes to the hoof of a horse or similar creature, whereupon the horseshoe affixes itself to the hoof. Removing a horseshoe also takes a {@action Magic|XPHB} action.
Horseshoes of a Zephyr.entries.1: While all four shoes are affixed to the hooves of a horse or similar creature, they allow the creature to move normally while floating 4 inches above a surface. This effect means the creature can cross or stand above nonsolid or unstable surfaces, such as water or lava. The creature leaves no tracks and ignores {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. In addition, the creature can travel for up to 12 hours a day without gaining {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels from extended travel.
Horseshoes of a Zephyr.name: Horseshoes of a Zephyr
Hourglass.name: Hourglass
House of Cards.entries.0: This deck of cards is decorated with geometric shapes that have a protective motif. While you're holding the deck, you can use an action to shuffle it and cause the cards to deal themselves out and transform into a shelter made of cards. The shelter can be shaped however you desire, but it must fit in a 40-foot cube centered on a point within 30 feet of you. The shelter has one door and up to four windows, and only you can open or close them. It has a floor and a roof, and it maintains a comfortable temperature inside.
House of Cards.entries.1: The shelter has AC 15, 50 hit points, and immunity to poison and psychic damage. The shelter lasts for 24 hours, until you dismiss it as an action, or until it is reduced to 0 hit points. When the shelter's duration ends, it transforms back into a deck of cards and appears in your hand. Once the deck has transformed into a shelter, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
House of Cards.name: House of Cards
Hunters Coat.entries.0: You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Hunters Coat.entries.1: The coat has 3 charges. When you hit a creature with an attack and that creature doesn't have all its hit points, you can expend 1 charge to deal an extra {@damage 1d10} necrotic damage to the target. The coat regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.
Hunters Coat.entries.2: The breastplate and shoulder protectors of this armor are made of leather that has been stiffened by being boiled in oil. The rest of the armor is made of softer and more flexible materials.
Hunters Coat.name: Hunter's Coat
Hunting Trap.entries.0: As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can set a Hunting Trap, which is a sawtooth steel ring that snaps shut when a creature steps on a pressure plate in the center. The trap is affixed by a heavy chain to an immobile object, such as a tree or a spike driven into the ground. A creature that steps on the plate must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d4} Piercing damage and have its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn. Thereafter, until the creature breaks free of the trap, its movement is limited by the length of the chain (typically 3 feet). A creature can use its action to make a {@dc 13} Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. Each failed check deals 1 Piercing damage to the trapped creature.
Hunting Trap.name: Hunting Trap
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.caption: Icewind Dale Trinkets
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.0.1: A small wooden figurine of a yawning walrus, painted in red and black
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.1.1: A pair of scrimshaw cufflinks with an image of a fisherman on a boat engraved on them
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.10.1: An expedition log with missing pages and a pressed flower used as a bookmark
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.11.1: An owl figurine carved from whalebone
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.12.1: A sewing box that smells of old wood and has three spools of blue thread inside
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.13.1: A scrimshaw-handled ink pen with black runic designs along its length
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.14.1: A brooch made from a small insect encased in amber
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.15.1: A scrimshaw pepper shaker etched with the letter W
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.16.1: An old, wooden-handled ice pick stained with blood that won't wash off
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.17.1: A fabric doll bearing an angry expression
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.18.1: A set of wind chimes made from seashells
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.19.1: A beautiful silver tin that, when opened, emits the smell of rotting fish
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.2.1: A small iron key with a frayed blue and gold cord tied to it
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.20.1: A bloodstained dreamcatcher made from fishing line, gold wire, and snowy owlbear feathers
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.21.1: A figurine of a polar bear made of ice that never melts
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.22.1: A snow globe that doesn't need to be shaken
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.23.1: A piece of sea glass shaped like a unicorn's horn
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.24.1: A dark blue scarf that gets lighter in shade the higher the altitude of the wearer
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.3.1: A small illustrated book of Northlander myths that has pages missing
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.4.1: A damaged scrimshaw cameo depicting a merfolk
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.5.1: A stone from a burial cairn with a tiny Dwarvish rune carved into it
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.6.1: A ripped cloth sail with a symbol you don't recognize
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.7.1: An Ulu knife with a scrimshaw handle
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.8.1: A jar containing an unidentifiable, sweet, sticky substance
Icewind Dale Trinket.entries.0.rows.9.1: A delicate glass ball painted with snowflakes, capped by a metal loop with a tiny hook attached to it
Icewind Dale Trinket.name: Icewind Dale Trinket
Icon of Ravenloft.entries.0: The Icon of Ravenloft is a 12-inch tall statuette made of the purest silver, weighing 10 pounds. It depicts a cleric kneeling in supplication.
Icon of Ravenloft.entries.1: The icon was given to Strahd by the archpriest Ciril Romulich, an old family friend, to consecrate the castle and its chapel.
Icon of Ravenloft.entries.2: While within 30 feet of the icon, a creature is under the effect of a {@spell protection from evil and good} spell against fiends and undead. Only a creature attuned to the icon can use its other properties.
Icon of Ravenloft.entries.3.entries.0: You can use an action to cast an {@spell augury} spell from the icon, with no material components required. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.
Icon of Ravenloft.entries.3.name: Augury
Icon of Ravenloft.entries.4.entries.0: You can use the icon as a holy symbol while using the Turn Undead or Turn the Unholy feature. If you do so, increase the save DC by 2.
Icon of Ravenloft.entries.4.name: Bane of the Undead
Icon of Ravenloft.entries.5.entries.0: While holding the icon, you can take an action to heal one creature that you can see within 30 feet of you. The target regains {@dice 3d8 + 3} hit points, unless it is an undead, a construct, or a fiend. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.
Icon of Ravenloft.entries.5.name: Cure Wounds
Icon of Ravenloft.name: Icon of Ravenloft
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.0: {@creature Iggwilv the Witch Queen|WBtW|Iggwilv} crafted this wondrous cauldron with the help of her adoptive mother, the archfey Baba Yaga. The cauldron has two forms. Only Iggwilv or Baba Yaga can change the cauldron from one form to another (by using an action to touch it), which either can do without being attuned to the item. In its first form, the cauldron is made of solid gold and embossed on the outside with images of bare-branched trees, falling leaves, and broomsticks. In its second form, the cauldron is made of iron and embossed on the outside with images of bats, toads, cats, lizards, and snakes—eight of each animal. In either form, the cauldron is roughly 3 feet in diameter and has a 2-foot-wide mouth, a round lid with a molded handle at the top, and eight clawed feet for stability. The cauldron weighs 80 pounds when empty, and it can hold up to 100 gallons of liquid.
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.1.entries.0: Any Humanoid creature that attunes to the cauldron must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be aged to the point of decrepitude. In this state, the creature's speed is halved, the range of its vision and hearing is reduced to 30 feet, and it has disadvantage on all ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws. The creature will reach the end of its natural life span in {@dice 3d8} days. Only a {@spell wish} spell or divine intervention can reverse this aging effect on the creature.
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.1.entries.1: Three hags can attune to the cauldron simultaneously, provided they have formed a coven. If the coven disbands, the attunement ends for all three hags.
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.1.name: Настройка
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.2.entries.0: Regardless of the form it takes, the cauldron has the following random properties:
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.2.entries.1.it: 1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Beneficial Properties|dmg|minor beneficial} property (determined by rolling on the {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Beneficial Properties|dmg|Minor Beneficial} Properties table in the Dungeon Master's Guide)

1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|dmg|minor detrimental} property (determined by rolling on the {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|dmg|Minor Detrimental} Properties table in the Dungeon Master's Guide)
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.2.name: Random Properties
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.3.entries.0: The gold cauldron has the following properties:
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.3.entries.1.it: If water is poured into the cauldron and stirred for 1 minute, it transforms into a hearty, hot stew that provides one nourishing meal for up to four people per gallon of water used. The stew remains hot while in the cauldron, then cools naturally after it is removed. The outside of the cauldron remains safe to touch despite the heat of the stew.

If wine is poured into the cauldron and stirred for 10 minutes, it transforms into a magical elixir that fortifies up to four people per gallon of wine used. Any creature that imbibes the elixir gains 10 temporary hit points. Any of the elixir that isn't consumed within 1 hour vanishes. Once used, this property of the cauldron can't be used again until the next dawn.

When the cauldron is filled with 90 gallons of water mixed with 10 gallons of wine, it can be used as the focus for a {@spell scrying} spell. When this spell is cast using the cauldron as its focus, the target of the spell fails its saving throw automatically, and the spell works even if its caster and the target are on different planes of existence. When the spell ends, all the liquid in the cauldron vanishes.

Dipping the bristles of an ordinary broom into the water-filled cauldron transforms the broom into a broom of flying for 3 days. Once used, this property of the cauldron can't be used again until the next dawn.
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.3.name: Gold Cauldron
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.4.entries.0: The iron cauldron has the following properties:
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.4.entries.1.it: You can use an action to scream into the empty cauldron, which magically summons a {@creature swarm of bats} that flies out of the cauldron and acts immediately after you in the initiative order. The swarm obeys your commands and disappears after 1 minute if it hasn't been destroyed by then. Once used, this property of the cauldron can't be used again until the next dawn.

If at least 1 gallon of blood is poured into the cauldron and stirred for 1 minute, it turns into a cloud of harmless black smoke that erupts from the cauldron and dissipates quickly. At the same time, all Humanoid creatures within a certain radius of the cauldron fall {@condition unconscious} for 1 hour, except those that are attuned to the cauldron or immune to being {@condition charmed}. The radius of the effect is 100 feet per gallon of blood used. An {@condition unconscious} creature awakens if it takes damage or if someone uses an action to shake or slap it. Once used, this property of the cauldron can't be used again until the next dawn.

Tying a dead frog or toad to the end of an ordinary branch and dipping it in the water-filled cauldron transforms the branch into a {@item wand of polymorph} with 3 charges. This wand can't recharge and crumbles to ashes when its final charge is expended. Once used, this property of the cauldron can't be used again for 8 days.

If you spend 1 minute touching the cauldron with a unicorn's horn while reciting the poem called "The Witch Queen's Cauldron" (see the accompanying sidebar), all creatures within 1,000 feet of the cauldron except those that are attuned to it become frozen in time. A time-frozen creature is {@condition incapacitated}, can't move or speak, doesn't age, and is unaware of its surroundings or the passage of time. Moreover, it can't be moved, harmed in any way, or affected by any other magic. All other conditions and effects on the creature are suppressed until it is no longer frozen in time. Destroying the cauldron, sending it to another plane of existence, or touching it with a unicorn's horn for 1 minute while reciting "The Witch Queen's Cauldron" ends the time-freezing effect on all creatures. The effect also ends on any creature that comes into contact with an antimagic field or is touched by a unicorn's horn.
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.4.name: Iron Cauldron
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.5.entries.0: The cauldron is a Medium object with AC 19, 80 hit points, and immunity to damage from any source other than a flame tongue or frost brand weapon. Reducing the cauldron to 0 hit points with such a weapon shatters the cauldron into eight pieces of roughly equal size, whereupon all ongoing effects created by the cauldron end as it loses its magic.
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.5.entries.1: The destruction of Iggwilv's Cauldron causes all hags in the multiverse to lose the Shared Spellcasting trait gained by being in a coven (see the "Hag Covens" sidebar in the Monster Manual).
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.5.name: Destroying the Cauldron
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.6.entries.0: If all eight pieces of the shattered cauldron are within 5 feet of one another, a {@spell wish} spell can reassemble them, restoring the cauldron and all its properties. The cauldron's reconstruction also restores the Shared Spellcasting trait of hag covens throughout the multiverse.
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.6.name: Reconstructing the Cauldron
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.7.entries.0: As explained in the description of Iggwilv's Cauldron, the poem titled "The Witch Queen's Cauldron" is an incantation that hints at the time-freezing property of the artifact:
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.7.entries.1.en: Eight cats perch atop eight dead attending
Eight lizards flee from eight rats scavenging
Eight toads climbing meet eight dead and falling
Eight snakes sneak under eight bats screaming
All on the cauldron that is ever seeming
Iggwilvs Cauldron.entries.7.name: The Witch Queen's Cauldron
Iggwilvs Cauldron.name: Iggwilv's Cauldron
Iggwilvs Horn.entries.0: You can use an action to blow this horn to cast one of the following spells from it: {@spell Arms of Hadar}, {@spell Fog Cloud}, {@spell Gust of Wind}, or {@spell Web}. If the spell requires a saving throw, the spell save DC is 13.
Iggwilvs Horn.entries.1: Once the horn has been used to cast a spell, it can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn.
Iggwilvs Horn.name: Iggwilv's Horn
Illuminators Tattoo.entries.0: Produced by a special needle, this magic tattoo features beautiful calligraphy, images of writing implements, and the like.
Illuminators Tattoo.entries.1.entries.0: To attune to this item, you hold the needle to your skin where you want the tattoo to appear, pressing the needle there throughout the attunement process. When the attunement is complete, the needle turns into the ink that becomes the tattoo, which appears on the skin.
Illuminators Tattoo.entries.1.entries.1: If your attunement to the tattoo ends, the tattoo vanishes, and the needle reappears in your space.
Illuminators Tattoo.entries.1.name: Tattoo Attunement
Illuminators Tattoo.entries.2.entries.0: While this tattoo is on your skin, you can write with your fingertip as if it were an ink pen that never runs out of ink.
Illuminators Tattoo.entries.2.entries.1: As an action, you can touch a piece of writing up to one page in length and speak a creature's name. The writing becomes {@condition invisible} to everyone other than you and the named creature for the next 24 hours. Either of you can dismiss the invisibility by touching the script (no action required). Once used, this action can't be used again until the next dawn.
Illuminators Tattoo.entries.2.name: Magical Scribing
Illuminators Tattoo.name: Illuminator's Tattoo
Illusionists Bracers.entries.0: A powerful illusionist of House Dimir originally developed these bracers, which enabled her to create multiple minor illusions at once. The bracers' power, though, extends far beyond illusions.
Illusionists Bracers.entries.1: While wearing the bracers, whenever you cast a cantrip, you can use a bonus action on the same turn to cast that cantrip a second time.
Illusionists Bracers.name: Illusionist's Bracers
Immovable Rod.entries.0: This iron rod has a button on one end. You can take a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to press the button, which causes the rod to become magically fixed in place. Until you or another creature takes a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to push the button again, the rod doesn't move, even if it defies gravity. The rod can hold up to 8,000 pounds of weight. More weight causes the rod to deactivate and fall. A creature can take a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to make a {@dc 30} Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check, moving the fixed rod up to 10 feet on a successful check.
Immovable Rod.name: Immovable Rod
Infernal Amulet.entries.0: While wearing this amulet, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your spells, and it grants a +2 bonus to your spell save DC and spell attack bonus.
Infernal Amulet.entries.1.entries.0: You're unwilling to part with this amulet while attuned to it and you wear it always. While wearing the amulet you have disadvantage on Strength saving throws and Strength checks. Only the {@spell Remove Curse} spell allows you to remove the item and end attunement.
Infernal Amulet.entries.1.name: Проклятие
Infernal Amulet.entries.2.entries.0: This item corrupts. See the "{@adventure Infernal Item Corruption|CoA|16|Infernal Item Corruption}" section.
Infernal Amulet.entries.2.name: Corrupting
