Lock of Trickery.entries.0: This lock appears to be an ordinary {@item Lock|XPHB} (of the type described in chapter 6 of the {@book Player's Handbook|XPHB}) and comes with a single key. The tumblers in this lock magically adjust to thwart burglars. Dexterity checks made to pick the lock have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.
Lock of Trickery.name: Lock of Trickery
Lock.entries.0: A Lock comes with a key. Without the key, a creature can use {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB} to pick this Lock with a successful {@dc 15} Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB}) check.
Lock.name: Lock
Locking Spellbook.entries.0: This 100-page leather-bound tome can be used as a Spellbook. It is closed with a lock that comes with a key. As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, a creature can try to pick the lock using {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB}, doing so with a successful DC 15 Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB}) check.
Locking Spellbook.name: Locking Spellbook
Lolths Sting.entries.0: A creature subjected to Lolth's Sting must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 hour. If the creature fails the save by 5 or more, the creature also has the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition while {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} in this way. The creature wakes up if it takes damage or if another creature takes an action to shake it awake.
Lolths Sting.name: Lolth's Sting
Longbow of the Healing Hearth.entries.0: This ivory longbow is inscribed with a prayer to the god Hiatea, the runes of which are entwined with gilded engravings of wheat stalks and deer antlers.
Longbow of the Healing Hearth.entries.1: You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. If you load no ammunition in the weapon, it produces its own, automatically creating one magic arrow when you pull back the string. The arrow created by the bow vanishes the instant after it hits or misses a target.
Longbow of the Healing Hearth.entries.2: The bow has 8 charges for the following properties, which you can use while wielding the bow. The bow regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} charges daily at dawn.
When you take the {@action Attack} action using the bow, you can expend 1 charge to replace one of your attacks with a blazing arrow of curative magic, which automatically hits one creature you can see within 150 feet of you. The target can then immediately spend and roll one of its unspent Hit Dice and regain a number of hit points equal to the roll plus your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1).
If the target has no unspent Hit Dice remaining, nothing happens. You can use a curative arrow only once per turn.
While holding the bow, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells from it (save {@dc 18}): {@spell create food and water} (1 charge), {@spell warding bond} (2 charges), {@spell guardian of faith} (3 charges).
Longbow of the Healing Hearth.name: Longbow of the Healing Hearth
Longship.name: Longship
Lords Alliance Trinket.entries.0.caption: Lords' Alliance Trinkets
A scrap from a defeated army's banner
A bit and bridle engraved with the name of a famous steed
The broken blade of a sword used in a famous battle
The left glove of a lord who went mysteriously missing
An articulated toy horse and knight
A spaulder emblazoned with the insignia of an unidentified lord
Lords Alliance Trinket.name: Lords' Alliance Trinket
Lords Ensemble.entries.0: The Masked Lords of Waterdeep don this ensemble when meeting with one another. This raiment renders each lord indistinguishable from the others. The ensemble consists of three pieces—a helm, an amulet, and a robe—that function as a single magic item when worn together, but only within the city of Waterdeep and its sewers. You become attuned to the ensemble as a single item.
Lords Ensemble.entries.1.entries.0: This bucket helm covers your head and conceals your face. Screens over the eyes help to shroud your identity without blinding you. While you wear the helm, your voice is magically altered to sound genderless, and you are immune to magic that allows other creatures to read your thoughts, to determine whether you are lying, to know your alignment, or to know your creature type. Creatures can communicate telepathically with you only if you allow it.
Lords Ensemble.entries.1.name: Lord's Helm
Lords Ensemble.entries.2.entries.0: This amulet bears the crest of Waterdeep. It functions as an {@item amulet of proof against detection and location}.
Lords Ensemble.entries.2.name: Lord's Amulet
Lords Ensemble.entries.3.entries.0: This elegant robe functions as a {@item ring of free action}, and it creates the illusion that you have a nondescript, androgynous humanoid build and stand 6 feet tall.
Lords Ensemble.entries.3.name: Lord's Robe
Lords Ensemble.name: Lord's Ensemble
Lorehold Primer.entries.0: The Lorehold Primer is a magic textbook created at Strixhaven's Lorehold College. The primer has 3 charges, and it regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. If you make an Intelligence ({@skill History}) or Intelligence ({@skill Religion}) check while holding the primer, you can expend 1 charge to give yourself {@dice 1d4} bonus to the check, immediately after you roll the {@dice d20}.
Lorehold Primer.entries.1: In addition, if you study the primer at the end of a long rest, you can choose one 1st-level spell from the cleric or wizard spell list. Before you finish your next long rest, you can cast the chosen spell once without a spell slot if you are holding the primer. Your spellcasting ability for this spell is your choice of Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma.
Lorehold Primer.name: Lorehold Primer
Lorehold Trinket.entries.0: When you make your character, you may roll once on the Lorehold Trinkets table, instead of on the {@item trinket|phb|Trinkets table} in the {@book Player's Handbook|PHB}, for your starting trinket.
Lorehold Trinket.entries.1.caption: Lorehold Trinkets
Lorehold Trinket.entries.1.rows.0.1: A map made of concentric circles that can be rotated around the page
Lorehold Trinket.entries.1.rows.1.1: A puzzle box bedecked with amber
Lorehold Trinket.entries.1.rows.2.1: A dented brass compass with a red needle
Lorehold Trinket.entries.1.rows.3.1: The head of a broken statue that houses the consciousness of a snarky sage
Lorehold Trinket.entries.1.rows.4.1: A sheet of parchment embossed with tactile lettering, glowing red
Lorehold Trinket.entries.1.rows.5.1: A broken dagger with a wavy blade and a serpentine hilt
Lorehold Trinket.name: Lorehold Trinket
Lost Crown of Besilmer.entries.0: This dwarven battle-helm consists of a sturdy open-faced steel helmet, decorated with a golden circlet above the brow from which seven small gold spikes project upward. You gain the following benefits while wearing the crown:
Lost Crown of Besilmer.entries.1.items.0: You have resistance to psychic damage.
Lost Crown of Besilmer.entries.1.items.1: You have advantage on saving throws against effects that would charm you.
Lost Crown of Besilmer.entries.1.items.2: You can use a bonus action to inspire one creature you can see that is within 60 feet of you and that can see or hear you. Once before the end of your next turn, the inspired creature can roll a {@dice d6} and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw it makes. This uses 1 charge from the crown. It has 3 charges, and it regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.
Lost Crown of Besilmer.name: Lost Crown of Besilmer
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.0: Not all lingering spirits are tragic souls, lost on their way to the hereafter. Some languish as prisoners, souls so wicked mortals dare not free them upon an unsuspecting afterlife.
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.1: Created by a figure of Vistani legend, Luba's Tarokka of Souls shaped the destiny of countless heroes. The prophecies of this deck of cards also revealed great evils and guided its creator into the path of nefarious forces. Untold times the deck's creator, Mother Luba, narrowly escaped doom, spared only by her keen insights. But even for her, not all wickedness could be escaped. In the most dire cases, Mother Luba managed to ensnare beings of pure evil amid the strands of fate, imprisoning them within her {@deck tarokka deck|CoS}. There these foul spirits dwell still, trapped within a nether-realm hidden amid shuffling cards, waiting for fate to turn foul—as it inevitably will.
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.2: Like all {@deck Tarokka Deck|CoS|tarokka decks}, the {@i Tarokka of Souls} is a lavishly illustrated collection of fifty-four cards, comprising the fourteen cards of the high deck and forty other cards divided into four suits: coins, glyphs, stars, and swords.
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.3.entries: The artifact has the following random properties, which you can determine by rolling on the tables in the "Artifacts" section of the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|DMG}:
2 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|dmg|minor detrimental} properties
2 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Beneficial Properties|dmg|minor beneficial} properties
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.3.name: Random Properties
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.4.entries: While holding the deck, you can use an action to cast one of the following spells (save {@dc 18}) from it: {@spell comprehend languages}, {@spell detect evil and good}, {@spell detect magic}, {@spell detect poison and disease}, {@spell locate object}, or {@spell scrying}. Once you use the deck to cast a spell, you can't cast that spell again from it until the next dawn.
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.4.name: Заклинания
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.5.entries: While holding the deck, you automatically succeed on Constitution saving throws made to maintain your {@status concentration} on divination spells.
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.5.name: Enduring Vision
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.6.entries: As an action, you can draw a card from the deck and twist the fortune of another creature you can see within 15 feet of you. Choose one of the following effects:
The creature has advantage on attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws for the next hour.
The creature has disadvantage on attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws for the next hour.
The deck can be used in this way twice, and you regain all expended uses at the next dawn.
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.6.name: Twist of Fate
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.7.entries: Whenever you use the Twist of Fate property, there is a chance that one of the souls trapped in the deck escapes. Roll {@dice d100} and consult the Souls of the Tarokka table. If you roll one of the high cards, the soul associated with it escapes. You can find its statistics in the {@book Monster Manual|MM}. If you roll a soul that has already escaped, roll again.

The released soul appears at a random location within {@dice 10d10} miles of you and terrorizes the living. Until the released soul is destroyed, it gains the benefit of a weal from the deck's Twist of Fate property, and both you and the original target of Twist of Fate suffer the effect of woe.
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.7.name: Prisoners of Fate
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.8.entries: If you go 7 days without using the Twist of Fate property, your attunement to Luba's Tarokka of Souls ends, and you can't attune to it again until after another creature uses Twist of Fate on you.
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.8.name: Shuffling Fate
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.9.entries: Luba's Tarokka of Souls can be destroyed only if all fourteen souls within are released and destroyed. This reveals a fifteenth soul, a {@creature lich}, that inhabits the Nether card, which appears only when the fourteen souls are defeated. If this ancient entity is destroyed, the Nether card vanishes and the deck becomes a normal {@deck tarokka deck|CoS}, with no special properties, but it includes a new card of the DM's design.

The creator of the Tarokka of Souls, Mother Luba was one of the most influential leaders of the Vistani. For untold generations, the Vistani have wandered the Shadowfell, which includes terrifying demiplanes like the vampire-haunted realm of Barovia. These travelers have learned many secrets of these domains and encountered countless others wandering amid the Shadowfell's horrors. Most Vistani bands accept well-intentioned wayfarers from diverse walks and of disparate origins, embracing any who seek to find a home amid the endless roads and vistas hidden amid the mists.

A halfling Vistani, Mother Luba led one of the largest groups of Vistani in the Shadowfell. She hailed from the same world that Count Strahd von Zarovich and Madam Eva came from, and she created a community of kindness and resilience-ever rare to find in the Plane of Shadow. She led her people in welcoming strangers, feeding the hungry, and defying the cruel. She and Madam Eva were once friends, until Madam Eva began bargaining with the creatures of the night. "We may wander amid the shadows," Mother Luba said. "But we must ever serve as a light to our fellow travelers."
Some years ago, Mother Luba disappeared into the mists, leaving behind only the Tarokka of Souls. It is said that if you draw the Mists card from it, you can hear the whispers of her kind voice.
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.entries.9.name: Destroying the Deck
Lubas Tarokka of Souls.name: Luba's Tarokka of Souls
Lucent Destroyer.entries.0: This magic weapon is a triple-barreled bronze musket. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. It requires no ammunition, its damage is radiant instead of piercing, and it doesn't have the loading property. The base of the weapon is emblazoned with the light rune.
Lucent Destroyer.entries.1: Additionally, while attuned to the weapon, you can cast {@spell dancing lights} from the musket at will.
Lucent Destroyer.entries.2.entries.0: As an action, you can invoke the weapon's rune to cast the {@spell sunbeam} spell (save {@dc 17}) with it. Once the rune has been invoked, it can't be invoked again until the next dawn.
Lucent Destroyer.entries.2.name: Invoking the Rune
Lucent Destroyer.name: Lucent Destroyer
Luminous War Pick.entries.0: The haft of this war pick is inlaid with crushed pearlescent stones that imbue the weapon with a faint luminescence. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this war pick.
Luminous War Pick.entries.1: While wielding the war pick, you can use a bonus action to cast the {@spell daylight} spell, choosing a point on the war pick. Once you use this bonus action, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
Luminous War Pick.name: Luminous War Pick
Lute crafted of exotic wood with mother-: Lute crafted of exotic wood with mother-of-pearl inlay and zircon gems (Brazen Coalition)
Lute of Thunderous Thumping.entries.0: This reinforced {@item lute|XPHB} can be wielded as a magic Club that deals an extra {@damage 2d8} Thunder damage on a hit.
Lute of Thunderous Thumping.entries.1.en: If you're a Bard, you can use your Charisma modifier instead of your Strength modifier when making a melee attack roll with the {@item lute|XPHB}, provided you sing or hum while making the attack.
Lute of Thunderous Thumping.name: Lute of Thunderous Thumping
Luxon Beacon.entries.0: This dodecahedron of faintly glowing crystal is heavier than it appears. A set of handles are affixed to its sides, and it pulsates and thrums when touched.
Luxon Beacon.entries.1.entries.0: A creature that touches the beacon and concentrates for 1 minute receives a Fragment of Possibility, which looks like a Tiny, grayish bead of energy that follows the creature around, staying within 1 foot of it at all times. The fragment lasts for 8 hours or until used. Once the beacon grants a Fragment of Possibility, it can't grant another until the next dawn. A creature with a Fragment of Possibility from a Luxon Beacon can't gain another Fragment of Possibility from any source.
Luxon Beacon.entries.1.entries.1: When a creature with a Fragment of Possibility makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw, it can expend its fragment to roll an additional {@dice d20} and choose which of the {@dice d20}s to use. Alternatively, when an attack roll is made against the creature, it can expend its fragment to roll a {@dice d20} and choose which of the {@dice d20}s to use, the one it rolled or the one the attacker rolled.
Luxon Beacon.entries.1.entries.2: If the original {@dice d20} roll has advantage or disadvantage, the creature rolls its {@dice d20} after advantage or disadvantage has been applied to the original roll.
Luxon Beacon.entries.1.name: Fragment of Possibility
Luxon Beacon.entries.2.entries.0: If a follower of the Luxon who has undergone a ritual of consecution dies within 100 miles of a Luxon Beacon, their soul is ensnared by it. This soul will be reincarnated within the body of a random humanoid baby developing within 100 miles of the beacon.
Luxon Beacon.entries.2.name: Soul Snare
Luxon Beacon.name: Luxon Beacon
Lyre of Building.entries.0: While holding this {@item lyre|PHB}, you can cast {@spell mending} as an action. You can also play the {@item lyre|PHB} as a reaction when an object or a structure you can see within 300 feet of you takes damage, causing it to be immune to that damage and any further damage of the same type until the start of your next turn.
Lyre of Building.entries.1: In addition, you can play the {@item lyre|PHB} as an action to cast {@spell fabricate}, {@spell move earth}, {@spell passwall}, or {@spell summon construct|TCE}, and that spell can't be cast from it again until the next dawn.
Lyre of Building.name: Lyre of Building
Mace of Disruption.entries.0: When you hit a Fiend or an Undead with this magic weapon, that creature takes an extra {@damage 2d6} Radiant damage. If the target has 25 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or fewer after taking this damage, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or be destroyed. On a successful save, the creature has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.
Mace of Disruption.entries.1.entries.0: While you hold this weapon, it sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet.
Mace of Disruption.entries.1.name: Лёгкое
Mace of Disruption.name: Mace of Disruption
Mace of Smiting.entries.0: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The bonus increases to +3 when you use the weapon to attack a Construct.
Mace of Smiting.entries.1: When you roll a 20 on an attack roll made with this weapon, the target takes an extra 7 Bludgeoning damage, or 14 Bludgeoning damage if it's a Construct. If a Construct has 25 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or fewer after taking this damage, it is destroyed.
Mace of Smiting.name: Mace of Smiting
Mace of Terror.entries.0: This magic weapon has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the weapon, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and expend 1 charge to release a wave of terror from it. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. While {@condition Frightened|XPHB} in this way, a creature must spend its turns trying to move as far away from you as it can, and it can't make {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. For its action, it can use only the {@action Dash|XPHB} action or try to escape from an effect that prevents it from moving. If it has nowhere it can move, the creature can take the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action. At the end of each of its turns, a creature repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Mace of Terror.name: Mace of Terror
Mace of the Black Crown (Awakened).name: Mace of the Black Crown (Awakened)
Mace of the Black Crown (Dormant).entrie: This mace has a haft of black iron and a ruby head with a fiendish countenance. Carrying the boon of Asmodeus, it is fit for the most powerful servants of the Nine Hells.
The Mace of the Black Crown is a sentient lawful evil weapon with an Intelligence of 20, a Wisdom of 12, and a Charisma of 18. It has hearing and {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 120 feet.
The weapon communicates telepathically with its wielder and can speak, read, and understand Common and Infernal.

A greedy {@creature erinyes} named Xartaza lives within the Mace of the Black Crown. Xartaza wants to recruit more souls for Asmodeus, so the weapon pushes its wielder toward lawful evil actions by manipulating the wielder's dreams. A former Blood War general, Xartaza hates demons and relishes crafting sound battle plans in any conflict.
The mace grants the following benefits in its dormant state:
You can speak, read, and write Infernal.
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

While holding the mace, you can use a bonus action to speak its Infernal command word, causing flames to erupt from the head. These flames shed bright light in a 40-foot radius and dim light for an additional 40 feet. While the mace is ablaze, it deals an extra {@damage 1d6} fire damage to any target it hits. The flames last until you use a bonus action to speak the command word again or until you are no longer holding the mace.

While holding the mace, you can use an action to summon an {@creature imp}. Any devil you summon with this mace is friendly to you and your companions for the duration. The imp obeys any verbal commands that you issue to it and returns to the Nine Hells 10 minutes after you summoned it. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

The Arms of the Betrayers advance in power in the same manner as the Vestiges of Divergence. In its dormant state, each of these artifacts has one {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Beneficial Properties|dmg|minor beneficial} property and one {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|dmg|minor detrimental} property. When the artifact attains an awakened state, it gains an additional {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Beneficial Properties|dmg|minor beneficial} property and an additional {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|dmg|minor detrimental} property. When the item reaches its exalted state, it gains a {@table Artifact Properties; Major Beneficial Properties|dmg|major beneficial} property. See "Artifact Properties" in chapter 7 of the Dungeon Master's Guide for more information.
Mace of the Black Crown (Dormant).name: Mace of the Black Crown (Dormant)
Mace of the Black Crown (Exalted).name: Mace of the Black Crown (Exalted)
Macuahuitl.entries.0: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this longsword, which is made of laminated wood, and inset with jagged teeth of obsidian. It deals an extra {@damage 2d6} damage to any creature of the plant type.
Macuahuitl.name: Macuahuitl
Maddgoths Helm.entries.0: While you wear this helm and are inside Maddgoth's castle, on its roof, or in its courtyard, you have immunity to all damage. If the helm is taken from the castle, it turns to dust and is destroyed.
Maddgoths Helm.name: Maddgoth's Helm
Magen Handbell.entries.0: While holding this brass handbell, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to ring it and summon a {@creature Terran Magen|NF} (see appendix B). The magen appears in an unoccupied space you choose within 30 feet of yourself, understands your languages, obeys your commands, and takes its turn immediately after you on your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count. The magen disappears after 1 hour, when it dies, or when you dismiss it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. The bell can't be used this way again for {@dice 1d6} days.
Magen Handbell.entries.1.entries.0: Whenever the magen summoned by this item starts its turn Bloodied, roll {@dice 1d6}. On a 6, the magen goes berserk.
Magen Handbell.entries.1.entries.1: While berserk, the magen no longer obeys your commands, and you can't dismiss it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. On each of its turns, the berserk magen attacks the nearest creature it can see. If no creature is near enough for the magen to move to and attack, the magen attacks an object. The magen remains berserk until it disappears, or until a creature holding the handbell takes an {@action Influence|XPHB} action and succeeds on a DC 15 Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check to regain control of the magen.
Magen Handbell.entries.1.name: Berserk Magen
Magen Handbell.name: Magen Handbell
Magnifying Glass.entries.0: A Magnifying Glass grants {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any ability check made to appraise or inspect a highly detailed item. Lighting a fire with a Magnifying Glass requires light as bright as sunlight to focus, tinder to ignite, and about 5 minutes for the fire to ignite.
Magnifying Glass.name: Magnifying Glass
Malachite.entries.0: A striated light and dark green gemstone.
Malachite.name: Malachite
Malice.entries.0: A creature subjected to Malice must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 hour. The creature also has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition while {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} in this way.
Malice.name: Malice
Manacles.entries.0: As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can use Manacles to bind an unwilling Small or Medium creature within 5 feet of yourself that has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, or {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition if you succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB}) check. While bound, a creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls, and the creature is {@condition Restrained|XPHB} if the Manacles are attached to a chain or hook that is fixed in place. Escaping the Manacles requires a successful {@dc 20} Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB}) check as an action. Bursting them requires a successful {@dc 25} Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check as an action.
Manacles.entries.1: Each set of Manacles comes with a key. Without the key, a creature can use {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB} to pick the Manacles' lock with a successful {@dc 15} Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB}) check.
Manacles.name: Manacles
Manifold Tool.entries.0: This tool takes the form of a wrench, a screwdriver, or another basic tool. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch the item and transform it into a type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice. Whatever form the tool takes, you have proficiency with it when you use it.
Manifold Tool.name: Manifold Tool
Mantle of Spell Resistance.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells while you wear this cloak.
Mantle of Spell Resistance.name: Mantle of Spell Resistance
Manual of Bodily Health.entries.0: This book contains health and diet tips, and its words are charged with magic. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying the book's contents and practicing its guidelines, your Constitution increases by 2, to a maximum of 30. The manual then loses its magic, but regains it in a century.
Manual of Bodily Health.name: Manual of Bodily Health
Manual of Clay Golems.entries.0: This tome contains information and incantations necessary to make a {@creature clay golem|XMM}. To decipher and use the manual, you must be a spellcaster with at least two 5th-level spell slots. A creature that can't use a {@i manual of golems} and attempts to read it takes {@damage 6d6} psychic damage.
Manual of Clay Golems.entries.1: To create a clay golem, you must spend 30 days, working without interruption with the manual at hand and resting no more than 8 hours per day. You must also pay 65,000 gp to purchase supplies.
Manual of Clay Golems.entries.2: Once you finish creating the golem, the book is consumed in eldritch flames. The golem becomes animate when the ashes of the manual are sprinkled on it. It is under your control, and it understands and obeys your spoken commands.
Manual of Clay Golems.name: Manual of Clay Golems
Manual of Flesh Golems.entries.0: This tome contains information and incantations necessary to make a {@creature flesh golem|XMM}. To decipher and use the manual, you must be a spellcaster with at least two 5th-level spell slots. A creature that can't use a {@i manual of golems} and attempts to read it takes {@damage 6d6} psychic damage.
Manual of Flesh Golems.entries.1: To create a flesh golem, you must spend 60 days, working without interruption with the manual at hand and resting no more than 8 hours per day. You must also pay 50,000 gp to purchase supplies.
Manual of Flesh Golems.entries.2: Once you finish creating the golem, the book is consumed in eldritch flames. The golem becomes animate when the ashes of the manual are sprinkled on it. It is under your control, and it understands and obeys your spoken commands.
Manual of Flesh Golems.name: Manual of Flesh Golems
Manual of Gainful Exercise.entries.0: This book describes fitness exercises, and its words are charged with magic. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying the book's contents and practicing its guidelines, your Strength increases by 2, to a maximum of 30. The manual then loses its magic, but regains it in a century.
Manual of Gainful Exercise.name: Manual of Gainful Exercise
Manual of Iron Golems.entries.0: This tome contains information and incantations necessary to make a {@creature iron golem|XMM}. To decipher and use the manual, you must be a spellcaster with at least two 5th-level spell slots. A creature that can't use a {@i manual of golems} and attempts to read it takes {@damage 6d6} psychic damage.
Manual of Iron Golems.entries.1: To create an iron golem, you must spend 120 days, working without interruption with the manual at hand and resting no more than 8 hours per day. You must also pay 100,000 gp to purchase supplies.
Manual of Iron Golems.entries.2: Once you finish creating the golem, the book is consumed in eldritch flames. The golem becomes animate when the ashes of the manual are sprinkled on it. It is under your control, and it understands and obeys your spoken commands.
Manual of Iron Golems.name: Manual of Iron Golems
Manual of Quickness of Action.entries.0: This book contains coordination and balance exercises, and its words are charged with magic. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying the book's contents and practicing its guidelines, your Dexterity increases by 2, to a maximum of 30. The manual then loses its magic, but regains it in a century.
Manual of Quickness of Action.name: Manual of Quickness of Action
Manual of Stone Golems.entries.0: This tome contains information and incantations necessary to make a {@creature stone golem|XMM}. To decipher and use the manual, you must be a spellcaster with at least two 5th-level spell slots. A creature that can't use a {@i manual of golems} and attempts to read it takes {@damage 6d6} psychic damage.
Manual of Stone Golems.entries.1: To create a stone golem, you must spend 90 days, working without interruption with the manual at hand and resting no more than 8 hours per day. You must also pay 80,000 gp to purchase supplies.
Manual of Stone Golems.entries.2: Once you finish creating the golem, the book is consumed in eldritch flames. The golem becomes animate when the ashes of the manual are sprinkled on it. It is under your control, and it understands and obeys your spoken commands.
Manual of Stone Golems.name: Manual of Stone Golems
Map or Scroll Case.entries.0: A {@item Map or Scroll Case|XPHB} holds up to 10 sheets of paper or 5 sheets of parchment.
Map or Scroll Case.name: Map or Scroll Case
Map.entries.0: If you consult an accurate Map, you gain a +5 bonus to Wisdom ({@skill Survival|XPHB}) checks you make to find your way in the place represented on it.
Map.name: Map
Marble font with gold inlay (Legion of D: Marble font with gold inlay (Legion of Dusk)
Mask of Changed Appearance.entries.0: This jeweled mask has 3 charges. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend 1 charge and change your face's appearance. You can't make yourself look like a different person, but you can smooth or deepen your wrinkles, whiten your teeth, hide or accentuate bags under your eyes, or perform other minor cosmetic changes. While your appearance is changed, the mask has the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition. Your changed appearance lasts 1 hour.
Mask of Changed Appearance.entries.1.ent: The mask regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the mask explodes in a harmless cloud of sweet-swelling powder and is destroyed.
Mask of Changed Appearance.name: Mask of Changed Appearance
Mask of the Beast.entries.0: This wooden mask is shaped in the likeness of a beast's visage and has 3 charges. While wearing the mask you can expend 1 charge and use the mask to cast the {@spell animal friendship} spell as an action. The mask regains all expended charges at dawn.
Mask of the Beast.name: Mask of the Beast
Mask of the Dragon Queen.entries.0: Individually, the five dragon masks resemble the dragons they are named for. When two or more of the dragon masks are assembled, however, they transform magically into the Mask of the Dragon Queen. Each mask shrinks to become the modeled head of a chromatic dragon, appearing to roar its devotion to {@creature Tiamat|RoT} where all the masks brought together are arranged crown-like on the wearer's head. Below the five masks, a new mask shapes itself, granting the wearer a draconic visage that covers the face, neck, and shoulders.
Mask of the Dragon Queen.entries.1: While you are attuned to and wear this mask, you can have any of the properties from any one mask. Additionally, you gain the Damage Absorption from each of the five dragon masks, and you gain five uses of the Legendary Resistance property.
Mask of the Dragon Queen.name: Mask of the Dragon Queen
Masons Tools.entries.0.items.0.name: Характеристика:
Masons Tools.entries.0.items.1.entries.0: Chisel a symbol or hole in stone ({@dc 10})
Masons Tools.entries.0.items.1.name: Использование:
Masons Tools.entries.0.items.2.name: Craft:
Masons Tools.name: Mason's Tools
Masque Charm.entries.0: A masque charm is a small silver pin. While wearing this charm, you can use an action to cast the {@spell disguise self} spell ({@dc 13} to discern the disguise). Once the spell is cast, it can't be cast from the charm again until the next sunset. When casting the spell, you can have the spell last for its normal 1 hour duration or for 6 hours. If you choose the 6-hour duration, the charm becomes nonmagical when the spell ends. In either case, the spell ends if the charm is removed from you.
Masque Charm.name: Masque Charm
Masquerade Tattoo.entries.0: Produced by a special needle, this magic tattoo appears on your body as whatever you desire.
Masquerade Tattoo.entries.1.entries.0: To attune to this item, you hold the needle to your skin where you want the tattoo to appear, pressing the needle there throughout the attunement process. When the attunement is complete, the needle turns into the ink that becomes the tattoo, which appears on the skin.
Masquerade Tattoo.entries.1.entries.1: If your attunement to the tattoo ends, the tattoo vanishes, and the needle reappears in your space.
Masquerade Tattoo.entries.1.name: Tattoo Attunement
Masquerade Tattoo.entries.2.entries.0: As a bonus action, you can shape the tattoo into any color or pattern and move it to any area of your skin. Whatever form it takes, it is always obviously a tattoo. It can range in size from no smaller than a copper piece to an intricate work of art that covers all your skin.
Masquerade Tattoo.entries.2.name: Fluid Ink
Masquerade Tattoo.entries.3.entries.0: As an action, you can use the tattoo to cast the {@spell disguise self} spell ({@dc 13} to discern the disguise). Once the spell is cast from the tattoo, it can't be cast from the tattoo again until the next dawn.
Masquerade Tattoo.entries.3.name: Disguise Self
Masquerade Tattoo.name: Masquerade Tattoo
Masterpiece painting in mahogany frame w: Masterpiece painting in mahogany frame with gold inlay (Legion of Dusk)
Mastiff.name: Mastiff
Mastix, Whip of Erebos.entries.0: Erebos wields Mastix, a whip capable of extending like an impossibly long shadow. In the hands of the god of the dead, the whip snares the reluctant dead and drags them into his realm. When Erebos grants his weapon to a mortal follower, it's typically to reclaim a powerful soul or to humiliate Heliod.
Mastix, Whip of Erebos.entries.1.entries: Erebos's whip seethes with the enervating energy of the Underworld. This magic whip grants a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. When you hit with an attack using this whip, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d8} necrotic damage and you regain hit points equal to half the amount of necrotic damage dealt.
Additionally, when you make an attack with the whip on your turn, you can increase the range of the attack to 300 feet. This property of the weapon can't be used again until the next dusk.
Mastix, Whip of Erebos.entries.1.name: Whip of the Dead
Mastix, Whip of Erebos.entries.2.entries: If you are a worshiper of Erebos, you gain all the following benefits for which you have the required piety:
The whip has 1 randomly determined {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|dmg|minor detrimental} property—a burden Erebos imposes to test his faithful.
The whip has 1 randomly determined {@table Artifact Properties; Major Beneficial Properties|dmg|major beneficial} property.
The whip has 1 additional randomly determined {@table Artifact Properties; Major Beneficial Properties|dmg|major beneficial} property.
If you aren't a worshiper of Erebos, the whip has 2 randomly determined {@table Artifact Properties; Major Detrimental Properties|dmg|major detrimental} properties.
See "Artifacts" in chapter 7 of the Dungeon Master's Guide for details on randomly determined properties.
Mastix, Whip of Erebos.entries.2.name: Blessing of the Dead
Mastix, Whip of Erebos.entries.3.entries: While carrying the whip, you can use an action to cast either {@spell circle of death} or {@spell dominate monster} (targeting only undead) from the whip. The save DC for these spells is 18. Once you use the whip to cast a spell, that spell can't be cast from it again until the next dusk.
Mastix, Whip of Erebos.entries.3.name: Erebos's Claim
Mastix, Whip of Erebos.entries.4.entries: To destroy the whip, it must be taken to the heights of Mount Hiastos in Nyx, unraveled by a Returned, and left to bask in continual daylight for one month.
Mastix, Whip of Erebos.entries.4.name: Destroying the Whip
Mastix, Whip of Erebos.name: Mastix, Whip of Erebos
Matalotok.entries.0: Matalotok, the Frost Father, is an ancient hammer fashioned by Thrym, the god of frost giants. The favored weapon of the demon lord {@creature Kostchtchie|BGDIA}, Matalotok is frigid to the touch and wreathed in mist.
Matalotok.entries.1: You are immune to cold damage while holding Matalotok. Whenever it deals damage to a creature, the hammer radiates a burst of intense cold in a 30-foot-radius sphere. Each creature in that area takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) cold damage.
Matalotok.name: Matalotok
Matchless Pipe.entries.0: A switch made of flint is built into the bowl of this fine wooden smoking pipe. With a few flicks of the switch, the pipe lights itself.
Matchless Pipe.name: Matchless Pipe
Mechanical Wonder (Domestic).entries.0: A Mechanical Wonder is a semi-autonomous object imbued with magical power. Mechanical Wonders vary greatly in size and form depending on their function.
Mechanical Wonder (Domestic).entries.1: A Mechanical Wonder is a dormant object until activated. To activate a Mechanical Wonder, you must wind the wonder with a handheld key unique to that wonder. It takes 1 minute to wind a Mechanical Wonder. While you wind it, you must take the {@action Utilize|XPHB} action each turn.
Mechanical Wonder (Domestic).entries.2: Once wound, a Mechanical Wonder remains activated for the duration specified in the wonder's description.
Mechanical Wonder (Domestic).entries.3: This bipedal wonder is designed to assist with household duties. It vaguely resembles whatever species its creator used as inspiration. An activated domestic wonder uses the {@creature Domestic Wonder|FRAiF} stat block. The wonder is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the wonder shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and uses its movement to avoid danger. The domestic wonder is destroyed if it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
Mechanical Wonder (Domestic).name: Mechanical Wonder (Domestic)
Mechanical Wonder (Flying).entries.0: A Mechanical Wonder is a semi-autonomous object imbued with magical power. Mechanical Wonders vary greatly in size and form depending on their function.
Mechanical Wonder (Flying).entries.1: A Mechanical Wonder is a dormant object until activated. To activate a Mechanical Wonder, you must wind the wonder with a handheld key unique to that wonder. It takes 1 minute to wind a Mechanical Wonder. While you wind it, you must take the {@action Utilize|XPHB} action each turn.
Mechanical Wonder (Flying).entries.2: Once wound, a Mechanical Wonder remains activated for the duration specified in the wonder's description.
Mechanical Wonder (Flying).entries.3: An activated flying wonder uses the {@creature Flying Wonder|FRAiF} stat block. The wonder is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the wonder shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and uses its movement to avoid danger. The flying wonder is destroyed if it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
Mechanical Wonder (Flying).name: Mechanical Wonder (Flying)
Mechanical Wonder (Gyrocopter).entries.0: A Mechanical Wonder is a semi-autonomous object imbued with magical power. Mechanical Wonders vary greatly in size and form depending on their function.
Mechanical Wonder (Gyrocopter).entries.1: A Mechanical Wonder is a dormant object until activated. To activate a Mechanical Wonder, you must wind the wonder with a handheld key unique to that wonder. It takes 1 minute to wind a Mechanical Wonder. While you wind it, you must take the {@action Utilize|XPHB} action each turn.
Mechanical Wonder (Gyrocopter).entries.2: This wagon-sized vehicle is made of brassy metal and has a spiral-shaped propeller atop it. Its midsection is open on two sides. Once wound, the gyrocopter wonder remains active for 8 hours. While active, the gyrocopter can be used as a vehicle; it requires one pilot.
Mechanical Wonder (Gyrocopter).entries.3: The gyrocopter wonder is a Huge object that weighs 500 pounds and has the following statistics: AC 14, HP 50, {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} 5 ft., and {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} 20 ft. The gyrocopter can bear up to 2,000 pounds of cargo; if it bears any more than this, it deactivates.
Mechanical Wonder (Gyrocopter).entries.4: Eight spectral cords are mounted to the inside of the cargo bay. A creature riding in the gyrocopter wonder can take a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to attach itself to or detach itself from one of these cords. A creature attached to a cord can leap from the gyrocopter wonder and glide, moving up to 5 feet horizontally for every 1 foot it descends in the air. If a creature attached to a cord is ever more than 500 feet away from the gyrocopter, the cord immediately disappears and returns to the wonder.
Mechanical Wonder (Gyrocopter).name: Mechanical Wonder (Gyrocopter)
Mechanical Wonder (Mobility).entries.0: A Mechanical Wonder is a semi-autonomous object imbued with magical power. Mechanical Wonders vary greatly in size and form depending on their function.
Mechanical Wonder (Mobility).entries.1: A Mechanical Wonder is a dormant object until activated. To activate a Mechanical Wonder, you must wind the wonder with a handheld key unique to that wonder. It takes 1 minute to wind a Mechanical Wonder. While you wind it, you must take the {@action Utilize|XPHB} action each turn.
Mechanical Wonder (Mobility).entries.2: While inactive, the mobility wonder resembles a metal turtle shell with straps. It is designed to be worn on your back or flank, depending on your anatomy. Once wound, the mobility wonder transforms into a personal transport device and remains active for 24 hours. While active, the mobility wonder increases your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} to 30 feet. Other creatures can't remove your mobility wonder from you unless you allow it.
Mechanical Wonder (Mobility).name: Mechanical Wonder (Mobility)
Medal of Muscle.entries.0: You can squeeze this medal tightly in the palm of your hand as an action. Doing so gives you advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws for 1 hour. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again, and the medal becomes nonmagical.
Medal of Muscle.name: Medal of Muscle
Medal of Wit.entries.0: You can press this medal to your temple as an action. Doing so gives you advantage on Intelligence checks and Intelligence saving throws for 1 hour. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again, and the medal becomes nonmagical.
Medal of Wit.name: Medal of Wit
Medal of the Conch.entries.0: When you use an action to rub this medal, you gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed for 1 hour. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again, and the medal becomes nonmagical.
Medal of the Conch.name: Medal of the Conch
Medal of the Horizonback.entries.0: When you would be hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to increase your AC by 5 until the start of your next turn, including against the triggering attack. You must be wearing the medal and able to see the creature that made the triggering attack to use this property. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again, and the medal becomes nonmagical.
Medal of the Horizonback.name: Medal of the Horizonback
Medal of the Maze.entries.0: When you use an action to trace the maze inscribed on this medal, you gain advantage on Wisdom checks and know the quickest route to the end of any nonmagical path or maze for 1 hour. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again, and the medal becomes nonmagical.
Medal of the Maze.name: Medal of the Maze
Medal of the Meat Pie.entries.0: You gain {@dice 2d4 + 2} temporary hit points when you use an action to press this medal to your mouth. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again, and the medal becomes nonmagical.
Medal of the Meat Pie.entries.1: While magical, this medal is slightly warm to the touch (as if it's fresh from the oven) and smells faintly of baked pie crust.
Medal of the Meat Pie.name: Medal of the Meat Pie
Medal of the Wetlands.entries.0: When you use an action to trace the edge of this medal, {@quickref difficult terrain||3} doesn't cost you extra movement for 1 hour. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again, and the medal becomes nonmagical.
Medal of the Wetlands.name: Medal of the Wetlands
Medallion of Thoughts.entries.0: The medallion has 5 charges. While wearing it, you can expend 1 charge to cast {@spell Detect Thoughts|XPHB} (save {@dc 13}) from it. The medallion regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.
Medallion of Thoughts.name: Medallion of Thoughts
Menga leaves (1 ounce).entries.0: The dried leaves of a menga bush can be ground, dissolved in a liquid, heated, and ingested. A creature that ingests 1 ounce of menga leaves in this fashion regains 1 hit point. A creature that ingests more than 5 ounces of menga leaves in a 24-hour period gains no additional benefit and must succeed on a {@dc 11} Constitution saving throw or fall {@condition unconscious} for 1 hour. The {@condition unconscious} creature awakens if it takes at least 5 damage on one turn.
Menga leaves (1 ounce).entries.1: A healthy menga bush usually has {@dice 2d6} ounces of leaves. Once picked, the leaves require 1 day to dry out before they can confer any benefit.
Menga leaves (1 ounce).name: Menga leaves (1 ounce)
Merchants Scale.entries.0: A scale includes a small balance, pans, and a suitable assortment of weights up to 2 pounds. With it, you can measure the exact weight of small objects, such as raw precious metals or trade goods, to help determine their worth.
Merchants Scale.name: Merchant's Scale
Mess Kit.entries.0: This tin box contains a cup and simple cutlery. The box clamps together, and one side can be used as a cooking pan and the other as a plate or shallow bowl.
Mess Kit.name: Mess Kit
Midnight Tears.entries.0: A creature that ingests Midnight Tears suffers no effect until the stroke of midnight. Any effect that ends the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition neutralizes this poison. If the poison hasn't been neutralized before midnight, the creature makes a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw, taking 31 ({@damage 9d6}) Poison damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
Midnight Tears.name: Midnight Tears
Mighty Servant of Leuk-o.entries.0: Named for the warlord who infamously employed it, the {@creature Mighty Servant of Leuk-o|TCE} is a fantastically powerful, 10-foot-tall machine that turns into an animate construct when piloted. Crafted of a gleaming black alloy of unknown origin, the servant is often described as a combination of a disproportioned dwarf and an oversized beetle. The servant contains enough space for 1 ton of cargo and a crew compartment within, from which up to two Medium creatures can control it-and potentially execute a spree of unstoppable destruction.
Mighty Servant of Leuk-o.entries.1: Tales of the servant's origins involve more conjecture than fact, often referring to otherworldly beings, the mysterious Barrier Peaks in Oerth, and the supposedly related device known as the {@i Machine of Lum the Mad}. The best details on the device's origins and operation can be found in the {@i Mind of Metal}, a tome of artificer's secrets that connects the device to the traditions of the lost Olman people, and which was written by Lum the Mad's several times over granddaughter, Lum the Maestro, while she reconstructed the long disassembled Mighty Servant of Leuk-o.
Mighty Servant of Leuk-o.entries.2.entri: Two creatures can be attuned to the servant at a time. If a third creature tries to attune to it, nothing happens.
The servant's controls are accessed by a hatch in its upper back, which is easily opened while there are no creatures attuned to the artifact.

Attuning to the artifact requires two hours, which can be undertaken as part of a long rest, during which time you must be inside the servant, interacting with its controls. While crew members are attuning themselves, any creature or structure outside and within 50 feet of the servant has a {@chance 25} chance of being accidentally targeted by one of its Destructive Fist attacks once during the attunement. This process must be undergone every time a creature attunes itself to the artifact.
Mighty Servant of Leuk-o.entries.2.name: Dangerous Attunement
Mighty Servant of Leuk-o.entries.3.entri: While any creatures are attuned to the artifact, attuned creatures can open the hatch as easily as any other door. Other creatures can open the hatch as an action with a successful {@dc 25} Dexterity check using {@item thieves' tools|PHB}. A {@spell knock} spell cast on the hatch also opens it until the start of the caster's next turn.

A creature can enter or exit through the hatch by spending 10 feet of movement. Those inside the servant have {@quickref Cover||3||total cover} from effects originating outside it. The controls within it allow creatures to see outside without obstruction.
While you are inside the servant, you can command it by using the controls. During your turn (for either attuned creature), you can command it in the following ways:
Open or close the hatch (no action required, once per turn)
Move the servant up to its speed (no action required)
As an action, you can command the servant to take one of the actions in its stat block or some other action.
When a creature provokes an opportunity attack from the servant, you can use your reaction to command the servant to make one Destructive Fist attack against that creature.
While there are no attuned creatures inside the servant, it is an inert object.
Mighty Servant of Leuk-o.entries.3.name: Controlling the Servant
Mighty Servant of Leuk-o.entries.4.entri: Upon his death, the soul of the mighty warlord Leuk-o was drawn into the artifact and has become its animating force. The servant has been known to attack or move of its own accord, particularly if doing so will cause destruction. Once every 24 hours, the servant, at the DM's discretion, takes one action while uncrewed.

If the servant loses half of its hit points or more, each creature attuned to it must succeed on a {@dc 20} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} for 24 hours. While {@condition charmed} in this way, the creature goes on a destructive spree, seeking to destroy structures and attack any unattuned creatures within sight of the servant, starting with those threatening the artifact-preferably using the servant, if possible.
Mighty Servant of Leuk-o.entries.4.name: Ghost in the Machine
Mighty Servant of Leuk-o.entries.5.entri: By inputting a specific series of lever pulls and button presses, the servant's two crew members can cause it to explode. The self-destruct code is not revealed to crew members when they attune to the artifact. If the code is discovered (the DM determines how), it requires two attuned crew members to be inside the servant and spend their actions on 3 consecutive rounds performing the command. Should the crew members begin the process of entering the code, though, the servant uses its Ghost in the Machine property and turns the crew members against each other.

If the crew members successfully implement the code, at the end of the third round, the servant explodes. Every creature in a 100-foot-radius sphere centered on the servant must make a {@dc 25} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 87 ({@damage 25d6}) force damage, 87 ({@damage 25d6}) lightning damage, and 87 ({@damage 25d6}) thunder damage. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage. Objects and structures in the area take triple damage. Creatures inside the servant are slain instantly and leave behind no remains.

This does not destroy the servant permanently. Rather, {@dice 2d6} days later, its parts—left arm, left leg, right arm, right leg, lower torso, and upper torso—drop from the sky in random places within 1,000 miles of the explosion. If brought within 5 feet of one another, the pieces reconnect and reform the servant.
Mighty Servant of Leuk-o.entries.5.name: Self-Destruct
Mighty Servant of Leuk-o.entries.6.entri: The servant can be destroyed in two ways. After it has self-destructed, its disconnected pieces can be melted down in one of the forge-temples of its ancient Olman creators. Alternatively, if the servant strikes the Machine of Lum the Mad, both artifacts explode in an eruption that is three times the size and three times the damage as the servant's self-destruct property.
Mighty Servant of Leuk-o.entries.6.name: Destroying the Servant
Mighty Servant of Leuk-o.name: Mighty Servant of Leuk-o
Military Saddle.entries.0: A saddle comes with a bit, a bridle, reins, and any other equipment needed to use the saddle. A Military Saddle gives {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any ability check you make to remain mounted.
Military Saddle.name: Military Saddle
Mimir.entries.0: This skull-shaped device is filled with knowledge. The device weighs 5 pounds and is covered with subtle etchings of planar sigils.
Mimir.entries.1: As a bonus action, you can toss the device into the air, whereupon it floats at a distance of {@dice 1d3} feet from you and you can access its properties. While the mimir is floating, a creature other than you can use an action to grasp or net the device, either by making a successful unarmed strike against AC 22 or a successful {@dc 22} Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check. You can use a bonus action to seize and stow the device.
Mimir.entries.2: The device has AC 22, 25 hit points, immunity to poison and psychic damage, and resistance to all other damage. It is considered to be an object that is being worn while it is floating near you.
Mimir.entries.3.entries.0: While the device is floating, you can use an action to cast {@spell legend lore} from the device. The device speaks the revealed lore aloud. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
Mimir.entries.3.name: Esoteric Knowledge
Mimir.entries.4.entries.0: The device knows basic, useful information about the planes of existence. While the device is floating, it verbally answers questions you or anyone you designate poses to it about that topic. It knows the information about the planes in the Dungeon Master's Guide, as well as basic information about the gate-towns of the Outlands (presented in chapter 3 of Sigil and the Outlands).
Mimir.entries.4.name: Planar Knowledge
Mimir.name: Mimir
Mind Crystal (Careful).entries.0: This gemstone contains a crystallized bit of spellcasting magic.
Mind Crystal (Careful).entries.1: When you cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action while holding the mind crystal, you can modify the spell in the way described below. You can use only a single mind crystal to modify the spell, and you can't use a mind crystal and a Metamagic option on the same spell. Once you use the mind crystal, it becomes a nonmagical gem worth 50 gp.
Mind Crystal (Careful).entries.2.entries: Choose up to three creatures affected by the spell. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell.
Mind Crystal (Careful).entries.2.name: Modify Spell
Mind Crystal (Careful).name: Mind Crystal (Careful)
Mind Crystal (Distant).entries.0: This gemstone contains a crystallized bit of spellcasting magic.
Mind Crystal (Distant).entries.1: When you cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action while holding the mind crystal, you can modify the spell in the way described below. You can use only a single mind crystal to modify the spell, and you can't use a mind crystal and a Metamagic option on the same spell. Once you use the mind crystal, it becomes a nonmagical gem worth 50 gp.
Mind Crystal (Distant).entries.2.entries: If the spell has a range of 5 feet or more and doesn't have a range of self, the spell's range increases by 100 feet. If the spell has a range of touch, its range becomes 30 feet.
Mind Crystal (Distant).entries.2.name: Modify Spell
Mind Crystal (Distant).name: Mind Crystal (Distant)
Mind Crystal (Empowered).entries.0: This gemstone contains a crystallized bit of spellcasting magic.
Mind Crystal (Empowered).entries.1: When you cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action while holding the mind crystal, you can modify the spell in the way described below. You can use only a single mind crystal to modify the spell, and you can't use a mind crystal and a Metamagic option on the same spell. Once you use the mind crystal, it becomes a nonmagical gem worth 50 gp.
Mind Crystal (Empowered).entries.2.entri: When you roll damage for the spell, you can reroll up to three damage dice. You must use the new rolls.
Mind Crystal (Empowered).entries.2.name: Modify Spell
Mind Crystal (Empowered).name: Mind Crystal (Empowered)
Mind Crystal (Extended).entries.0: This gemstone contains a crystallized bit of spellcasting magic.
Mind Crystal (Extended).entries.1: When you cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action while holding the mind crystal, you can modify the spell in the way described below. You can use only a single mind crystal to modify the spell, and you can't use a mind crystal and a Metamagic option on the same spell. Once you use the mind crystal, it becomes a nonmagical gem worth 50 gp.
Mind Crystal (Extended).entries.2.entrie: If the spell has a duration of 1 minute or longer, double the spell's duration, to a maximum duration of 24 hours.
Mind Crystal (Extended).entries.2.name: Modify Spell
Mind Crystal (Extended).name: Mind Crystal (Extended)
Mind Crystal (Heightened).entries.0: This gemstone contains a crystallized bit of spellcasting magic.
Mind Crystal (Heightened).entries.1: When you cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action while holding the mind crystal, you can modify the spell in the way described below. You can use only a single mind crystal to modify the spell, and you can't use a mind crystal and a Metamagic option on the same spell. Once you use the mind crystal, it becomes a nonmagical gem worth 50 gp.
Mind Crystal (Heightened).entries.2.entr: Choose one creature affected by the spell. That creature has disadvantage on the first saving throw it makes against the spell.
Mind Crystal (Heightened).entries.2.name: Modify Spell
Mind Crystal (Heightened).name: Mind Crystal (Heightened)
Mind Crystal (Quickened).entries.0: This gemstone contains a crystallized bit of spellcasting magic.
Mind Crystal (Quickened).entries.1: When you cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action while holding the mind crystal, you can modify the spell in the way described below. You can use only a single mind crystal to modify the spell, and you can't use a mind crystal and a Metamagic option on the same spell. Once you use the mind crystal, it becomes a nonmagical gem worth 50 gp.
Mind Crystal (Quickened).entries.2.entri: You change the spell's casting time to 1 bonus action for this casting.
Mind Crystal (Quickened).entries.2.name: Modify Spell
Mind Crystal (Quickened).name: Mind Crystal (Quickened)
Mind Crystal (Subtle).entries.0: This gemstone contains a crystallized bit of spellcasting magic.
Mind Crystal (Subtle).entries.1: When you cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action while holding the mind crystal, you can modify the spell in the way described below. You can use only a single mind crystal to modify the spell, and you can't use a mind crystal and a Metamagic option on the same spell. Once you use the mind crystal, it becomes a nonmagical gem worth 50 gp.
You cast the spell without any somatic or verbal components for this casting.
Mind Crystal (Subtle).entries.2.name: Modify Spell
Mind Crystal (Subtle).name: Mind Crystal (Subtle)
Mind Flayer Skull.entries.0: While you have the skull in your possession, you are {@condition invisible} to mind flayers, as is anything you are wearing or carrying.
Mind Flayer Skull.name: Mind Flayer Skull
Mind Lash.entries.0: In the hands of any creature other than a {@creature mind flayer}, a mind lash functions as a normal whip. In the hands of an illithid, this magic weapon strips away a creature's will to survive as it also strips away flesh, dealing an extra {@damage 2d4} psychic damage to any target it hits. Any creature that takes psychic damage from the mind lash must also succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or have disadvantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Mind Lash.name: Mind Lash
Mind Sharpener.entries.0: The item has 4 charges. When you fail a Constitution saving throw to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend 1 of the item's charges to succeed instead. The item regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.
Mind Sharpener.name: Mind Sharpener
Mindblasting Cap.entries.0: This soft, violet cap bears stitching in the pattern of folds on a brain.
Mindblasting Cap.entries.1: As a bonus action while wearing the cap, you can project psychic energy in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 5d8} psychic damage and has the stunned condition for 1 minute. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only. At the end of each of its turns, a stunned creature can repeat the saving throw, ending the stunned condition on itself on a success.
Mindblasting Cap.entries.2: Once this bonus action is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
Mindblasting Cap.name: Mindblasting Cap
Mindguard Crown.entries.0: While you wear this adamantine crown, you have advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws, and you have resistance to psychic damage.
Mindguard Crown.name: Mindguard Crown
Miners Pick.name: Miner's Pick
Mirror of Life Trapping.entries.0: When this 4-foot-tall, 2-foot-wide mirror is viewed indirectly, its surface shows faint images of creatures. The mirror weighs 50 pounds, and it has AC 11, HP 10, {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison and Psychic damage, and {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Bludgeoning damage. It shatters and is destroyed when reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
Mirror of Life Trapping.entries.1: If the mirror is hanging on a vertical surface and you are within 5 feet of it, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and use a command word to activate it. It remains activated until you take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and repeat the command word to deactivate it.
Mirror of Life Trapping.entries.2: Any creature other than you that sees its reflection in the activated mirror while within 30 feet of the mirror must succeed on a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw or be trapped, along with anything it is wearing or carrying, in one of the mirror's twelve extradimensional cells. A creature that knows the mirror's nature makes the save with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}, and Constructs succeed on the save automatically.
Mirror of Life Trapping.entries.3: An extradimensional cell is an infinite expanse filled with thick fog that reduces visibility to 10 feet. Creatures trapped in the mirror's cells don't age, and they don't need to eat, drink, or sleep. A creature trapped within a cell can escape using magic that permits planar travel. Otherwise, the creature is confined to the cell until freed.
Mirror of Life Trapping.entries.4: If the mirror traps a creature but its twelve extradimensional cells are already occupied, the mirror frees one trapped creature at random to accommodate the new prisoner. A freed creature appears in an unoccupied space within sight of the mirror but facing away from it. If the mirror is shattered, all creatures it contains are freed and appear in unoccupied spaces near it.
Mirror of Life Trapping.entries.5: While within 5 feet of the mirror, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to name one creature trapped in it or call out a particular cell by number. The creature named or contained in the named cell appears as an image on the mirror's surface. You and the creature can then communicate.
Mirror of Life Trapping.entries.6: In a similar way, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and use a second command word to free one creature trapped in the mirror. The freed creature appears, along with its possessions, in the unoccupied space nearest to the mirror and facing away from it.
Mirror of Life Trapping.entries.7: Placing the mirror inside an extradimensional space created by a {@item Bag of Holding|XDMG}, Portable Hole, or similar item instantly destroys both items and opens a gate to the Astral Plane. The gate originates where the one item was placed inside the other. Any creature within 10 feet of the gate and not behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} is sucked through it to a random location on the Astral Plane. The gate then closes. The gate is one-way only and can't be reopened.
Mirror of Life Trapping.name: Mirror of Life Trapping
Mirror of Reflected Pasts.entries.0: This mirror of elven design allows those who stare into it to reflect on positive memories. The 3-foot-tall mirror weighs 25 pounds, and it has AC 11, 10 hit points, and vulnerability to bludgeoning damage. It shatters and is destroyed if reduced to 0 hit points.
Mirror of Reflected Pasts.entries.1: While holding the mirror upright, you can use an action to speak its command word and activate it. While activated, the mirror hovers in the air, and it can be destroyed but not moved. It remains activated until you use an action to speak the command word again or your attunement to the mirror ends, at which point the mirror harmlessly floats to the ground. Once the mirror has been deactivated, it can't be activated again until the next dawn.
Mirror of Reflected Pasts.entries.2: If a non-Construct creature other than you sees its reflection in the activated mirror while within 30 feet of it, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition paralyzed} until the mirror is deactivated or until that creature can no longer see the mirror. A creature {@condition paralyzed} by the mirror can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to this mirror's effect for the next 24 hours.
Mirror of Reflected Pasts.entries.3: While {@condition paralyzed} by the mirror, the creature sees events from their past reflected in the mirror's glass. These memories aren't real, but rather idealized versions of those occurrences. Nearby observers can glimpse flashes of these memories if looking indirectly at the mirror.
Mirror of Reflected Pasts.name: Mirror of Reflected Pasts
Mirror of the Past.entries.0: The holder of this platinum hand mirror can learn something about the history of a specific object or creature by taking an action to gaze into the mirror and think of the target. Instead of the holder's reflection, the mirror presents scenes from the target's past. Information conveyed is accurate, but it is random and cryptic, and presented in no particular order. Once it is activated, the mirror gives its information for 1 minute or less, then returns to normal. It can't be used again until the next dawn.
Mirror of the Past.name: Mirror of the Past
Mirror.entries.0: A handheld steel Mirror is useful for personal cosmetics but also for peeking around corners and reflecting light as a signal.
Mirror.name: Mirror
Mistral Mantle.entries.0: This thick, fur-lined cloak has the frost rune stitched on the hem in silvery blue thread. Frigid wind swirls around the cloak, regardless of the weather.
Mistral Mantle.entries.1: While wearing this cloak, you have resistance to cold damage. Additionally, when you move within 5 feet of a creature, you can cause the cloak's cold wind to buffet the creature. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d6} cold damage and have the prone condition. A creature can be affected by the mantle only once during a turn.
Mistral Mantle.entries.2.entries.0: As an action, you can invoke the mantle's rune to cast the {@spell sleet storm} spell (save {@dc 14}) with it. When you use the mantle to cast the spell, the area of the spell isn't {@quickref difficult terrain||3} for you, and you can see through the storm, ignoring the normal penalties of a {@quickref Vision and Light||2||heavily obscured} area.
Mistral Mantle.entries.2.entries.1: Once the rune has been invoked, it can't be invoked again until the next dawn.
Mistral Mantle.entries.2.name: Invoking the Rune
Mistral Mantle.name: Mistral Mantle
Mizzium Apparatus.entries.0: Innovation is a dangerous pursuit, at least the way the mages of the Izzet League engage in it. As protection against the risk of an experiment going awry, they have developed a device to help channel and control their magic. This apparatus is a collection of leather straps, flexible tubing, glass cylinders, and plates, bracers, and fittings made from a magic-infused metal alloy called mizzium, all assembled into a harness. The item weighs 8 pounds.
Mizzium Apparatus.entries.1: While you are wearing the mizzium apparatus, you can use it as an arcane focus. In addition, you can attempt to cast a spell that you do not know or have prepared. The spell you choose must be on your class's spell list and of a level for which you have a spell slot, and you must provide the spell's components.
Mizzium Apparatus.entries.2: You expend a spell slot to cast the spell as normal, but before resolving it you must make an Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) check. The DC is 10 + twice the level of the spell slot you expend to cast the spell.
Mizzium Apparatus.entries.3: On a successful check, you cast the spell as normal, using your spell save DC and spellcasting ability modifier. On a failed check, you cast a different spell from the one you intended. Randomly determine the spell you cast by rolling on the table for the level of the spell slot you expended. If the slot is 6th level or higher, roll on the table for 5th-level spells.
Mizzium Apparatus.entries.4: If you try to cast a cantrip you don't know, the DC for the Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) check is 10, and on a failed check, there is no effect.
Mizzium Apparatus.entries.5.caption: 1st-Level Spells
Mizzium Apparatus.entries.6.caption: 2nd-Level Spells
Mizzium Apparatus.entries.7.caption: 3rd-Level Spells
Mizzium Apparatus.entries.8.caption: 4th-Level Spells
Mizzium Apparatus.entries.9.caption: 5th-Level Spells
Mizzium Apparatus.name: Mizzium Apparatus
Mizzium Mortar.entries.0: This short tube, about 2 feet long and 6 inches in diameter, is made from mizzium, a magically enhanced metal alloy forged by the Izzet League. The end that's pointed toward a target is open, and a glowing ball of molten metal can be seen at the other end as long as the mortar has at least 1 charge remaining.
Mizzium Mortar.entries.1: The mortar has 4 charges for the following properties. It regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.
Mizzium Mortar.entries.2.entries.0: You can expend 1 charge as an action to loose a 30-foot cone of molten mizzium. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 5d4} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
Mizzium Mortar.entries.2.name: Molten Spray
Mizzium Mortar.entries.3.entries.0: You can expend 3 charges as an action to launch a hail of molten projectiles in a 20-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder centered on a point you can see within 60 feet of you. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes {@damage 5d8} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
Mizzium Mortar.entries.3.name: Mizzium Bombard
Mizzium Mortar.name: Mizzium Mortar
Monster Camouflage.entries.0: A suit of Monster Camouflage looks like a Beast or Monstrosity, such as an {@creature owlbear|XMM}. To discern that you're disguised, a creature must take the {@action Study|XPHB} action to inspect your appearance and succeed on a DC 10 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB} or {@skill Nature|XPHB}) check. The creature has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on this check if it is within 30 feet of you and automatically succeeds on this check if you do anything the monster you're disguised as couldn't do.
Monster Camouflage.name: Monster Camouflage
Monster Hunters Pack.entries.1.items.5: a {@item holy water (flask)|phb|flask of holy water}
Monster Hunters Pack.name: Monster Hunter's Pack
Moodmark Paint.entries.0: This thick, black paint is stored in a small jar, containing enough paint to apply moodmarks to one creature. The paint is dabbed on the face in spots or markings that often resemble the eyes of insects or spiders. Applying the paint in this way takes 1 minute.
Moodmark Paint.entries.1: For the next 8 hours, the marks change to reflect your mental state. A creature that can see you and makes a successful {@dc 10} Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check can discern whether you are happy, sad, angry, disgusted, surprised, or afraid, as well as the main source of that emotion. For example, you might communicate fear caused by a monster you just saw around the corner, grief at the loss of a friend, or happiness derived from pride in your performance in combat. A dark elf has advantage on this check.
Moodmark Paint.name: Moodmark Paint
Moon Card.entries.0: As an action, you can speak a command word while touching this card and transform into a wolflike biped for 1 minute. For that duration, you have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage, and when you hit a target with a weapon or an unarmed strike, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} force damage. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
Moon Card.name: Moon Card
Moonstone.entries.0: A white with pale-blue glow gemstone.
Moonstone.name: Moonstone
Moorbounder.entries.0: Beasts of burden are common throughout Xhorhas, and often horses and other riding animals are the best or only option. For those who require speed and have a little more coin to spend, the best option is often a moorbounder. However, moorbounders that haven't undergone proper training or established bonds of trust with their masters tend to attack and even eat their riders.
Moorbounder.entries.1: Moorbounders can cost anywhere from 300 to 500 gp each. They're often cheaper and more abundant in towns on the wastes than in cities like Rosohna.
Moorbounder.name: Moorbounder
Moss Agate.entries.0: A pink or yellow white with mossy gray or green markings gemstone.
Moss Agate.name: Moss Agate
Mournland Trinket.entries.0.caption: Mournland Trinkets
Mournland Trinket.entries.0.rows.0.1: The favorite childhood toy of one of the adventurers, floating in midair
Mournland Trinket.entries.0.rows.1.1: A tin whistle that makes beautiful colors and patterns magically appear when it's played
Mournland Trinket.entries.0.rows.4.1: A silver lapel pin of a finely detailed gorgon's head with ruby eyes
Mournland Trinket.entries.0.rows.5.1: A monocle that can be used as a telescope
Mournland Trinket.entries.0.rows.6.1: A six-inch-tall mechanical marmoset in need of minor repairs
Mournland Trinket.entries.0.rows.7.1: A small medallion made of silver, depicting a tower with an eye at the top of it and other smaller eyes embedded in the sides
Mournland Trinket.entries.0.rows.8.1: A cloak pin made of iron in the shape of two hammers joined by a semicircular haft
Mournland Trinket.entries.0.rows.9.1: A wooden dinosaur toy that has movable legs
Mournland Trinket.name: Mournland Trinket
Mudslick Tower.entries.0: You can use an action to place this 1-inch-diameter granite sphere on the ground and speak its command word, which is "petrification" in Terran. The sphere rapidly grows into a stout tower that remains until you use an action to touch the tower and speak the command word again, whereupon the tower shrinks back to a 1-inch-diameter granite sphere. The tower must be empty to shrink in this way. The tower bristles with muddy knobs that constantly extrude and retract across its surface, as though the tower were breathing through a coating of thick mud.
Mudslick Tower.entries.1: Each creature in the area where the tower appears must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 10d10} bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
