Piwafwi of Fire Resistance.entries.0: This dark spider silk cloak is made by {@creature drow}. It is a {@item cloak of elvenkind}. It also grants resistance to fire damage while you wear it. It loses its magic if exposed to sunlight for 1 hour without interruption.
Piwafwi of Fire Resistance.entries.1: While you wear this cloak with its hood up, Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks made to see you have disadvantage. and you have advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide, as the cloak's color shifts to camouflage you. Pulling the hood up or down requires an action.
Piwafwi of Fire Resistance.name: Piwafwi of Fire Resistance
Pixie Dust.entries.0: As an action, you can sprinkle this dust on yourself or another creature you can see within 5 feet of you. The recipient gains a flying speed of 30 feet and the ability to hover for 1 minute. If the creature is airborne when this effect ends, it falls safely to the ground, taking no damage and landing on its feet.
Pixie Dust.entries.1: A small packet holds enough pixie dust for one use.
Pixie Dust.name: Pixie Dust
Planar Key.entries.0: This key is etched with sigils.
Planar Key.entries.1: A planar key begins existence with {@dice 1d4 + 1} charges and cannot regain charges. When the key's last charge is expended, the key's sigils and magic fade away, but the key remains.
Planar Key.entries.2: While touching the key, you can use your action to expend 1 charge and have the key cast {@spell plane shift} limited to the key's predefined destination only. Each planar key links to a single destination defined when the key is made.
Planar Key.name: Planar Key
Planar Philosopher Trinket.entries.0.row: Locket with a picture of my mentor and an inscription I can't read
Bleached cranium rat skull with colored glass beads in its eye sockets
Torn parchment with half a rebus painted on it
Fragment of a bronze blade covered in verdigris
Broken holy symbol of a forgotten god
Planar Philosopher Trinket.name: Planar Philosopher Trinket
Planecallers Codex.entries.0: The pages of this book are bound in fiend hide, and its cover is embossed with a diagram of the Great Wheel of the multiverse. When found, the book contains the following spells: {@spell banishment}, {@spell find familiar}, {@spell gate}, {@spell magic circle}, {@spell planar binding}, and {@spell summon elemental|TCE}. It functions as a spellbook for you.
Planecallers Codex.entries.1: While you are holding the book, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your wizard spells.
Planecallers Codex.entries.2: The book has 3 charges, and it regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. You can use the charges in the following ways while holding it:
Planecallers Codex.entries.3.items.0: If you spend 1 minute studying the book, you can expend 1 charge to replace one of your prepared wizard spells with a different spell in the book. The new spell must be of the conjuration school.
Planecallers Codex.entries.3.items.1: When you cast a conjuration spell that summons or creates one creature, you can expend 1 charge to grant that creature advantage on attack rolls for 1 minute.
Planecallers Codex.name: Planecaller's Codex
Plate of Knights Fellowship.entries.0: This gleaming set of silver-and-gold plate armor never tarnishes.
Plate of Knights Fellowship.entries.1: While wearing this armor, you can use a bonus action to summon the spirit of a warrior to your aid. The spirit's corporeal form manifests in an unoccupied space of your choice within 30 feet of you, and it uses the {@creature knight} stat block. The spirit disappears when it drops to 0 hit points or after 1 minute, whichever comes first.
Plate of Knights Fellowship.entries.2: The spirit is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, the spirit shares your initiative count but takes its turn immediately after yours. The spirit obeys your commands (no action required by you); if you don't issue any commands, the spirit takes the {@action Dodge} action and uses its movement to avoid danger.
Plate of Knights Fellowship.entries.3: Once this bonus action is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
Plate of Knights Fellowship.entries.4: Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body.
Plate of Knights Fellowship.name: Plate of Knight's Fellowship
Platinum (pp).entries.0: Common coins come in several different denominations based on the relative worth of the metal from which they are made. The three most common coins are the gold piece (gp), the silver piece (sp), and the copper piece (cp).
Platinum (pp).entries.1: With one gold piece, a character can buy a belt pouch, 50 feet of good rope, or a goat. A skilled (but not exceptional) artisan can earn one gold piece a day. The gold piece is the standard unit of measure for wealth, even if the coin itself is not commonly used. When merchants discuss deals that involve goods or services worth hundreds or thousands of gold pieces, the transactions don't usually involve the exchange of individual coins. Rather, the gold piece is a standard measure of value, and the actual exchange is in gold bars, letters of credit, or valuable goods.
Platinum (pp).entries.2: One gold piece is worth ten silver pieces, the most prevalent coin among commoners. A silver piece buys a laborer's work for a day, a flask of lamp oil, or a night's rest in a poor inn.
Platinum (pp).entries.3: One silver piece is worth ten copper pieces, which are common among laborers and beggars. A single copper piece buys a candle, a torch, or a piece of chalk.
Platinum (pp).entries.4: In addition, unusual coins made of other precious metals sometimes appear in treasure hoards. The electrum piece (ep) and the platinum piece (pp) originate from fallen empires and lost kingdoms, and they sometimes arouse suspicion and skepticism when used in transactions. An electrum piece is worth five silver pieces, and a platinum piece is worth ten gold pieces.
Platinum (pp).entries.5: A standard coin weighs about a third of an ounce, so fifty coins weigh a pound.
Platinum (pp).name: Platinum (pp)
Platinum 10-Zino Coin.name: Platinum 10-Zino Coin
Platinum 100-Zino Coin.name: Platinum 100-Zino Coin
Platinum Scarf.entries.0: This scarf is made of sturdy cloth and covered in platinum-colored scales.
Platinum Scarf.entries.1: As an action, you can pull a scale from the scarf and speak a command word. When you do so, choose one of the following effects:
Platinum Scarf.entries.2.entries.0: The scale disappears, and you or a creature you touch regains {@dice 10d4} hit points.
Platinum Scarf.entries.2.name: Breath of Life
Platinum Scarf.entries.3.entries.0: For 1 hour or until you dismiss it (no action required), the scale becomes a {@item +1 shield}, which you or another creature can use. A creature wielding the shield has immunity to radiant damage.
Platinum Scarf.entries.3.name: Platinum Shield
Platinum Scarf.entries.4.entries.0: For 1 hour or until you dismiss it (no action required), the scale becomes a magic {@item light hammer|phb}, which you or another creature can use. The weapon deals {@damage 2d4} radiant damage, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for a light hammer. It deals an extra {@damage 2d4} radiant damage to chromatic dragons.
Platinum Scarf.entries.4.entries.1: Once three scales have been pulled from the scarf, no more scales can be removed until the next dawn, when all the missing scales grow back. If you pull off a scale but don't speak a command word, it disappears after 1 minute.
Platinum Scarf.entries.4.name: Radiant Hammer
Platinum Scarf.name: Platinum Scarf
Platinum bracelet set with an emerald.na: Platinum bracelet set with an emerald
Platinum headdress with topaz sun symbol: Platinum headdress with topaz sun symbol (Sun Empire)
Platinum ring with yellow sapphire (Sun: Platinum ring with yellow sapphire (Sun Empire)
Platinum staff topped with amber (Sun Em: Platinum staff topped with amber (Sun Empire)
Platinum.name: Platinum
Playing Cards.entries.0.items.0.name: Характеристика:
Discern whether someone is cheating ({@dc 10}), or win the game ({@dc 20})
Playing Cards.entries.0.items.1.name: Использование:
Playing Cards.name: Playing Cards
Poison Absorbing Tattoo.name: Poison Absorbing Tattoo
Poison Soaked Kukri.entries.0: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically coat the blade of this Dagger with poison. The poison remains for 1 minute or until an attack using this weapon hits a creature. That creature must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 2d8} Poison damage and have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. This {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} can't be used again this way until the next dawn.
Poison Soaked Kukri.name: Poison Soaked Kukri
Poisoners Kit.entries.0.items.0.name: Характеристика:
Poisoners Kit.entries.0.items.1.name: Использование:
Poisoners Kit.entries.0.items.2.name: Craft:
Poisoners Kit.name: Poisoner's Kit
Pole of Angling.entries.0: This item functions as a Pole. While holding it, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cause it to transform into a fishing pole with a hook, a line, and a reel, or have the fishing pole revert to a Pole.
Pole of Angling.name: Pole of Angling
Pole of Collapsing.entries.0: This item functions as a Pole. While holding it, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to collapse it into a 1-foot-long rod for ease of storage (the pole's weight doesn't change) or cause the 1-foot-long rod to revert to a Pole. The rod elongates only as far as the surrounding space allows.
Pole of Collapsing.name: Pole of Collapsing
Pole.entries.0: A Pole is 10 feet long. You can use it to touch something up to 10 feet away. If you must make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check as part of a High or {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB}, you can use the Pole to vault, giving yourself {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check.
Pole.name: Pole
Pony.name: Pony
Portable Hole.entries.0: This fine black cloth, soft as silk, is folded up to the dimensions of a handkerchief. It unfolds into a circular sheet 6 feet in diameter.
Portable Hole.entries.1: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to unfold a Portable Hole and place it on or against a solid surface, whereupon the Portable Hole creates an extradimensional hole 10 feet deep. The cylindrical space within the hole exists on a different plane of existence, so it can't be used to create open passages. Any creature inside an open Portable Hole can exit the hole by climbing out of it.
Portable Hole.entries.2: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to close a Portable Hole by taking hold of the edges of the cloth and folding it up. Folding the cloth closes the hole, and any creatures or objects within remain in the extradimensional space. No matter what's in it, the hole weighs next to nothing.
Portable Hole.entries.3: If the hole is folded up, a creature within the hole's extradimensional space can take an action to make a {@dc 10} Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check. On a successful check, the creature forces its way out and appears within 5 feet of the Portable Hole. A closed Portable Hole holds enough air for 1 hour of breathing, divided by the number of breathing creatures inside.
Portable Hole.entries.4: Placing a Portable Hole inside an extradimensional space created by a {@item Bag of Holding|XDMG}, {@item Heward's Handy Haversack|XDMG}, or similar item instantly destroys both items and opens a gate to the Astral Plane. The gate originates where the one item was placed inside the other. Any creature within 10 feet of the gate and not behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} is sucked through it and deposited in a random location on the Astral Plane. The gate then closes. The gate is one-way only and can't be reopened.
Portable Hole.name: Portable Hole
Portable Ram.entries.0: You can use a Portable Ram to break down doors. When doing so, you gain a +4 bonus to the Strength check. One other character can help you use the ram, giving you {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on this check.
Portable Ram.name: Portable Ram
Portal Compass.entries.0: This portable arcane instrument points in the direction of the last portal it passed through while it and the portal are on the same plane of existence. If that portal no longer exists, the needle becomes static until the compass passes through a new portal.
Portal Compass.name: Portal Compass
Portfolio Keeper.entries.0: At rank 2, you gain the use of a weathered leather pouch that is an uncommon magic item. The portfolio keeper holds and organizes notes, brochures, and business cards bearing your contact information. It also has an inexhaustible supply of brochures related to your franchise's current branding scheme.
Portfolio Keeper.entries.1: When you meet someone for the first time, their details and a rough sketch are magically stored on a small parchment card in the portfolio keeper. You can access the details of any such stored card as a bonus action.
Portfolio Keeper.entries.2.entries.0: At rank 3, the power of your portfolio keeper becomes more potent, making it a rare magic item. When you give out a business card stored in your portfolio keeper, you can choose to have the card allow its recipient to contact you through your {@item sending stone|AI} as if they had cast a {@spell sending} spell. A business card loses this power seven days after it has been given out, and no more than five cards can have this power at one time.
Portfolio Keeper.entries.2.entries.1: You can also use your {@item sending stone|AI} to cast {@spell sending} and contact anyone who has one of your business cards. Once you use this feature of the stone, you cannot use it again until dawn seven days later.
Portfolio Keeper.entries.2.name: Cards of Sending
Portfolio Keeper.entries.3.entries.0: Also at rank 4, you are able to make a focused effort to keep a potential customer's attention. When you provide a humanoid with a business card from your portfolio keeper, you can cast {@spell charm person} on them (save {@dc 15}). The spell ends prematurely if the character loses the business card.
Portfolio Keeper.entries.3.name: Charming Introduction
Portfolio Keeper.name: Portfolio Keeper
Pot of Awakening.entries.0: If you plant an ordinary shrub in this 10-pound clay pot and let it grow for 30 days, the shrub magically transforms into an {@creature Awakened Shrub|XMM} at the end of that time. When the shrub awakens, its roots break the pot, destroying it.
Pot of Awakening.entries.1: The awakened shrub is {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} toward you and obeys your commands. Absent commands from you, it does nothing.
Pot of Awakening.name: Pot of Awakening
Potion of Acid Resistance.name: Potion of Acid Resistance
Potion of Advantage.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you gain advantage on one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw of your choice that you make within the next hour.
Potion of Advantage.entries.1: This potion takes the form of a sparkling, golden mist that moves and pours like water.
Potion of Advantage.name: Potion of Advantage
Potion of Animal Friendship.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you can cast the level 3 version of the {@spell Animal Friendship|XPHB} spell (save {@dc 13}).
Potion of Animal Friendship.entries.1: Agitating this potion's muddy liquid brings little bits into view: a fish scale, a hummingbird feather, a cat claw, or a squirrel hair.
Potion of Animal Friendship.name: Potion of Animal Friendship
Potion of Aqueous Form.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you transform into a pool of water. You return to your true form after 10 minutes or if you are {@condition incapacitated} or die.
Potion of Aqueous Form.entries.1: You're under the following effects while in this form:
Potion of Aqueous Form.entries.2.items.0: You have a swimming speed of 30 feet. You can move over or through other liquids. You can enter and occupy the space of another creature. You can rise up to your normal height, and you can pass through even Tiny openings. You extinguish nonmagical flames in any space you enter.
Potion of Aqueous Form.entries.2.items.1: You have resistance to nonmagical damage. You also have advantage on Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution saving throws.
Potion of Aqueous Form.entries.2.items.2: You can't talk, attack, cast spells, or activate magic items. Any objects you were carrying or wearing meld into your new form and are inaccessible, though you continue to be affected by anything you're wearing, such as armor.
Potion of Aqueous Form.name: Potion of Aqueous Form
Potion of Clairvoyance.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the {@spell Clairvoyance|XPHB} spell (no {@status Concentration|XPHB} required).
Potion of Clairvoyance.entries.1: An eyeball bobs in this potion's yellowish liquid but vanishes when the potion is opened.
Potion of Clairvoyance.name: Potion of Clairvoyance
Potion of Climbing.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} for 1 hour. During this time, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) checks to climb.
Potion of Climbing.entries.1: This potion is separated into brown, silver, and gray layers resembling bands of stone. Shaking the bottle fails to mix the colors.
Potion of Climbing.name: Potion of Climbing
Potion of Cloud Giant Strength.entries.0: When you drink this potion, your Strength score changes to 27 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect on you if your Strength is equal to or greater than that score.
Potion of Cloud Giant Strength.entries.1: This potion's transparent liquid has floating in it a sliver of fingernail from a cloud giant.
Potion of Cloud Giant Strength.name: Potion of Cloud Giant Strength
Potion of Cold Resistance.name: Potion of Cold Resistance
Potion of Comprehension.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the {@spell Comprehend Languages|XPHB} spell for 1 hour.
Potion of Comprehension.entries.1: This potion's liquid is a clear concoction with bits of salt and soot swirling in it.
Potion of Comprehension.name: Potion of Comprehension
Potion of Diminution.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you gain the "reduce" effect of the {@spell Enlarge/Reduce|XPHB} spell for {@dice 1d4} hours (no {@status Concentration|XPHB} required).
Potion of Diminution.entries.1: The red in the potion's liquid continuously contracts to a tiny bead and then expands to color the clear liquid around it. Shaking the bottle fails to interrupt this process.
Potion of Diminution.name: Potion of Diminution
Potion of Dragons Majesty.entries.0: This potion looks like liquid gold, with a single scale from a {@filter chromatic|bestiary|search=chromatic dragon}, {@filter gem|bestiary|search=gem dragon}, or {@filter metallic|bestiary|search=metallic dragon} dragon suspended in it. When you drink this potion, you transform into an adult dragon of the same kind as the dragon the scale came from. The transformation lasts for 1 hour. Any equipment you are wearing or carrying melds into your new form or falls to the ground (your choice). For the duration, you use the game statistics of the adult dragon instead of your own, but you retain your languages, personality, and memories. You can't use a dragon's Change Shape or its legendary or lair actions.
Potion of Dragons Majesty.name: Potion of Dragon's Majesty
Potion of Fire Breath.entries.0: After drinking this potion, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to exhale fire at a target within 30 feet of yourself. The target makes a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. The effect ends after you exhale the fire three times or when 1 hour has passed.
Potion of Fire Breath.entries.1: This potion's orange liquid flickers, and smoke fills the top of the container and wafts out whenever it is opened.
Potion of Fire Breath.name: Potion of Fire Breath
Potion of Fire Giant Strength.entries.0: When you drink this potion, your Strength score changes to 25 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect on you if your Strength is equal to or greater than that score.
Potion of Fire Giant Strength.entries.1: This potion's transparent liquid has floating in it a sliver of fingernail from a fire giant.
Potion of Fire Giant Strength.name: Potion of Fire Giant Strength
Potion of Fire Resistance.name: Potion of Fire Resistance
Potion of Flying.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} for 1 hour and can hover. If you're in the air when the potion wears off, you fall unless you have some other means of staying aloft.
Potion of Flying.entries.1: This potion's clear liquid floats at the top of its container and has cloudy white impurities drifting in it.
Potion of Flying.name: Potion of Flying
Potion of Force Resistance.name: Potion of Force Resistance
Potion of Frost Giant Strength.entries.0: When you drink this potion, your Strength score changes to 23 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect on you if your Strength is equal to or greater than that score.
Potion of Frost Giant Strength.entries.1: This potion's transparent liquid has floating in it a sliver of fingernail from a frost giant.
Potion of Frost Giant Strength.name: Potion of Frost Giant Strength
Potion of Gaseous Form.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the {@spell Gaseous Form|XPHB} spell for 1 hour (no {@status Concentration|XPHB} required) or until you end the effect as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Potion of Gaseous Form.entries.1: This potion's container seems to hold fog that moves and pours like water.
Potion of Gaseous Form.name: Potion of Gaseous Form
Potion of Giant Size.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you become Huge for 24 hours if you are Medium or smaller, otherwise the potion does nothing. For that duration, your Strength becomes 25, if it isn't already higher, and your hit point maximum is doubled (your current hit points are doubled when you drink the potion). In addition, the reach of your melee attacks increases by 5 feet.
Potion of Giant Size.entries.1: Everything you are carrying and wearing also increases in size for the duration. When rolling damage for weapons enlarged in this manner, roll three times the normal number of dice; for example, an enlarged longsword would deal {@damage 3d8} slashing damage (instead of {@dice 1d8}), or {@damage 3d10} slashing damage (instead of {@dice 1d10}) when used with two hands.
Potion of Giant Size.entries.2: When the effect ends, any hit points you have above your hit point maximum become temporary hit points. This potion is a pale white liquid made from the tongue of a giant clam, with a pungent aroma akin to that of rotting algae. It tastes sweet, however, when consumed.
Potion of Giant Size.name: Potion of Giant Size
Potion of Greater Healing.entries.0: You regain {@dice 4d4 + 4} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} when you drink this potion. The potion's red liquid glimmers when agitated.
Potion of Greater Healing.name: Potion of Greater Healing
Potion of Greater Invisibility.entries.0: This potion's container looks empty but feels as though it holds liquid. When you drink the potion, you have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition for 1 hour.
Potion of Greater Invisibility.name: Potion of Greater Invisibility
Potion of Growth.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you gain the "enlarge" effect of the {@spell Enlarge/Reduce|XPHB} spell for 10 minutes (no {@status Concentration|XPHB} required).
Potion of Growth.entries.1: The red in the potion's liquid continuously expands from a tiny bead to color the clear liquid around it and then contracts. Shaking the bottle fails to interrupt this process.
Potion of Growth.name: Potion of Growth
Potion of Healing.entries.0: This potion is a magic item. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can drink it or administer it to another creature within 5 feet of yourself. The creature that drinks the magical red fluid in this vial regains {@dice 2d4 + 2} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. The potion's red liquid glimmers when agitated.
Potion of Healing.name: Potion of Healing
Potion of Heroism.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you gain 10 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} that last for 1 hour. For the same duration, you are under the effect of the {@spell Bless|XPHB} spell (no {@status Concentration|XPHB} required).
Potion of Heroism.entries.1: This potion's blue liquid bubbles and steams as if boiling.
Potion of Heroism.name: Potion of Heroism
Potion of Hill Giant Strength.entries.0: When you drink this potion, your Strength score changes to 21 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect on you if your Strength is equal to or greater than that score.
Potion of Hill Giant Strength.entries.1: This potion's transparent liquid has floating in it a sliver of fingernail from a hill giant.
Potion of Hill Giant Strength.name: Potion of Hill Giant Strength
Potion of Invisibility.entries.0: This potion's container looks empty but feels as though it holds liquid. When you drink the potion, you have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition for 1 hour. The effect ends early if you make an attack roll, deal damage, or cast a spell.
Potion of Invisibility.name: Potion of Invisibility
Potion of Invulnerability.entries.0: For 1 minute after you drink this potion, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all damage.
Potion of Invulnerability.entries.1: This potion's syrupy liquid looks like liquefied iron.
Potion of Invulnerability.name: Potion of Invulnerability
Potion of Lightning Resistance.name: Potion of Lightning Resistance
Potion of Longevity.entries.0: When you drink this potion, your physical age is reduced by {@dice 1d6 + 6} years, to a minimum of 13 years. Each time you subsequently drink a Potion of Longevity, there is 10 percent cumulative chance that you instead age by {@dice 1d6 + 6} years.
Potion of Longevity.entries.1: Suspended in this amber liquid is a tiny heart that, against all reason, is still beating. These ingredients vanish when the potion is opened.
Potion of Longevity.name: Potion of Longevity
Potion of Maximum Power.entries.0: The first time you cast a damage-dealing spell of 4th level or lower within 1 minute after drinking the potion, instead of rolling dice to determine the damage dealt, you can instead use the highest number possible for each die.
Potion of Maximum Power.entries.1: This glowing purple liquid smells of sugar and plum, but it has a muddy taste.
Potion of Maximum Power.name: Potion of Maximum Power
Potion of Mind Control (beast).entries.0: When you drink a potion of mind control, you can cast a dominate spell (save {@dc 15}) on a specific creature if you do so before the end of your next turn. If you don't, the potion is wasted.
Potion of Mind Control (beast).entries.1: A potion of mind control produces the effect of a {@spell dominate beast} spell. If the target's initial saving throw fails, the effect lasts for 1 hour, with no {@status concentration} required on your part. The {@condition charmed} creature has disadvantage on new saving throws to break the effect during this time.
Potion of Mind Control (beast).name: Potion of Mind Control (beast)
Potion of Mind Control (humanoid).entrie: When you drink a potion of mind control, you can cast a dominate spell (save {@dc 15}) on a specific creature if you do so before the end of your next turn. If you don't, the potion is wasted.

A potion of mind control produces the effect of a {@spell dominate person} spell. If the target's initial saving throw fails, the effect lasts for 1 hour, with no {@status concentration} required on your part. The {@condition charmed} creature has disadvantage on new saving throws to break the effect during this time.
Potion of Mind Control (humanoid).name: Potion of Mind Control (humanoid)
Potion of Mind Control (monster).entries: When you drink a potion of mind control, you can cast a dominate spell (save {@dc 15}) on a specific creature if you do so before the end of your next turn. If you don't, the potion is wasted.

A potion of mind control produces the effect of a {@spell dominate monster} spell. If the target's initial saving throw fails, the effect lasts for 1 hour, with no {@status concentration} required on your part. The {@condition charmed} creature has disadvantage on new saving throws to break the effect during this time.
Potion of Mind Control (monster).name: Potion of Mind Control (monster)
Potion of Mind Reading.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the {@spell Detect Thoughts|XPHB} spell (save {@dc 13}) for 10 minutes (no {@status Concentration|XPHB} required).
Potion of Mind Reading.entries.1: This potion's dense, purple liquid has an ovoid cloud of pink floating in it.
Potion of Mind Reading.name: Potion of Mind Reading
Potion of Necrotic Resistance.name: Potion of Necrotic Resistance
Potion of Poison Resistance.name: Potion of Poison Resistance
Potion of Poison.entries.0: This concoction looks, smells, and tastes like a {@item Potion of Healing|XDMG} or another beneficial potion. However, it is actually poison masked by illusion magic. {@spell Identify|XPHB} reveals its true nature.
Potion of Poison.entries.1: If you drink this potion, you take {@damage 4d6} Poison damage and must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 hour.
Potion of Poison.name: Potion of Poison
Potion of Polychromy.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you and everything you are wearing or carrying take on a rainbow-hued appearance for 1 hour. During that time, you can use a bonus action to turn any color or combination of colors you choose. If you mimic the colors of your surroundings, your hues continually shift to match your surroundings, and you have advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks until you change your colors again or the potion wears off.
Potion of Polychromy.entries.1: The potion is separated into seven brightly colored bands of immiscible liquids and has a syrupy taste.
Potion of Polychromy.name: Potion of Polychromy
Potion of Possibility.entries.0: When you drink this clear potion, you gain two Fragments of Possibility, each of which looks like a Tiny, grayish bead of energy that follows you around, staying within 1 foot of you at all times. Each fragment lasts for 8 hours or until used.
Potion of Possibility.entries.1: When you make an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw, you can expend your fragment to roll an additional {@dice d20} and choose which of the {@dice d20}s to use. Alternatively, when an attack roll is made against you, you can expend your fragment to roll a {@dice d20} and choose which of the {@dice d20}s to use, the one you rolled or the one the attacker rolled.
Potion of Possibility.entries.2: If the original {@dice d20} roll has advantage or disadvantage, you roll your {@dice d20} after advantage or disadvantage has been applied to the original roll.
Potion of Possibility.entries.3: While you have one or more Fragments of Possibility from this potion, you can't gain another Fragment of Possibility from any source.
Potion of Possibility.name: Potion of Possibility
Potion of Psionic Fortitude.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you have advantage for 1 hour on saving throws you make to avoid or end the charmed or stunned condition on yourself.
Potion of Psionic Fortitude.entries.1: This black potion swirls with shimmering flecks of pink and purple.
Potion of Psionic Fortitude.name: Potion of Psionic Fortitude
Potion of Psychic Resistance.name: Potion of Psychic Resistance
Potion of Pugilism.entries.0: After you drink this potion, each {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} you make deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Force damage on a hit. This effect lasts 10 minutes.
Potion of Pugilism.entries.1: This potion is a thick green fluid that tastes like spinach.
Potion of Pugilism.name: Potion of Pugilism
Potion of Radiant Resistance.name: Potion of Radiant Resistance
Potion of Speed.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the {@spell Haste|XPHB} spell for 1 minute (no {@status Concentration|XPHB} required) without suffering the wave of lethargy that typically occurs when the effect ends.
Potion of Speed.entries.1: This potion's yellow fluid is streaked with black and swirls on its own.
Potion of Speed.name: Potion of Speed
Potion of Stone Giant Strength.entries.0: When you drink this potion, your Strength score changes to 23 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect on you if your Strength is equal to or greater than that score.
Potion of Stone Giant Strength.entries.1: This potion's transparent liquid has floating in it a sliver of fingernail from a stone giant.
Potion of Stone Giant Strength.name: Potion of Stone Giant Strength
Potion of Storm Giant Strength.entries.0: When you drink this potion, your Strength score changes to 29 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect on you if your Strength is equal to or greater than that score.
Potion of Storm Giant Strength.entries.1: This potion's transparent liquid has floating in it a sliver of fingernail from a storm giant.
Potion of Storm Giant Strength.name: Potion of Storm Giant Strength
Potion of Superior Healing.entries.0: You regain {@dice 8d4 + 8} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} when you drink this potion. The potion's red liquid glimmers when agitated.
Potion of Superior Healing.name: Potion of Superior Healing
Potion of Supreme Healing.entries.0: You regain {@dice 10d4 + 20} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} when you drink this potion. The potion's red liquid glimmers when agitated.
Potion of Supreme Healing.name: Potion of Supreme Healing
Potion of Thunder Resistance.name: Potion of Thunder Resistance
Potion of Vitality.entries.0: When you drink this potion, it removes any {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels you have and ends the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition on you. For the next 24 hours, you regain the maximum number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} for any {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} you spend.
Potion of Vitality.entries.1: This potion's crimson liquid regularly pulses with dull light, calling to mind a heartbeat.
Potion of Vitality.name: Potion of Vitality
Potion of Watchful Rest.entries.0: When you drink this potion, you gain the following benefits for the next 8 hours: magic can't put you to sleep, and you can remain awake during a long rest and still gain its benefits. This sweet, amber-colored brew has no effect on creatures that don't require sleep, such as elves.
Potion of Watchful Rest.name: Potion of Watchful Rest
Potion of Water Breathing.entries.0: You can breathe underwater for 24 hours after drinking this potion.
Potion of Water Breathing.entries.1: This potion's cloudy green fluid smells of the sea and has a jellyfish-like bubble floating in it.
Potion of Water Breathing.name: Potion of Water Breathing
Potters Tools.entries.0.items.0.name: Характеристика:
Discern what a ceramic object held in the past 24 hours ({@dc 15})
Potters Tools.entries.0.items.1.name: Использование:
Potters Tools.entries.0.items.2.name: Craft:
Potters Tools.name: Potter's Tools
Pouch.entries.0: A Pouch holds up to 6 pounds within one-fifth of a cubic foot.
Pouch.name: Pouch
Powered Armor.entries.0: This suit of technologically advanced plate armor includes an under-suit that can fully seal, a helmet with a full face mask and crystal lenses in the eyeholes, and a set of gauntlets. The armor is powered by an {@item energy cell} stored in a compartment on the thigh plate.
Powered Armor.entries.1: Placing a full {@item energy cell} in the armor gives the armor 24 charges. A suit of powered armor functions as a suit of normal plate armor, even when it has 0 charges remaining.
Powered Armor.entries.2.entries.0: As an action, you can expend any number of the armor's charges to activate it; the armor remains active for 1 hour per charge expended. You can use a bonus action to deactivate the armor early, but doing so doesn't recover any expended charges.
Powered Armor.entries.2.entries.1: While the armor is active, you gain the following benefits:
Powered Armor.entries.2.name: Activating the Armor
Powered Armor.entries.3.entries.0: You have advantage on Strength checks, and your carrying capacity is doubled.
Powered Armor.entries.3.name: Augmented Physicality
Powered Armor.entries.4.entries.0: The armor seals airtight and provides its own atmosphere. You can breathe normally in any environment and withstand extreme temperatures, and you're unaffected by harmful gases, as well as contact and inhaled poisons.
Powered Armor.entries.4.name: Environmental Adaptation
Powered Armor.entries.5.entries.0: When you would take damage, you can use your reaction to expend 1 of the armor's charges to deploy a defensive force field. Roll {@dice 3d10} and reduce the damage taken by the total rolled.
Powered Armor.entries.5.name: Force Field
Powered Armor.entries.6.entries.0: As a bonus action, you can expend 1 of the armor's charges to gain a flying speed equal to your walking speed for 1 minute. If you're airborne when this duration ends, you fall.
Powered Armor.entries.6.name: Propulsion
Powered Armor.entries.7.entries.0: While the armor has charges remaining, its {@item energy cell} can't be removed. Once the armor has 0 charges, you can replace the {@item energy cell} with a new cell by using an action or a bonus action.
Powered Armor.entries.7.name: Replacing the Energy Cell
Powered Armor.name: Powered Armor
Prehistoric Figurine of Wondrous Power,: Larger and more roughly hewn than typical figurines of wondrous power, these statuettes depict dinosaurs and related creatures from the earliest days of the world.

As an action, you can throw a prehistoric figurine of wondrous power to a point on the ground within 60 feet of yourself while speaking a command word, whereupon the figurine magically transforms into a living creature. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is friendly to you and your companions. It understands your languages and obeys your spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions. See the Monster Manual for the creature's statistics.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its statuette form. It reverts to a figurine early if it drops to 0 hit points or if you use an action to speak the command word again while touching it. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified in the figurine's description.
This figurine becomes a {@creature triceratops} for up to 6 hours and can be ridden as a mount. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 10 days have passed.
{@creature triceratops||Carnelian Triceratops}
Prehistoric Figurine of Wondrous Power, Carnelian Triceratops
Larger and more roughly hewn than typical figurines of wondrous power, these statuettes depict dinosaurs and related creatures from the earliest days of the world.

As an action, you can throw a prehistoric figurine of wondrous power to a point on the ground within 60 feet of yourself while speaking a command word, whereupon the figurine magically transforms into a living creature. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is friendly to you and your companions. It understands your languages and obeys your spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions. See the Monster Manual for the creature's statistics.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its statuette form. It reverts to a figurine early if it drops to 0 hit points or if you use an action to speak the command word again while touching it. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified in the figurine's description.
This figurine, crafted from rare green jasper, becomes a {@creature tyrannosaurus rex} for up to 1 hour. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 14 days have passed.

Whenever you command the figurine while it's in tyrannosaurus rex form (including commanding it to revert to figurine form), you must roll a {@dice d20}. On a 1, you lose control of the figurine, and it becomes hostile to you and your companions until it is reduced to 0 hit points, at which point it reverts to figurine form.
{@creature tyrannosaurus rex||Jasper Tyrannosaurus Rex}
Prehistoric Figurine of Wondrous Power, Jasper Tyrannosaurus Rex
Larger and more roughly hewn than typical figurines of wondrous power, these statuettes depict dinosaurs and related creatures from the earliest days of the world.

As an action, you can throw a prehistoric figurine of wondrous power to a point on the ground within 60 feet of yourself while speaking a command word, whereupon the figurine magically transforms into a living creature. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is friendly to you and your companions. It understands your languages and obeys your spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions. See the Monster Manual for the creature's statistics.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its statuette form. It reverts to a figurine early if it drops to 0 hit points or if you use an action to speak the command word again while touching it. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified in the figurine's description.

This figurine becomes a {@creature pteranodon} for up to 8 hours. If your size is Medium or smaller, you can ride the pteranodon as a mount. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed.
{@creature pteranodon||Kyanite Pteranodon}
Prehistoric Figurine of Wondrous Power, Kyanite Pteranodon
Larger and more roughly hewn than typical figurines of wondrous power, these statuettes depict dinosaurs and related creatures from the earliest days of the world.

As an action, you can throw a prehistoric figurine of wondrous power to a point on the ground within 60 feet of yourself while speaking a command word, whereupon the figurine magically transforms into a living creature. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is friendly to you and your companions. It understands your languages and obeys your spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions. See the Monster Manual for the creature's statistics.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its statuette form. It reverts to a figurine early if it drops to 0 hit points or if you use an action to speak the command word again while touching it. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified in the figurine's description.
This figurine becomes a {@creature plesiosaurus} for up to 12 hours and can be ridden as a mount. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 4 days have passed.
While you are riding the plesiosaurus, you can use it to cast {@spell water breathing} at will.
{@creature plesiosaurus||Pyrite Plesiosaurus}
Prehistoric Figurine of Wondrous Power, Pyrite Plesiosaurus
Prespra.entries.0: A creature subjected to Prespra must succeed on a {@dc 11} Constitution saving throw or take 3 ({@dice 1d6}) Poison damage and have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition. The creature also has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition while {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} in this way. The creature repeats the save at the start of each of its turns, taking 3 ({@dice 1d6}) Poison damage on a failed save. On a successful save, the effect ends for the creature.
Prespra.name: Prespra
Pressure Capsule.entries.0: This small capsule is made of beeswax blended with sand and a variety of enchanted water plants. A creature who consumes a pressure capsule ignores the effects of swimming at depths greater than 100 feet (see "{@book Unusual Environments|dmg|5|Unusual Environments}" in chapter 5 of the Dungeon Master's Guide).
Pressure Capsule.name: Pressure Capsule
Pride Silk Outfit.entries.0: An outfit made of pride silk weighs 4 pounds and costs 500 gp. If you aren't wearing armor, your base Armor Class is 11 + your Dexterity modifier while wearing it.
Pride Silk Outfit.name: Pride Silk Outfit
Pride Silk.entries.0: The hearty silk unique to the region has long been a point of pride with the locals. When processed and woven properly, the resulting cloth is pride silk: an extremely flexible material with the durability of tanned leather. Pride silk has become a favorite cloth for use on military ship sails, war banners, and other utilitarian applications, but has also gained popularity in affluent circles. High-end clothing made from pride silk is both fashionable and protective, leading many nobles and wealthy merchants to seek full outfits and formal gowns made with it. The tailors skilled enough to create such outfits are few and far between, so while pride silk might be expensive as a raw material, outfits made of the cloth are prohibitively expensive for most common folk.
Pride Silk.name: Pride Silk
Priests Pack.entries.0: A Priest's Pack contains the following items: {@item Backpack|XPHB}, {@item Blanket|XPHB}, {@item Holy Water|XPHB}, {@item Lamp|XPHB}, 7 days of {@item Rations|XPHB}, {@item Robe|XPHB}, and {@item Tinderbox|XPHB}.
Priests Pack.name: Priest's Pack
Primal Amulet.entries.0: This beaded jade necklace bears an ancient symbol of the River Heralds. When worn, the primal amulet allows its wearer to cast {@spell speak with animals}, {@spell locate object}, and {@spell pass without trace}. Once the amulet has been used to cast a spell, it can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn.
Primal Amulet.name: Primal Amulet
Prismari Primer.entries.0: The Prismari Primer is a magic textbook created at Strixhaven's Prismari College. The primer has 3 charges, and it regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. If you make a Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) or a Charisma ({@skill Performance}) check while holding the primer, you can expend 1 charge to give yourself a {@dice 1d4} bonus to the check, immediately after you roll the {@dice d20}.
Prismari Primer.entries.1: In addition, if you study the primer at the end of a long rest, you can choose one 1st-level spell from the bard or sorcerer spell list. Before you finish your next long rest, you can cast the chosen spell once without a spell slot if you are holding the primer. Your spellcasting ability for this spell is your choice of Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma.
Prismari Primer.name: Prismari Primer
Prismari Trinket.entries.0: When you make your character, you may roll once on the Prismari Trinkets table, instead of on the {@item trinket|phb|Trinkets table} in the {@book Player's Handbook|PHB}, for your starting trinket.
Prismari Trinket.entries.1.caption: Prismari Trinkets
Prismari Trinket.entries.1.rows.0.1: A pair of rose-tinted glasses with glittery frames
Prismari Trinket.entries.1.rows.1.1: A stoppered glass bottle that, when opened, plays a brassy orchestral piece
Prismari Trinket.entries.1.rows.2.1: A quartet of hovering water motes in a vial
Prismari Trinket.entries.1.rows.4.1: A tiara capped with a crystal that crackles with harmless lightning
Prismari Trinket.name: Prismari Trinket
Prismatic Well.entries.0: This well enhances the colors of the city of Harmony and invigorates the population. When the well is at full power, all good-aligned creatures that see the colors experience feelings of happiness and have advantage on all Charisma checks.
Prismatic Well.name: Prismatic Well
Professor Orb.entries.0: Each professor orb takes the form of a smooth, solid, 5-pound sphere of smoky gray quartz about the size of a grapefruit. Close examination reveals two or more pinpricks of silver light deep inside the sphere.
Professor Orb.entries.1: A Professor Orb is sentient and has the personality of a scholar. Its alignment is determined by rolling on the alignment table in the "{@book Sentient Magic Items|DMG|7|Sentient Magic Items}" section in chapter 7 of the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|dmg}. Regardless of its disposition, the orb has an Intelligence of 18, and Wisdom and Charisma scores determined by rolling {@dice 3d6} for each ability. The orb speaks, reads, and understands four languages, and can see and hear normally out to a range of 60 feet. Unlike most sentient items, the orb has no will of its own and can't initiate a conflict with the creature in possession of it.
Professor Orb.entries.2: A Professor Orb has extensive knowledge of four narrow academic subjects. When making an Intelligence check to recall lore from any of its areas of expertise, the orb has a +9 bonus to its roll (including its Intelligence modifier).
Professor Orb.entries.3: In addition to the knowledge it possesses, a professor orb can cast the {@spell mage hand} cantrip at will. It uses the spell only to transport itself. Its spellcasting ability is Intelligence.
Professor Orb.name: Professor Orb
Professor Skant.entries.0: The {@item professor orb|WDMM} owned by Vellynne Harpell and stolen by Nass Lantomir calls itself Professor Skant. It is lawful good, and it has a Wisdom of 11 and a Charisma of 9 {@note (as a professor orb, it has an Intelligence of 18)}. It speaks and reads Common, Draconic, Elvish, and Loross (the dead language of the Empire of Netheril). Professor Skant is a chatterbox and assumes all humanoids are dunderheads. When elaborating on its areas of expertise, it adopts an unintentionally patronizing tone. It has the following four areas of expertise:
Professor Skant.entries.1.items.0: The history of Netheril (see the "{@adventure Fate of Netheril|IDRotF|17|Fate of Netheril}" sidebar)
Professor Skant.entries.1.items.1: Vampirism and the traits of vampires
Professor Skant.entries.1.items.2: Rituals surrounding the making, bottling, and drinking of Elverquisst (a rare, ruby-colored elven liquor distilled from sunshine and rare summer fruits)
Professor Skant.entries.2: When making an Intelligence check to recall lore from any of its areas of expertise, the orb has a +9 bonus to its roll (including its Intelligence modifier).
Professor Skant.entries.3: Professor Skant takes the form of a smooth, solid, 5-pound sphere of smoky gray quartz about the size of a grapefruit. Close examination reveals two or more pinpricks of silver light deep inside the sphere.
Professor Skant.entries.4: Professor Skant can see and hear normally out to a range of 60 feet. Unlike most sentient items, the orb has no will of its own and can't initiate a conflict with the creature in possession of it.
Professor Skant.entries.5: In addition to the knowledge it possesses, Professor Skant can cast the {@spell mage hand} cantrip at will. It uses the spell only to transport itself. Its spellcasting ability is Intelligence.
Professor Skant.name: Professor Skant
Propeller Helm.entries.0: While worn, the helm allows its wearer to use an action to cast the {@spell levitate} spell, requiring no components. The helm's propeller spins and whirs loudly until the spell ends. Each time the spell ends, there is a {@chance 50} chance that the helm loses its magic and becomes nonmagical.
Propeller Helm.name: Propeller Helm
Prosthetic Limb.entries.0: This magic item replaces a lost limb—a hand, an arm, a foot, a leg, or a similar body part. While the prosthetic is attached, it functions identically to the part it replaces. You can detach or reattach it as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, and it can't be removed against your will while you are alive.
Prosthetic Limb.name: Prosthetic Limb
Protective Verses.entries.0: This leather-bound spellbook is reinforced with iron and silver fittings and an iron lock ({@dc 20} to open). As an action, you can touch the book's cover and cause it to lock as if you cast {@spell arcane lock} on it. When found, the book contains the following spells: {@spell arcane lock}, {@spell dispel magic}, {@spell globe of invulnerability}, {@spell glyph of warding}, {@spell Mordenkainen's private sanctum}, {@spell protection from evil and good||protection from evil}, and {@spell symbol}. It functions as a spellbook for you.
Protective Verses.entries.1: While you are holding the book, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your wizard spells.
Protective Verses.entries.2: The book has 3 charges, and it regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. You can use the charges in the following ways while holding it:
Protective Verses.entries.3.items.0: If you spend 1 minute studying the book, you can expend 1 charge to replace one of your prepared wizard spells with a different spell in the book. The new spell must be of the abjuration school.
Protective Verses.entries.3.items.1: When you cast an abjuration spell, you can expend 1 charge to grant a creature you can see within 30 feet of you {@dice 2d10} temporary hit points.
Protective Verses.name: Protective Verses
Prying Blade.entries.0: A hooked blade useful on board a ship or as a cutting tool in the wilderness, this {@item +1 shortsword} grants its wielder advantage on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks to climb or to escape while {@condition restrained}.
Prying Blade.name: Prying Blade
Psi Crystal.entries.0: This crystal grants you telepathy for as long as you remain attuned to it. See the {@book introduction of the Monster Manual|MM|0|Telepathy} for rules on how this telepathy works.
Psi Crystal.entries.1: The crystal also glows with a purplish inner light while you are attuned to it.
Psi Crystal.entries.2: The higher your intelligence, the greater the light's intensity and the greater the range of the telepathy (see the Psi Crystal Properties table).
Psi Crystal.entries.3.caption: Psi Crystal Properties
Psi Crystal.entries.3.rows.0.2: Dim light out to a range of 5 feet
Psi Crystal.entries.3.rows.1.2: Bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet
Psi Crystal.entries.3.rows.2.2: Bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet
Psi Crystal.entries.3.rows.3.2: Bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet
Psi Crystal.name: Psi Crystal
Psychic Absorbing Tattoo.name: Psychic Absorbing Tattoo
Psychic Blade.name: Psychic Blade
Purple Dragon Knight Trinket.entries.0.r: A common purple quartz cut to resemble a dragon's eye
A signal whistle audible only to amethyst dragons
A robin's egg encased in amber
A whetstone that leaves any blade it hones faintly glittery
A vamplate taken from a slain rival jouster
Purple Dragon Knight Trinket.name: Purple Dragon Knight Trinket
Purple Worm Poison.entries.0: A creature subjected to Purple Worm Poison makes a {@dc 21} Constitution saving throw, taking 35 ({@damage 10d6}) Poison damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
Purple Worm Poison.name: Purple Worm Poison
Puzzle Card.entries.0: As an action, you can hold this card aloft and magically disable each trap within 60 feet of you that isn't behind {@quickref cover||3||total cover}. A trap, for the purpose of this property, includes any object or mechanism that would inflict a sudden effect that was intended by its maker to cause harm or inconvenience. If the trap is the effect of a spell, such as {@spell Alarm}, that trap is suppressed for 10 minutes instead of being disabled. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
Puzzle Card.name: Puzzle Card
Pyroconverger.entries.0: A Pyroconverger is an Izzet-made flamethrower. It carries a risk of malfunction each time you use it.
Pyroconverger.entries.1: As an action, you can cause the Pyroconverger to project fire in a 10-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
Pyroconverger.entries.2: Each time you use the Pyroconverger, roll a {@dice d10} and add the number of times you have used it since your last long rest. If the total is 11 or higher, the Pyroconverger malfunctions: you take {@damage 4d6} fire damage, and you can't use the Pyroconverger again until you finish a long rest.
Pyroconverger.name: Pyroconverger
Pyxis of Pandemonium.entries.0: A creature that touches this ornate wooden vessel for 1 minute gains the benefits of a short rest. That creature also gains the effects of the {@spell bless} spell until the creature finishes a short or long rest. The creature can't gain these benefits again until it finishes a long rest.
Pyxis of Pandemonium.entries.1: If the vessel is opened, roll on the Pyxis of Pandemonium table to determine what happens. Any spells cast by the vessel have a spell save DC of 17. One minute after the vessel is opened, it disappears. It reappears, sealed, in a random location on the same plane of existence 24 hours later.
Pyxis of Pandemonium.entries.2.entries.0: Any creature that gains the benefit of a short rest from the vessel hears cloying telepathic whispers emanating from it. That creature must make a {@dc 17} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is {@condition charmed} by the vessel for 1 hour. The {@condition charmed} creature does everything it can to open the vessel as soon as possible. On a successful save, the creature is immune to the vessel's whispers for 24 hours.
Pyxis of Pandemonium.entries.2.name: Проклятие
Pyxis of Pandemonium.entries.3.rows.0.1: {@b Androphagia.} Each creature within 60 feet of the vessel must succeed on a {@dc 17} Wisdom saving throw or go berserk for 1 minute. The berserk creature must begin its turn using the {@action Attack} action to make one melee or ranged attack (its choice) against the creature nearest to it. The berserk creature can repeat the save at the end of its turn, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Pyxis of Pandemonium.entries.3.rows.1.1: {@b Bile Blight.} The vessel casts the {@spell harm} spell on each creature within 30 feet of it.
Pyxis of Pandemonium.entries.3.rows.2.1: {@b Flood.} The vessel casts the {@spell tsunami} spell at a point of the DM's choice within 120 feet of it.
Pyxis of Pandemonium.entries.3.rows.3.1: {@b Medusa's Gaze.} The vessel casts the {@spell flesh to stone} spell on each creature within 30 feet of it.
Pyxis of Pandemonium.entries.3.rows.4.1: {@b Labyrinth.} The vessel casts the {@spell maze} spell on each creature within 30 feet of it.
Pyxis of Pandemonium.entries.3.rows.5.1: {@b Nightmare.} Tendrils of shadow seep from the vessel and form into {@dice 1d4} {@creature shadow demon||shadow demons} (see the Monster Manual for their stat block), which appear in unoccupied spaces within 30 feet of it and are hostile.
Pyxis of Pandemonium.entries.3.rows.6.1: {@b Swarming Insects.} The vessel casts the {@spell insect plague} spell, centered on itself and with a radius of 30 feet.
Pyxis of Pandemonium.entries.3.rows.7.1: {@b Unbridled Revel.} The vessel casts the {@spell Otto's irresistible dance} spell on each creature within 30 feet of it.
Pyxis of Pandemonium.name: Pyxis of Pandemonium
Quaals Feather Token, Anchor.entries.0: This object looks like a feather.
Quaals Feather Token, Anchor.entries.1: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to touch the token to a boat or ship. For the next 24 hours, the vessel can't be moved by any means. Touching the token to the vessel again ends the effect. When the effect ends, the token disappears.
Quaals Feather Token, Anchor.name: Quaal's Feather Token, Anchor
Quaals Feather Token, Bird.entries.0: This object looks like a feather.
Quaals Feather Token, Bird.entries.1: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to toss the token 5 feet into the air. The token disappears and an enormous, multicolored bird takes its place. The bird has the statistics of a {@creature Roc|XMM}, but it can't attack. It obeys your simple commands and can carry up to 500 pounds while flying at its maximum speed (16 miles per hour for a maximum of 144 miles per day, with a 1-hour rest for every 3 hours of flying) or 1,000 pounds at half that speed. The bird disappears after flying its maximum distance for a day or if it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. You can dismiss the bird as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action.
Quaals Feather Token, Bird.name: Quaal's Feather Token, Bird
Quaals Feather Token, Fan.entries.0: This object looks like a feather.
Quaals Feather Token, Fan.entries.1: If you are on a boat or ship, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to toss the token up to 10 feet in the air. The token disappears, and a giant flapping fan takes its place. The fan floats and creates a strong wind. This wind can fill the sails of one ship, increasing its speed by 5 miles per hour for 8 hours. You can dismiss the fan as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action.
Quaals Feather Token, Fan.name: Quaal's Feather Token, Fan
This object looks like a feather.

You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to touch the token to a body of water at least 60 feet in diameter. The token disappears, and a 50-foot-long, 20-foot-wide boat shaped like a swan takes its place. The boat is self-propelled and moves across water at a speed of 6 miles per hour. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while on the boat to command it to move or to turn up to 90 degrees. The boat remains for 24 hours and then disappears. You can dismiss the boat as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action.
Quaals Feather Token, Swan Boat.name: Quaal's Feather Token, Swan Boat
Quaals Feather Token, Tree.entries.0: This object looks like a feather.
Quaals Feather Token, Tree.entries.1: You must be outdoors to use this token. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to touch it to an unoccupied space on the ground. The token disappears, and in its place a nonmagical oak tree springs into existence. The tree is 60 feet tall and has a 5-foot-diameter trunk, and its branches at the top spread out in a 20-foot radius.
Quaals Feather Token, Tree.name: Quaal's Feather Token, Tree
Quaals Feather Token, Whip.entries.0: This object looks like a feather.
Quaals Feather Token, Whip.entries.1: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to throw the token to a point within 10 feet of yourself. The token disappears, and a floating whip takes its place. You can then take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make a melee spell attack against a creature within 10 feet of the whip, with an attack bonus of +9. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d6 + 5} Force damage.
Quaals Feather Token, Whip.entries.2: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can direct the whip to fly up to 20 feet and repeat the attack against a creature within 10 feet of the whip. The whip disappears after 1 hour, when you take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to dismiss it, or when you die or have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.
Quaals Feather Token, Whip.name: Quaal's Feather Token, Whip
Quandrix Primer.entries.0: The Quandrix Primer is a magic textbook created at Strixhaven's Quandrix College. The primer has 3 charges, and it regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. If you make an Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) or an Intelligence ({@skill Nature}) check while holding the primer, you can expend 1 charge to give yourself a {@dice 1d4} bonus to the check, immediately after you roll the {@dice d20}.
Quandrix Primer.entries.1: In addition, if you study the primer at the end of a long rest, you can choose one 1st-level spell from the druid or wizard spell list. Before you finish your next long rest, you can cast the chosen spell once without a spell slot if you are holding the primer. Your spellcasting ability for this spell is your choice of Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma.
Quandrix Primer.name: Quandrix Primer
Quandrix Trinket.entries.0: When you make your character, you may roll once on the Quandrix Trinkets table, instead of on the {@item trinket|phb|Trinkets table} in the {@book Player's Handbook|PHB}, for your starting trinket.
Quandrix Trinket.entries.1.caption: Quandrix Trinkets
Quandrix Trinket.entries.1.rows.0.1: A small succulent in a dodecahedral clay pot
Quandrix Trinket.entries.1.rows.1.1: A blue knit hat that looks a bit like a bottle folding in on itself
Quandrix Trinket.entries.1.rows.2.1: A model hypercube carved from green crystal, showcasing the fourth dimension
Quandrix Trinket.entries.1.rows.3.1: A crumpled test on the theory of gravity manipulation, with a failing grade on the front and the name of a famous Quandrix professor
Quandrix Trinket.entries.1.rows.4.1: A blue tetrahedron that, when tapped twice, projects a recording of an old mathematics lecture
Quandrix Trinket.entries.1.rows.5.1: A round bread roll cut so that someone could spread butter on both halves without ever lifting the knife
Quandrix Trinket.name: Quandrix Trinket
Quarterstaff of the Acrobat.entries.0: You have a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Quarterstaff of the Acrobat.entries.1: While holding this weapon, you can cause it to emit green {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} out to 10 feet, either as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} or after you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, or you can extinguish the light as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Quarterstaff of the Acrobat.entries.2: While holding this weapon, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to alter its form, turning it into a 6-inch rod (for ease of storage) or a 10-foot pole, or reverting it a Quarterstaff; the weapon will elongate only as far as the surrounding space allows.
Quarterstaff of the Acrobat.entries.3: In certain forms, the weapon has the following additional properties.
Quarterstaff of the Acrobat.entries.4.en: While holding this weapon, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) checks.
Quarterstaff of the Acrobat.entries.4.na: Acrobatic Assist (Quarterstaff and 10-Foot Pole Forms Only)
Quarterstaff of the Acrobat.entries.5.en: When you are hit by an attack while holding the weapon, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to twirl the weapon around you, gaining a +5 bonus to your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} against the triggering attack, potentially causing the attack to miss you. You can't use this property again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Quarterstaff of the Acrobat.entries.6.en: This weapon has {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} with a normal range of 30 feet and a long range of 120 feet. Immediately after you make a ranged attack with the weapon, it flies back to your hand.
Quarterstaff of the Acrobat.name: Quarterstaff of the Acrobat
Quartz.entries.0: A white, smoky gray, or yellow gemstone.
Quartz.name: Quartz
Quiver of Ehlonna.entries.0: Each of the quiver's three compartments connects to an extradimensional space that allows the quiver to hold numerous items while never weighing more than 2 pounds. The shortest compartment can hold up to 60 {@item Arrow|XPHB|Arrows}, {@item Bolt|XPHB|Bolts}, or similar objects. The midsize compartment holds up to 18 {@item Javelin|XPHB|Javelins} or similar objects. The longest compartment holds up to 6 long objects, such as bows, {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB|Quarterstaff's}, or {@item Spear|XPHB|Spears}.
Quiver of Ehlonna.entries.1: You can draw any item the quiver contains as if doing so from a regular quiver or scabbard.
Quiver of Ehlonna.name: Quiver of Ehlonna
Quiver.entries.0: A Quiver holds up to 20 Arrows.
Quiver.name: Quiver
Radiance.entries.0: While holding this wand, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls. In addition, you ignore {@quickref Cover||3||half cover} when making a spell attack. Radiance is in the form of an exquisite golden hand mirror.
Radiance.entries.1: While surrounded by darkness, it sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. A creature that is attuned to Radiance can use a bonus action while holding the mirror to cast the {@spell enhance ability} spell, choosing itself and no other creature as the spell's target. Once this property of the wand is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
Radiance.name: Radiance
Radiant Absorbing Tattoo.name: Radiant Absorbing Tattoo
Rain Catcher.entries.0: A rain catcher is a simple contraption consisting of a 5-foot-square leather tarp and a wooden frame with legs. When the tarp is stretched across the wooden frame, it forms a basin that can catch 2 gallons of drinking water per inch of rainfall and hold up to 8 gallons. The tarp and wooden frame fold up for easy transport.
Rain Catcher.name: Rain Catcher
Rakdos Guild Signet.entries.0: This ring, adorned with the symbol of Rakdos, allows you to cast {@spell hellish rebuke}. A guild signet is sometimes awarded to a guild member whose renown score in that guild is 5 or higher, as a reward for performing special services for the guild. Aside from its magical properties, the ring is also an indicator of Rakdos' recognition and favor.
Rakdos Guild Signet.entries.1: A signet has 3 charges, and it regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing it, you can expend 1 charge to cast the associated spell (save {@dc 13}).
Rakdos Guild Signet.name: Rakdos Guild Signet
Rakdos Keyrune.entries.0: This dark granite keyrune is marbled with scarlet veins and carved with the leering visage of a mischievous demon. When activated, it transforms into a {@creature cackler|GGR} for up to 1 hour.
Rakdos Keyrune.entries.1: When you use an action to speak the item's command word and place the keyrune on the ground in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you, the keyrune transforms into a {@creature cackler|GGR}. If there isn't enough space for the creature, the keyrune doesn't transform.
Rakdos Keyrune.entries.2: The creature is friendly to you, your companions, and other members of your guild (unless those guild members are hostile to you). It understands your languages and obeys your spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the creature takes the {@action Dodge} action and moves to avoid danger.
Rakdos Keyrune.entries.3: At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its keyrune form. It reverts early if it drops to 0 hit points or if you use an action to speak the command word again while touching it. When the creature reverts to its keyrune form, it can't transform again until 36 hours have passed.
Rakdos Keyrune.name: Rakdos Keyrune
Rakdos Riteknife.entries.0: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Its blade is cruelly serrated, and its hilt resembles a demonic head and wings. Whenever you slay a creature with an attack using the dagger, the creature's soul is imprisoned inside the dagger, and that creature can be restored to life only by a {@spell wish} spell. The dagger can hold a maximum of five souls.
Rakdos Riteknife.entries.1: For each soul imprisoned in the dagger, your attacks with it deal an extra {@damage 1d4} necrotic damage on a hit. While the dagger is within 5 feet of you, your dreams are haunted by whispers from the trapped souls.
Rakdos Riteknife.entries.2: The dagger has the following additional properties.
Rakdos Riteknife.entries.3.entries.0: As a bonus action, you can release any number of stored souls from the dagger to regain {@dice 1d10} hit points per soul released.
Rakdos Riteknife.entries.3.name: Siphon Vitality
Rakdos Riteknife.entries.4.entries.0: If the dagger holds five souls, you can use this property: As a reaction immediately after you hit a creature with the dagger and deal damage to that target, you can release all five souls. If the target now has fewer than 75 hit points, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or die. If the target dies, you can't use this property again until you finish a long rest.
Rakdos Riteknife.entries.4.name: Annihilation
Rakdos Riteknife.name: Rakdos Riteknife
Ranseur of Torture.entries.0: Forged of infernal iron and Hellfire through the help of Dispater, the Ranseur of Torture has been Belial's weapon of choice since he first became an archdevil.
Ranseur of Torture.entries.1: It is so directly tied to him that one of his worshipped symbols is a two-pronged fork. The weapon never leaves his side, and he is a master at wielding it.
Ranseur of Torture.entries.2: The Ranseur of Torture is a magic pike. You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon, and it does {@damage 2d10} piercing damage instead of its regular damage. When you hit a good aligned creature with it, that creature takes an extra {@damage 1d10} necrotic damage.
Ranseur of Torture.entries.3.entries.0: The Ranseur of Torture has the following random properties:
Ranseur of Torture.entries.3.entries.1.i: 1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Beneficial Properties|dmg|minor beneficial} property
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Major Beneficial Properties|dmg|major beneficial} property
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|dmg|minor detrimental} property
Ranseur of Torture.entries.3.name: Random Properties
Ranseur of Torture.entries.4.entries.0: Damage dealt by the Ranseur of Torture is particularly dangerous, as it can only be restored through magical means. Resting, food, and medicine won't restore hit points removed by the pike. If the damage goes untreated for 7 days, the missing hit points are permanently removed, and can be recovered only through casting a {@spell Regenerate} or {@spell Wish} spell on the victim.
Ranseur of Torture.entries.4.name: Wounding
Ranseur of Torture.entries.5.entries.0: If a creature with less than its full hit points is hit by this pike, the Ranseur of Torture deals {@dice 1d10} additional piercing damage. If a creature is killed by the pike, their soul is sent to Phlegethos and can be restored only through casting a {@spell Wish} spell on the victim.
Ranseur of Torture.entries.5.name: Execution
Ranseur of Torture.entries.6.entries.0: Each time a creature takes damage from the Ranseur of Torture, they must make a Constitution save, with a DC equaling the damage they took. On a failed save, their movement speed is reduced by 5 feet until the end of their next turn. This penalty stacks, though movement speed can't be reduced below 0.
Ranseur of Torture.entries.6.name: Staggered
Ranseur of Torture.entries.7.entries.0: Spellcasters damaged by the Ranseur of Torture have disadvantage on Constitution checks made to maintain {@status concentration} until the damage is healed. If a creature is {@status concentration||concentrating} on a spell when damaged by the ranseur, their {@status concentration} is immediately ended.
Ranseur of Torture.entries.7.name: Interrupting
Ranseur of Torture.entries.8.entries.0: Only through the written consent of both Dispater and Belial can the Ranseur of Torture be destroyed. If either or both of the archdevils are dead, then the new archdevil to take their mantle must give permission in their stead. The two writs must be wrapped around each of the prongs of the weapon, then the weapon must be tossed into the Pit of Flame. If performed successfully, the Hellfire briefly burns a different hue.
Ranseur of Torture.entries.8.name: Destroying the Ranseur
Ranseur of Torture.name: Ranseur of Torture
Rations.entries.0: Rations consist of travel-ready food, including jerky, dried fruit, hardtack, and nuts. See "{@hazard Malnutrition|XPHB}" for the risks of not eating.
Rations.name: Rations
Reapers Scream.entries.0: The spikes of this iron morningstar glow with sickly, pale light. The death rune is inscribed on its shaft and inlaid with pearl.
Reapers Scream.entries.1: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon, and attacks with this weapon deal necrotic damage instead of piercing damage.
Reapers Scream.entries.2: When you attack a creature with this weapon and roll a 20 on the attack roll, you gain 10 temporary hit points. Any creature that hits you with a melee attack while you have 1 or more of these temporary hit points takes 10 necrotic damage.
Reapers Scream.entries.3.entries.0: As a bonus action, you can invoke the weapon's rune, unleashing the screams of every creature slain by the weapon in one cacophonous burst. Each creature of your choice within 60 feet of you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or have the stunned condition until the start of your next turn.
Reapers Scream.entries.3.entries.1: Once the rune has been invoked, it can't be invoked again until the next dawn.
Reapers Scream.entries.3.name: Invoking the Rune
Reapers Scream.name: Reaper's Scream
Red Chromatic Rose.name: Red Chromatic Rose
Red Dragon Mask.entries.0: This mask of glossy crimson has swept-back horns and spiked cheek ridges. The mask reshapes to fit a wearer attuned to it. While you are wearing the mask and attuned to it, you can access the following properties.
Red Dragon Mask.entries.1.entries.0: You have resistance against fire damage. If you already have resistance to fire damage from another source, you instead have immunity to fire damage. If you already have immunity to fire damage from another source, whenever you are subjected to fire damage, you take none of that damage and regain a number of hit points equal to half the damage dealt of that type.
Red Dragon Mask.entries.1.name: Damage Absorption
Red Dragon Mask.entries.2.entries.0: While you are wearing no armor, you can add your Charisma bonus to your Armor Class.
Red Dragon Mask.entries.2.name: Draconic Majesty
Red Dragon Mask.entries.3.entries.0: If you have a breath weapon that requires rest to recharge, it gains a recharge of 6.
Red Dragon Mask.entries.3.name: Dragon Breath
Red Dragon Mask.entries.4.entries.0: You gain {@sense darkvision} out to 60 feet, or to an additional 60 feet if you already have that sense. Once per day, you can gain {@sense blindsight} out to 30 feet for 5 minutes.
Red Dragon Mask.entries.4.name: Dragon Sight
Red Dragon Mask.entries.5.entries.0: You can speak and understand Draconic. You also have advantage on any Charisma check you make against Red Dragons.
Red Dragon Mask.entries.5.name: Dragon Tongue
Red Dragon Mask.entries.6.entries.0: (1/Day) If you fail a saving throw, you can choose to succeed instead.
Red Dragon Mask.entries.6.name: Legendary Resistance
Red Dragon Mask.entries.7.entries.0: If you deal fire damage to a creature or flammable object, it starts burning. At the start of each of its turns, a creature burning in this way takes {@damage 1d6} fire damage. A creature that can reach the burning target can use an action to extinguish the fire.
Red Dragon Mask.entries.7.name: Dragon Fire
Red Dragon Mask.name: Red Dragon Mask
Red Dragon Scale Mail.name: Red Dragon Scale Mail
